louvre_cl010001686.title: documentary papyrus
louvre_cl010001686.description: A documentary papyrus from the Byzantine period (6th-7th centuries), representing an administrative or legal document from Late Antiquity. This fragmentary manuscript exemplifies the continuation of papyrus-based record-keeping traditions in Byzantine Egypt, serving as material evidence of governmental, legal, or commercial activities during the transition from Roman to Byzantine rule.

louvre_cl010083352.title: ostracon
louvre_cl010083352.description: An ostracon from the Roman period (-30 - 395 CE), made of terracotta. This archaeological artifact represents a common writing surface used in ancient Egypt, typically consisting of a pottery shard or limestone flake used for writing. During the Roman period in Egypt, ostraca served various practical purposes including administrative records, letters, receipts, and educational exercises, demonstrating the continuation of Egyptian scribal traditions under Roman rule.

louvre_cl010006242.title: mushroom-shaped gaming piece

Fragment 2: Aphrodite (chiton, revealing, breast, leg, crossed). Condition of the work: Fragment 1: The head and right forearm of Aphrodite are missing. Only the right arm of Eros remains on Aphrodite's left shoulder. Fragment 2: Only the torso remains from antiquity. The joining of the two statuettes is a modern assembly from the 17th century.

louvre_cl010175753.title: cylinder seal
louvre_cl010175753.description: Decoration: inscription. Object details: Cylinder depicting from left to right: the god from whose shoulders spring two fish-filled streams, holds a vase from which two streams flow. He stands on a fish-goat swimming on streams personified by two small goddesses whose lower bodies merge with them. These goddesses hold flowing vases, which join the streams issuing from the god. Then praying goddess and 'figure with mace', also holding a harpe. Before him, similar figure striking down a naked captive whom he holds by the hand. The captive has long hair, a pointed object attached to his belt and curved shoes. Above, two small praying goddesses on either side of a starred disc placed above the crescent. Inscription...

louvre_cl010171727.title: Cylinder Seal
louvre_cl010171727.description: Decoration: god of the waters; spouting vase; Usmu; goddess; worshipper; hero (nude); staff. Details about the object: Cylinder seal. The god of the abyss of fresh waters surrounding the world: Enki or Ea, god of wisdom, in the company of the hero who is one of the Seven Sages, holding the post of his door. The two-faced steward introduces the personal goddess of a worshipper, owner of the seal. The god also appears in a boat, navigating in his domain. The image of the god of the waters was also suitable for the father-god, chief of the pantheon of each city.

Details of the object: Symmetrically arranged around a horizontal line where two serpent heads are located, are two frontal heads adorned with two long double-curved horns, each accompanied by two profile heads, one of a lion and one of a bird of prey. louvre_cl010341684.title: fabric
louvre_cl010341684.medium:
Material: wool (burgundy), linen
Technique: tapestry, plain weave
louvre_cl010341684.description: Two fragments of a wide L-shaped band of monochrome burgundy tapestry set into a linen foundation fabric. The monochrome band is decorated with a geometric network created with flying shuttle technique and bordered by an ecru trim formed of small right triangles. The bottom of fragment 2 features a reserved square, within which is inscribed a diamond that contains at its center an eight-petaled floral motif traced with flying shuttle. In each corner of the square are placed large pastilles marked with a small ecru point at their center, and framed by two small pastilles placed on either side.

louvre_cl010322535.title: Star with Simurgh
louvre_cl010322535.medium: Ceramic (siliceous paste), metallic luster decoration on opaque glaze
louvre_cl010322535.description: A star-shaped ceramic piece featuring the simurgh, a mythical bird from Persian mythology. Created in the 13th to early 14th century (1200-1315), this work exemplifies the sophisticated ceramic traditions of medieval Islamic art, particularly the technique of metallic luster decoration applied over opaque glaze on siliceous paste.

louvre_cl010332977.description: A ceramic tile featuring a scattered pattern of composite flowers and flowering buds, created in the second half of the 16th century (circa 1560-1580). The piece exemplifies Iznik pottery techniques with painted decoration applied on engobe under a transparent glaze, using siliceous paste ceramic material.

louvre_cl010330609.title: Quran Stand
louvre_cl010330609.medium: Wood, painted, gilded and varnished decoration; wood marquetry; metal
louvre_cl010330609.description: A Quran stand from the 18th-19th centuries, crafted from wood with painted, gilded and varnished decoration, featuring wood marquetry and metal elements. This devotional object from Iran represents the Islamic tradition of creating beautiful supports for the sacred text of the Quran.

louvre_cl010329680.title: Small bowl with conical foot decorated with so-called 'grain of rice' pattern
louvre_cl010329680.medium: Ceramic (siliceous paste), pierced and painted decoration under transparent glaze
louvre_cl010329680.description: Small white pierced bowl of round shape, slightly flared, on a conical foot. Around the perimeter a band of pierced geometric ornaments whose cutouts are filled with the colorless enamel of the glaze. At the bottom a stylized black motif formed of four palmettes and a flower. Siliceous paste, grain of rice decoration painted in black under colorless translucent glaze.

louvre_cl010330155.title: Pitcher
louvre_cl010330155.description: Jug in grey unglazed earthenware. The cylindrical body is decorated with vertical stripes topped with semicircles. The low and very thin neck is held by a small handle, both of which are broken and reglued, with a small spout on the body.

louvre_cl020017349.description: Album description: Factitious album of red leather with gold geometric motif. A label on the initial endpaper indicates: 'RF 4189 / Vinchon Auguste Jean-Baptiste / Paris 1789 Ems 1855 / Sketches for the grisaille paintings of rooms IV, VII, VIII and IX of the Charles X Museum / 122 sketches, 51 pages, 3 notes'. Album of sketches for the grisaille paintings of Rooms IV, VII, VII and IX of the Charles X Museum.

louvre_cl020142948.title: Animal eye; studies of a fortification element and a capital
louvre_cl020142948.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with green-black marbled paper with red leather spine, composed of 43 numbered sheets from 1 to 43, on paper of various colors, of which folios 6, 7, 10, 11, 14, 16, 19, are blank. Trace of torn folio between folios 41 and 42. On the front cover, red stamp "ED" from Delacroix's sale. White label glued next to the stamp. H. 0.140; L. 0.216.

louvre_cl020024924.title: Bridge in the foreground of a wooded landscape
louvre_cl020024924.medium: brown ink with pen; brown wash; gray wash; graphite
louvre_cl020024924.description: Description of the album: Album bound in parchment composed of 65 sheets. Formerly stored in a cardboard case covered with green marbled paper, with a dark green leather spine fitted with a garnet leather label bearing the inscription: 'ALBUM of a DUTCH ARTIST'. Landscapes and studies of figures and animals. H. 0.190; 0.085. One drawn folio has been cut out between folios 8 and 9.

louvre_cl020130968.title: Man and animals on a beach
louvre_cl020130968.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, with back and covers of dark green leather, with golden fillets, offering numerous seaside views. H.0.770; L. 0.600.

louvre_cl020122511.title: Study sheet with various characters
louvre_cl020122511.description: A preparatory drawing study featuring various human figures, executed in lead pencil. This work appears to be related to Delacroix's famous painting 'The Massacre at Chios', serving as character studies for the larger composition. The sheet contains multiple figure studies that explore different poses and expressions of the subjects.

louvre_cl020549934.title: Head of a Man
louvre_cl020549934.medium: Crayon manner, printed in brown sanguine. Signed 'Boucher del' at bottom left and at right: 'hakman S.'. At bottom center: 'AParis chés le Ve de F. Chéreau rue St. Jacques aux 2. Piliers d'Or.' Watermark: T (well) Dupuy Fin Auvergne 1742. Printed in red sanguine.

Missing sheet between folios 47 and 48. On the upper cover is glued a white, round label, bearing in pencil, the number 82. H: 0.102; L: 0.153. It contains 56 folios.

louvre_cl020022353.description: Description of the album: Factitious album of 109 numbered folios from 1 to 111 (RF 6952 to RF 7108, RF 7052 detached) on which are pasted original drawings, lithographs, maps and newspaper extracts relating to Louis-Philippe's journey to Windsor Castle in 1844. A manuscript of 117 numbered sheets accompanies it. The album is preparatory to the collection of lithographs: 'Journey of H.M. Louis-Philippe I, King of the French to Windsor Castle', published in Paris by Pingret and in London by Ackermann, January 31, 1846.

louvre_cl020592178.title: Those Who Take Up Others' Faults
louvre_cl020592178.medium: Etching. Print numbered at bottom right.
louvre_cl020592178.description: Description of the album: [Frontispiece]: Emblemata Saecularia, Mira et Jucunda Varietate Saeculi Hujus Mores ita experimentia ut sodalitatum symbolis insigniisque conscribendis et depingendis peraccomoda sint versibus Latinis rithmisque Germanicis, Gallicis, Belgicis: speciali item declamatione de amore exornata./ Secular amusing new artistic pieces/ depicting the current world's course/ adorned with artful Latin German/ French and Dutch carmina and rhymes/ quite useful for an elegant lineage and heraldic book / Jo. Theodorum et Jo. Israelem de Bry fratres germanos, ciues Francoford./ Frankfurt, Year 1596. Preface in Latin; Arguments in Latin and German; Captions to illustrations in Latin, German and sometimes French; Notice to readers.

louvre_cl020596517.title: Congo
louvre_cl020596517.medium: Etching. Typography printed below the composition.
louvre_cl020596517.description: Description of the album: [Frontispiece:] Historical games of the kings of France, renowned Queens, geography and metamorphosis / By the late Mr J. DESMARETS Counselor/ Secretary & Controller General of / the Extraordinary of Wars / And engraved by DO LA BELLA. These same games are accommodated as easy cards / to play, & will be sold separately .....IN PARIS, / At NICOLAS LE CLERC's, on the Quay / des Augustins, at the descent of Pont S. Michel, near the hotel de Luynes, at the image S.Lambert / AND / At FLORENT LE COMTE's, rue S. Jacques, near / the Fountain S. Benoist, at the Royal Cipher / M.DC. XCVIII. / With the King's Privilege'. Notice to the reader. This volume contains four games invented in 1644 by Jean Desmarets de Saint-Sorlin to serve in the education of Louis XIV. The last three suites appeared at

louvre_cl020572365.title: Notes: series of various addresses, most crossed out
louvre_cl020572365.description: Description of the album: Notebook of notes and sketches called 'green notebook', with modern cardboard cover covered in almond green paper, identical spine bearing in gold letters the mention CARNET. H: 0.195; L: 0.135. It is composed of beige paper sheets, 3 blank unnumbered, then 97 numbered in pencil, top right, 1 to 97 and then 2 blank unnumbered. Sheet dimensions: H: 0.190; 0.125. Folios 25, 26, 45, 46, 47, 48, 56, 58, 59, 60, 62, 64, 65, 66, 75 are blank. Folio 78 is detached. A fragment of the album's original cover is attached to the front endpaper, thus forming a gusset, on which is glued a sheet of paper with notes. Another fragment of the album's original cover is attached to the contr

Several sheets were torn out prior to the numbering in graphite on the front of each sheet, in the upper right. Traces of missing pages between folios 2 and 3, 9 and 10, 13 and 14, 19 and 20, 20 and 21, 22 and 23, 23 and 24.

Each plate is signed: A, b-v,.a.-.m, n-o, .p.-q., r-s, .t.-.v., illustrated. This is the first edition. Complete copy, 40 plates enhanced with colors with Latin text.

louvre_cl020537861.title: Bonaparte Crowned by Victory, Allegory Related to the Battle of Marengo
louvre_cl020537861.description: This preparatory drawing depicts an allegorical scene of Bonaparte being crowned by the personification of Victory, commemorating his triumph at the Battle of Marengo. The work employs black stone tracing as the primary medium, with additional pen and black ink work and brown wash techniques.

louvre_cl020131173.title: Sailboat sailing towards the right
louvre_cl020131173.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, with back and covers of dark green leather, with golden fillets, offering numerous seaside views. H.0.770; L. 0.600.

louvre_cl020575053.title: Landscape
louvre_cl020575053.medium: crayon and watercolor on paper. Pencil pagination: 33. Former pencil pagination crossed out: 18
louvre_cl020575053.description: Description of the album: Sketch notebook with natural leather-colored cardboard cover, brown cloth spine (Canson et Montgolfier notebook no.1010, stamp on back endpaper) detachable sheets. On the front cover: date in center in pencil, retraced in black "1941-42", date in pencil left edge "1941-1942[42 crossed out]", pencil annotations: "Thonon" "Charles" "Paul" "Gabriel"; bottom left Catalogue Raisonné label: "ct126 (1941-42)", removed during 2010 restoration. Initials M in pencil.

louvre_cl020584448.title: An Angel Playing the Trumpet
louvre_cl020584448.description: Description of the album: Typographic incunabulum book. -2°. Illustrated. Venice, Aldus Manutius for Leonardo Grassi, XII 1499. It is composed of 230 leaves signed from a to z and from A to F. The complete title on the first leaf is 'HYPNEROTOMACHIA POLIPHILI, VBI HV / MANA OMNIA NON NISISOMNIVM / ESSE DOCET. ATQVE OBITER/ PLVRIMA SCITV SANE / QUAM DIGNA COM / MEMORAT.*** ** * / CAVTVMEST, NEHVNCLI / BR VM QVEAT IMPPRIME / RE'. The signatures g and h are inverted.

louvre_cl020227997.title: Prairie with Trees Near a River
louvre_cl020227997.medium: Watercolor. Signed at bottom right: 'H. Zuber' on verso, indication: '1900'.

louvre_cl020117785.title: Various manuscript annotations
louvre_cl020117785.medium: brown ink with pen; lead pencil
louvre_cl020117785.description: Description of the album: Cardboard sketchbook with broken covers, covered with red paper, with a dark green leather spine, bearing on the front cover the red wax seal of the Corot Sale. Folio 12, blank, and folio 22 are torn. Traces of 2 missing folios between folios 2 and 3, between folios 5 and 6, between folios 7 and 8, between folios 12 and 13. H. 0.117; L. 0.168.

louvre_cl020516886.title: The Virgin Seated Holding the Sleeping Child Jesus

louvre_cl020119139.title: Barrels
louvre_cl020119139.description: Description of the album: Notebook with blue marbled cardboard cover and green spine, with a label glued on the front cover indicating 'n° 14 / 1833', composed of 53 sheets, of which folios 28, 29 and 46 to 51 are blank, and an envelope containing drawing RF 8722. BIS. A label from 'Bourienne, Paper merchant, rue des Carmes, n° 77, in Rouen', is glued on the front endpaper.

louvre_cl020137631.title: Sculpture with a standing figure, one arm extended
louvre_cl020137631.medium: brown ink with pen; graphite; blue-violet paper
louvre_cl020137631.description: Album description: Notebook, bound in green color composed of 66 numbered folios. H.16 L.9.6 cm.

louvre_cl020233287.title: Numbered Annotations and Geometric Sketches
louvre_cl020233287.description: Album description: Cardboard album of brown-violet grained paper comprising 74 sheets. One sheet torn between folios 1 and 2, 10 and 11, 16 and 17, 27 and 28, 43 and 44, 64 and 65, 2 sheets between folios 12 and 13, 18 and 19, 44 and 45, 3 sheets between folios 47 and 48, several sheets between folios 40 and 41. It contains landscape sketches, soldier studies, and handwritten notes. H: 0.200; W: 0.320.

louvre_cl020107130.title: Study for the figure of a seated evangelist
louvre_cl020107130.medium: Black stone, pen and brown ink

louvre_cl020206676.title: Admiration with Astonishment
louvre_cl020206676.medium: Etching and engraving. Annotated at the top, in the middle: 'admiration with astonishment' and on the right: '4'.
louvre_cl020206676.description: Description of the album: Factitious album assembled by Morel d'Arleux, bringing together 63 drawings, bound in blue calfskin with gold lace, spine with three red leather labels, interior covers in blue and pink marbled paper. On the first page, in pen and black ink: 'French School / volume 18, P - 63 drawings'. The drawings by Le Brun and his workshop relate to the expression of passions, subject of his lectures at the Academy in 1668.

louvre_cl020225802.title: Plan
louvre_cl020225802.medium: Annotated, in pencil, at top left: 'convent of the crucifix at Spoleto'

louvre_cl020231363.title: Bearded Head
louvre_cl020231363.description: Description of the album: Sketch album with beige cardboard cover. Inscription on the front cover: No. 118. Handwritten inscription on the front endpaper in brown ink. Inscription in pink ink: no. 4 on the initial

louvre_cl020580556.description: Album description: H: 0.628 m W: 0.489 m D: 0.038 m Open book: 0.977 m

louvre_cl020210370.title: View of the Bridge of Grenoble
louvre_cl020210370.medium: Inscription at upper left: 'View of the Bridge of Grenoble'. Common mounting with drawing INV 33008.
louvre_cl020210370.description: A topographical drawing depicting the bridge of Grenoble, executed in brown ink with pen, watercolor, and graphite point. The work features an inscription identifying the subject and is mounted together with another related drawing.

louvre_cl020508699.title: Leaving School
louvre_cl020508699.medium: Pen, black ink, gray wash, black chalk, reworked with stylus. Three green spots. Framing line in pen and gray ink, reworked with stylus. Mounted at two opposite corners. Inscription in black chalk, bottom center: la Sortie du Collège. Numbering in graphite pencil, top right: 108. The verso of the drawing was treated with sanguine wash and stumped for engraving; traces of wax seal. Restored in 2002
louvre_cl020508699.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, bound in sheepskin, with gilded tools and six ribs on the spine, titled 'OEUVRES / DES / ST. AUBINS'. Composed of 155 foliated leaves, bearing 287 drawings (RF 52178 to 52465). On the flyleaf, two pasted bookplates, the first of Martine Marie Pol de Béhague, with a lectern and the motto: 'NAITRE / PLEVRER (crossed out and corrected to) APPRENDRE / MOVRIR', the second of Hubert de Ganay.

louvre_cl020515169.title: Saint Jude Thaddeus and Saint Simon

louvre_cl020220034.title: Caricature: horse kicking a man
louvre_cl020220034.medium: Pen and brown ink inscriptions, left: '11'; bottom: 'Ah blast! an Arab horse that has not frankly rallied to the French'. Torn drawing.
louvre_cl020220034.description: Description of the album: Composite album with marbled paper covers and brown cloth spine, grouping drawings RF 23618 to RF 24028. Folio 77 is followed by folio 77 bis. H: 0.310; L: 0.445.

louvre_cl020042736.title: Three figures violently arguing around a table

louvre_cl020108619.title: Group of Three Men and Four Women
louvre_cl020108619.medium: brown ink with pen and brush

louvre_cl020036505.title: Manuscript annotations on the upper endpaper
louvre_cl020036505.medium: Pen and brown ink, gray wash. Page glued on cardboard mounted in accordion fold with the cover board. Annotations, at upper left in pen and brown ink: 6 #; in center: Monsieur / Dépairs; and, a little lower right, in black ink and crossed out: Morin. The inventory number RF 35698 is inscribed in graphite pencil at the top of the page.
louvre_cl020036505.description: Description of the album: This notebook was drawn during the artist's stay in Rome between 1692 and 1699. It is now covered with a parchment binding, contemporary with its creation. It bears on the upper board the autograph signature Oppenord. The inscription on the back of the binding, ESQUISE DE OPPENOR, is by another hand and probably later. The notebook now consists of ninety-seven leaves, three of which have been added (two at the beginning and one at the end), very probably during the 19th or 20th century. The notebook initially had ninety-eight leaves, but leaves 1, 77, 91 and 94 are missing. Each of the rectos is numbered in black ink at the upper right, from 2 to 95. This first numbering was done very certain

louvre_cl020608345.title: Savoyard
louvre_cl020608345.description: Album description: Collection containing 'Studies taken from the common people or the Cries of Paris', five series, after drawings by Edmé Bouchardon, engraved by Count de Caylus and Etienne Fessard, in Paris, 1737-1746. H: 0.328 m L: 0.252 m D: 0.027 m Open book: 0.493 m

louvre_cl020121987.title: Young Nude Woman, from Behind
louvre_cl020121987.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with red paper, with dark green leather spine, composed of 43 numbered sheets from 1 to 43. Traces of torn folios between folios 39 and 40. Red wax seal "ED" and label glued on the front cover. H. 0.108; W. 0.150.

Following an error in the counting of inventory numbers, inventory numbers 491 DR, 492 DR and 493 DR are not assigned. H: 27.6 cm; W: 11.5 cm

louvre_cl020032902.medium: Pen and gray ink, gray wash. Traces of black chalk.
louvre_cl020032902.description: Description of the album: Notebook with green cardboard cover, comprising in the current state 60 sheets of watermarked laid paper (watermark: crowned fleur-de-lis [circa Churchill, 1985, no. 407]) bound in 9 gatherings (for the structure of the notebook, see document associated with record RF 29955,1). Vertical chain lines (spacing between chain lines: 28 mm). On the back cover, inscription in pen and brown ink: 'No. 4'. The notebook is foliated in pencil, top right, from 1 to 60, and presents, on the reverse of the back cover (also numbered, in pencil, top left: '61') the label of the paper merchant Niodot.

louvre_cl020131208.title: Two canoes and a sailboat near a jetty

louvre_cl020578898.description: Album description: H: 0.628 m W: 0.489 m D: 0.038 m Open book: 0.977 m

louvre_cl020127986.description: Description of the album: Factitious album with back and covers of black leather with gold fillets containing 34 folios numbered from 1 to 32. H.69.8 L.56 cm.

louvre_cl020228100.title: Text
louvre_cl020228100.description: Description of the album: Album bound in calfskin, with spine decorated with gold motifs, with a red leather label bearing the inscription in gold letters: 'Test./ de / Carac. Arms on the covers.

louvre_cl020038868.title: Young child dressed in a short tunic
louvre_cl020038868.description: Description of the album: Sketch album with green cardboard cover, bearing in ink the inscription: 'no. 7'. Pencil inscription: 'L.V. from Mr. Scribe prior and priest of Artouge in Monmeveille

louvre_cl020024192.title: Handwritten Notes
louvre_cl020024192.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album, covered with black grained embossed paper, with black leather spine, bearing on the upper cover: 'ALBUM', mainly offering landscapes or pieces of military equipment. H. 0.102; L. 0.172.

louvre_cl020593167.title: The Suffering of Job
louvre_cl020593167.description: Description of the album: [Title:] Historiarum Veteris Testamenti icones ad vivum expressae. Lyon, Subscuto coloniensis, published by Ioannem & Franciscum Frellonios, brothers, 1543. - Notice to the reader. Numbered guard pages. 92 woodcut engravings after Holbein.

louvre_cl020228575.description: Description of the album: Artificial cardboard album, covered with yellow and brown marbled paper, with blue cloth spine, grouping drawings INV 29131 to INV 29147. It contains figure studies, soldiers, and animals. H: 0.303; W: 0.220.

louvre_cl020201883.title: Woman, front view, holding an amphora with both hands: Saint Mary Magdalene

louvre_cl020007010.medium: brown ink with pen; wash (brown); red chalk

louvre_cl020219047.title: Handwritten text in French language
louvre_cl020219047.description: Description of the album: Notebook partially covered with checkered cloth, the front and back covers bear the monogram: PGO and the title: Ancient Mahorie Cult, as well as drawings. It is composed of 38 numbered sheets in pencil, at the top right, 1 to 38. A second numbering, in pen and black ink was added by the artist, at the top right of the sheets, on recto and verso, 1 to 53. Folios 30 to 36 are blank. H: 216; L: 174.

louvre_cl020013987.title: Head of an old man, three-quarters view, turned to the right
louvre_cl020013987.medium: red chalk; paper washed with brown

louvre_cl020214200.title: Nude Boy, from Behind, Turning, Holding a Serpent

louvre_cl020508461.title: Frontispiece of the Saint-Aubin Book
louvre_cl020508461.medium: Pen, brown and black inks, gray and beige wash, over preparatory drawing in black chalk or charcoal. Watermarked paper. Annotation in pen and brown ink and gray wash, in center: DRAWINGS OF THE ST AUBINS; in black chalk, bottom right: 1716. Numbering in pen and black ink, top right: 1; in black chalk, inside cartouche, top center: 2. Museum mark bottom right. Mounted on folio 1 of the Book.
louvre_cl020508461.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, bound in sheepskin, with gilt tooling and six raised bands on the spine, titled 'WORKS / OF THE / ST. AUBINS'. Composed of 155 numbered folios, bearing 287 drawings (RF 52178 to 52465). On the endpaper, two pasted ex-libris, the first of Martine Marie Pol de Béhague, with a lectern and the motto: 'TO BE BORN / TO CRY (crossed out and corrected to) TO LEARN / TO DIE', the second of Hubert de Ganay.

louvre_cl020208284.title: Whale Fishing: Mooring Alongside the Ship

louvre_cl020118250.title: Character Sketches
louvre_cl020118250.description: Description of the album: Bound album with cardboard cover covered in purple embossed paper and brown cloth spine, composed of 138 sheets. On the front cover, wax seal from the Corot sale. At the top of the first page, Corot noted in pencil: 'Given in Brest, May 11, 1857, by Mrs. Camille Bernier'. Missing folio between 55 and 56. Blank folios: 101 to 105, 109 to 121, 123 to 131, 136 and 137. 5 sheets torn out between folio 116 and folio 117. Torn folios: 136, 137, 138. H. 0.168; W. 0.108

louvre_cl020021535.title: Portrait of Adolphe Thiers, bust, frontal view

louvre_cl020208545.title: A disciple of Saint Bruno: study for the sixth painting
louvre_cl020208545.medium: gray-beige paper; black chalk; white highlights
louvre_cl020208545.description: Description of the album: Factitious album in red morocco leather printed in gold containing drawings, studies and engravings by Le Sueur for the life of Saint Bruno, painted in the cloister of the Carthusians of Paris. Interior covers of red, green and beige marbled paper.

louvre_cl020115233.title: Man seen half-length, turned to the right, holding a whip behind his back
louvre_cl020115233.medium: Black crayon and white highlights on beige paper. Annotation in upper left in black crayon: '10'.

louvre_cl020007408.title: Christ Giving the Keys to Saint Peter
louvre_cl020007408.medium: Black stone, pen and brown ink and brown wash, squared for transfer in black stone. H. 327; W. 270 mm; octagonal in shape. Annotated, bottom right, in black stone: muciano. Fully mounted on a Mariette mount: H. 535; W. 403 mm. On the mount, in a cartouche, in pen and black ink: HIER. MUTIANO.
louvre_cl020007408.description: A drawing depicting the biblical scene of Christ giving the keys to Saint Peter, executed in black stone, pen and brown ink with brown wash. The work is octagonal in shape and shows evidence of being squared for transfer, suggesting it may have been a preparatory drawing for a larger work. The composition includes religious figures and sheep symbolism, representing the moment when Christ entrusted Peter with spiritual authority over the Church.

louvre_cl020565649.title: Architecture
louvre_cl020565649.medium: Pen, brown ink and brown wash
louvre_cl020565649.description: Album description: This album brings together 110 drawings distributed across 76 folios. Item no. 320 from the Hippolyte Destailleur sale: 'Collection of drawings representing ancient monuments, sacrificial altars, funerary stones, doors and triumphal arches, cities with ancient monuments, temples, etc. One hundred and eight pen and sepia drawings in one folio volume, marbled calf, red edges. Precious collection by one of the most brilliant artists of the Italian Renaissance, probably Sansovino. The series of doors and arches decorated with sculpture is particularly remarkable'.

louvre_cl020144420.title: Henri Robert seen as a bust, three-quarters to the left, in lawyer's robes

louvre_cl020038527.title: Statue of 'MERCURY'
louvre_cl020038527.medium: Statue identified and located in the Château de Richelieu below, left, in pen and brown ink. Annotated, bottom left, in pen and black ink: [Mercur]io, top left, in pen and brown ink: Veu and finally, bottom left, in pen and brown ink: [Co]urt. L. 1886a bottom right.
louvre_cl020038527.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious album, with parchment cover, boards with the arms of Cardinal de Richelieu, remounted, comprising 91 folios, numbered at bottom right. H. 0.413; L. 0.267

louvre_cl020010061.title: An Angel Holding a Palm and a Crown
louvre_cl020010061.medium: Red chalk. Trace of plate mark with cuvette on verso. Trace of off-center fold toward bottom. Lower left in graphite: Domini. Verso: upper left, 42 C in graphite, 42 retouched with pen and brown ink. Watermark: fleur-de-lis in a circle surmounting the letters CB.
louvre_cl020010061.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, bound in green leather. Studies after antique or religious sculptures. H. 0.700; L. 0.550.

louvre_cl020131685.title: A Pier with a Construction and Several Figures
louvre_cl020131685.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, with green leather cover with gold fillets, composed of 41 numbered folios. H. 0.700; L. 0.560.

louvre_cl020036608.title: Decorative Sculptural Motifs
louvre_cl020036608.medium: Pen and brown and black inks, gray wash over graphite underdrawing
louvre_cl020036608.description: Album description: This sketchbook was drawn during the artist's stay in Rome between 1692 and 1699. It is now covered with a parchment binding, contemporary to its creation. It bears on the upper cover the autograph signature Oppenord. The inscription on the back of the binding, ESQUISE DE OPPENOR, is by another hand and probably later. The sketchbook now consists of ninety-seven leaves, three of which have been added (two at the beginning and one at the end), most likely during the 19th or 20th century.

louvre_cl020577777.title: Juno
louvre_cl020577777.medium: Etching and engraving. Below the first square line is engraved, on the left: 'R.B del', on the right: 'N.B. Sculp'. Below the square line are engraved the title, a quatrain and the address: 'Junon / ...../ A Paris Chez N. Bonnart, ruë St. Iacques, à l'aigle, avec privil.'
louvre_cl020577777.description: Album description: H: 0.376 m W: 0.250 m D: 0.025 m Open book: 0.514 m

louvre_cl020563175.title: Sketch of two figures in armor, one standing, the other seated, three-quarters to the left
louvre_cl020563175.medium: graphite pencil on paper. Drawn lengthwise
louvre_cl020563175.description: Description of the album: Sketch album with cardboard cover covered in beige cloth with pencil holder, comprising 26 sheets. On the front endpaper, blue printed label: MOIRINAT / 184 Fg St Honoré / PARIS. On the back endpaper and on folio 26 verso: handwritten annotations in graphite pencil, expenses from a trip from St Malo and partial plan of ramparts and fortifications. 1893-1896. Notebook dimensions: H. 00,090; L. 00,130

louvre_cl020233312.title: Studies of soldiers and a bicorne

One sheet torn between folios 1 and 2, 10 and 11, 16 and 17, 27 and 28, 43 and 44, 64 and 65, 2 sheets between folios 12 and 13, 18 and 19, 44 and 45, 3 sheets between folios 47 and 48, several sheets between folios 40 and 41. It contains landscape sketches, soldier studies, and handwritten notes. H: 0.200; W: 0.320.

louvre_cl020227912.title: The Three Musketeers: musketeer reading a letter from a servant
louvre_cl020227912.description: Description of the album: Factitious album bound in gray-beige cloth, oblong format, bearing on the upper cover a label with almost completely erased annotations. It groups together drawings RF 28589 to RF 28656 pasted on 50 sheets and on the lower endpaper. Some drawings are illustrations for The Three Musketeers by Alexandre Dumas. H: 0.235; W: 0.300.

louvre_cl020539113.title: Dido Taking Her Own Life
louvre_cl020539113.medium: Engraving. The inscription 'DIDO' is engraved on the stone at the bottom right of the composition. Signed with the monogram AA at the bottom right.

louvre_cl020020911.title: Standing Man Holding a Pickaxe and Rolling Up His Sleeves

louvre_cl020039438.description: Album description: This drawing is part of a factitious album grouping 29 works (RF 31663 to RF 31691). Album from the stationer Niodot. The drawings are mounted in full on mounting sheets of equal dimensions: H: 0.365; W: 0.433.

louvre_cl020135447.title: Draped Woman
louvre_cl020135447.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious notebook of brown embossed paper with floral motifs, with a brown grained paper spine, containing studies after Antique works, sculptures including some from the Renaissance, and numerous handwritten annotations (mostly addresses). Contains 89 folios. H.16.5 W.11.2 cm.

louvre_cl020032160.title: Study of a seated little girl and a scepter
louvre_cl020032160.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with bistre cloth, comprising 43 numbered sheets of paper in various colors. Some sheets have been torn out (before folio 1, between folios 15 and 16, and folio 27 is cut in half). Stretched closing elastic. Pencil holder on the binding. On the front cover, round white label with pencil inscription: 'AL6'. Supplier label on the front endpaper. Studio stamp on several folios. H. 0.09; L. 0.142.

louvre_cl020012541.title: Armorial Page
louvre_cl020012541.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious album. Bound in brown leather, spine bearing the title in gold letters: 'Collection of Coats of Arms', with a label on the upper cover bearing the inscription: 'coats of arms, French School 17th century'. Another label on the front cover bearing no. 12728 (Napoleon III no.). The upper headcap of the binding is torn off. On the verso of the endpaper: no. 10528. On folio 3, a stamp from the Louvre library. Studies of blazons. On the back pastedown, three wax seals. H. 0.460; W. 0.296.

louvre_cl020118948.title: Sketch
louvre_cl020118948.description: Description of the album: Two folios missing between folio 1 and 2, one folio missing between folio 5 and 6, one folio missing between folio 32 and 33, one folio missing between folio 36 and 37, one folio missing between folio 52 and 53.

louvre_cl020220339.title: View of the old palace of Potsdam with the parade ground in front

louvre_cl020587096.title: Ewer

Bourniche, color merchants. Sketches of landscapes from Boulogne or northern France, and studies of animals and figures. Folios 47 and 48 are blank. H. 0.144; W. 0.220.

louvre_cl020000415.medium: Quill pen and brown ink over black chalk traces
louvre_cl020000415.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with marbled brown paper. It bears on the cover a label with the following mention: 'Stefano della Bella', and on the spine, another, annotated: 'vol. I'. This volume is paginated from I to 126, each of the sixty-three sheets that compose it bearing a number on the front and back. The drawings are glued, either in full, or by a tab on the sheet of the album. H: 0.460; L: 0.365.

louvre_cl020202730.title: Half-figure of a woman, turning away

louvre_cl020119505.title: Cat; horseman; sketches

Trace of missing folio between folios 18 and 19. Folio 41 is torn. A label is glued on the front paste-down: 'A la palette de Rubens - Leroy - rue de Sèvres, N° 6'. H: 120; W: 0.200.

louvre_cl020219485.title: Manuscript Notes
louvre_cl020219485.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album, covered with black-violet embossed paper, with navy embossed cloth spine, composed of 17 numbered sheets. H. 0.186; W. 0.255. Missing one folio before folio 1 and between folios 8 and 9.

louvre_cl020105372.title: Scene from Roman History
louvre_cl020105372.description: Description of the album: Subjects drawn from the history of the seven legendary kings of Rome, drawings intended for the illustration of a Livy edition by Laurentius Valla and Henricus Glareanus. Modern binding, half parchment. Marbled covers.

louvre_cl020022590.title: Group of Two Figures in a Landscape
louvre_cl020022590.description: Description of the album: Album covered in blue cardboard with decorative motif and brown spine, composed of 60 foliated sheets, of which folio 47 is blank. It brings together studies of landscapes and figures. Trace of missing folio between folios 10 and 11, 37 and 38, 44 and 45. Folio 59 torn. H: 0.158; L: 0.109.

louvre_cl020514115.title: A window with antique-style balcony
louvre_cl020514115.medium: Black stone, pen and black ink, gray wash on parchment. Inscription in pen and black ink: For a dormer window / in the antique style
louvre_cl020514115.description: Description of the album: Album consisting of 7 notebooks containing 28 architectural drawings on parchment, in pen and black ink, and gray wash, over a black stone outline. First notebook: 11 sheets of paper forming 22 folios of which the first 20 are missing; second notebook: 2 sheets of parchment forming 4 folios; third notebook: 2 sheets of parchment forming 4 folios of which the first 2 are missing; fourth notebook: 4 sheets of parchment forming 8 folios; fifth notebook: 4 sheets of parchment forming 8 folios; sixth notebook: 3 sheets of parchment forming 6 folios; seventh notebook: 10 sheets of paper forming 20 folios of which the last 19 are missing.

louvre_cl020514796.description: Description of the album: Album with brown imitation leather paper cover, brown coated cloth spine containing 62 folios of cream laid paper, with a booklet of chamois-colored paper. On the cover, in gold letters: Album. Label on the back paste-down: Lafage aîné, rue des Boucheries, 8, Quimper. Folios 13 to 15, 54 to 62 are blank. Loose sheet, RF 52742. H. 0.138; L. 0.212.

louvre_cl020011435.title: Mystical Marriage of Saint Catherine
louvre_cl020011435.description: A religious drawing depicting the mystical marriage of Saint Catherine, featuring Saint Catherine, Jesus Christ, and the Virgin Mary in a sacred scene representing the spiritual union between the saint and the divine.

louvre_cl020208109.title: Plan and Profile of a Cascade
louvre_cl020208109.medium: Black chalk, red chalk, pen and black ink, gray wash, watercolor. Fully dimensioned in toises, feet and inches, in pen and black ink. Scale marked in toises, in the lower right section. Annotated in pen and black ink at top: 'Profile. There is from the ledge of the large water feature to the top of the Avenue 39 feet 10 inches 1/2 slope'; annotated on a horizontal line marking the middle: 'Water features Avenues glacis avenues glacis Half moon.'
louvre_cl020208109.description: This is a technical architectural drawing showing both plan and elevation views of a cascade water feature, created around 1672-1676. The drawing is executed in multiple media including black chalk, red chalk, pen and ink, gray wash, and watercolor. It features comprehensive measurements in traditional French units (toises, feet, and inches) and includes detailed annotations describing the various elements of the garden design including water features, avenues, and glacis. The drawing appears to be a working document for the construction of garden features at the Château de Sceaux.

louvre_cl020574692.title: Landscape with Buildings
louvre_cl020574692.medium: crayon on vellum paper. Pagination in pencil. On verso, pagination in pencil; former pagination in black ink crossed out in graphite
louvre_cl020574692.description: Description of the album: Notebook with chamois-colored cardboard cover, brown cloth spine (Canson et Montgolfier n°1010, stamp on back endpaper), vellum paper, detachable sheets. On front cover: letter 'F' in black ink at top right; recent white label with date in pencil '1934' at bottom left; catalogue raisonné label 'ct106 (1934)' removed during 2010 restoration. On front endpaper: autograph manuscript annotations (addresses) and non-autograph ones. Round label: 1934. Catalogue Raisonné label: Ct106 (1934). Annotation in pencil at bottom left: C 2. On back endpaper: annotation at bottom right: C 3. Black ink stamp. 38 folios. Continuous pagination of notebooks (graphite pencil

louvre_cl020627511.title: Dedications
louvre_cl020627511.description: Description of the album: Liber amicorum (friendship book) of the Princess of Salm, composed between January 1st, 1809 and 1841. Original binding in ebony shagreen, covers decorated with blind-stamped border. On the upper cover, inscription in gold letters 'Album', surmounted by the crowned cipher 'C.S'. Smooth spine decorated with blind fillets, lining and endpapers of watered paper, gilt edges (period binding). Dedicated by Constance Salm to her friends and 'TO ALL THOSE WHO LOVE OR CULTIVATE LETTERS, SCIENCES OR ARTS'. All pages are mounted on tabs. Approximately 331 pages numbered in red ink and pencil from 1 to 186 and 189 to 331, between pages 186 and 189, 20 leaves lettered A to T. Missing pages 125, 126, 149, 150, 187, 188, 265, 266. No page 80. Blank pages are 1, 6, 34, 68, 71,

louvre_cl020230313.medium: Black chalk, gray wash, partially reworked with pen and brown ink

louvre_cl020133419.title: Two Figures Forging Weapons
louvre_cl020133419.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious album, bound in brown-violet paper with green cloth spine. A label from 'Cartloleria di G.F. Ferrini - Roma' is glued on the front endpaper. Contains studies of Italian landscapes and figures, religious and secular subjects. Damaged binding. Contains dried flowers between the front endpaper and folio 1. Folio 13 very fragile. H. 0.118; L. 0.179.

Inscription on the label: 'French School'. On the first folio, handwritten presentation: 'Representation of Ships constructed by order of Louis XIV, King of France, under the ministry of Jean-Baptiste Colbert, Marquis of Seguelay, based on designs by Charles Lebrun, first painter to the king. Drawn and measured exactly by Jean Berain'. On the second folio, table of names of ships contained in this collection.

louvre_cl020203822.title: Two Angels Holding Flowers
louvre_cl020203822.medium: Belonged to the 'Albums known as Maratta' from the Lambert Krahe collection

louvre_cl020012555.medium: Inscription in pen and brown ink, with inscription above: 'Guild of Assenede'
louvre_cl020012555.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious album. Bound in brown leather, spine bearing the title in gold letters: 'Collection of Coats of Arms', with a label on the upper cover bearing the inscription: 'coats of arms, French School 17th century'. louvre_cl020549716.title: Head of a Young Peasant Woman
louvre_cl020549716.medium: Crayon manner and sanguine printing. Signed lower left: 'F. Boucher inv. del.'; right: 'Demarteau l.ne Sculp.'; lower center: 'from the Cabinet of Madame d'Azaincourt. / In Paris at Demarteau's rue de la Pelterie at the Bell.' Numbered lower right: 'No. 89'. Printed in red sanguine.

louvre_cl020143536.title: Head of an elderly bearded man, in left profile
louvre_cl020143536.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, covered in red cloth, comprising 36 numbered sheets in the upper right in pencil from 1 to 39. H. 0.320; W. 0.248.

louvre_cl020128098.title: Bovines
louvre_cl020128098.description: Description of the album: Factitious album with black leather spine and covers with gold fillets containing 34 folios numbered from 1 to 32. H.69.8 L.56 cm.

louvre_cl020032849.title: Figures in Antique Style, Supporting the Body of a Nude Dead Man
louvre_cl020032849.medium: Pen and black ink, brown wash, black chalk. The drawing extends onto folio 4 recto (left strip of the sheet).
louvre_cl020032849.description: Description of the album: Notebook with green cardboard cover, comprising in its current state 60 sheets of watermarked laid paper (watermark: crowned fleur-de-lis [circa Churchill, 1985, no. 407]) bound in 9 gatherings (for the notebook's structure, see document associated with record RF 29955,1). Vertical chain lines (spacing between chain lines: 28 mm). On the back cover, inscription in pen and brown ink: 'No. 4'. The notebook is foliated in pencil, top right, from 1 to 60, and presents, on the reverse of the back cover (also numbered, in pencil, top left: '61') the label of the paper merchant Niodot.

louvre_cl020206724.title: Astonishment with Fear: head seen from the front
louvre_cl020206724.medium: Black chalk, pen and black ink, on yellowed white paper. Upper right corner added. Annotated in pen and black ink at top: 'Astonishment with fear' and in middle: 'B'; at bottom: 'Composite movement'.
louvre_cl020206724.description: Description of the album: Factitious album assembled by Morel d'Arleux, bringing together 63 drawings, bound in blue calfskin with gold lace, spine with three red leather labels, interior covers in blue and pink marbled paper. On the first page, in pen and black ink: 'French School / volume 18, P - 63 drawings'. The drawings by Le Brun and his workshop relate to the expression of passions, subject of his lectures at the Academy in 1668.

louvre_cl020224845.title: Man in bust, and study of right hand

louvre_cl020215136.title: Study for a sculptural group, and annotations
louvre_cl020215136.medium: black ink with pen; lead pencil
louvre_cl020215136.description: Description of the album: Notebook bound in beige canvas. Contains studies for sculptural groups (Oedipus and the Sphinx, Hope and Poverty, Agriculture and Commerce), address notations, moral and philosophical reflections. Traces of missing folios between folios 8 and 9 and folios 14 and 15. H. 0.148; L. 0.100.

louvre_cl020573637.title: Baby Studies (faces)
louvre_cl020573637.medium: crayon and violet crayon on vellum paper. Pagination in pencil: 23. On verso, pagination in pencil: 24; old pagination in black ink crossed out in pencil: 26
louvre_cl020573637.description: Description of the album: Sketchbook with Lyon cardboard cover (blue), black cloth spine, vellum paper with detachable 'dotted sheets'. Monogram and stamp: 'MAUD' in brown stencil, flat reverse of upper cover / 'Maud' vertical black folio 14). On the front cover: bottom left: Catalogue Raisonné label 'ct 51 (1916)', removed during restoration in 2010.

louvre_cl020225577.title: Lucca: plan of a building
louvre_cl020225577.medium: Inscription in graphite pencil, top left: 'Lucca'. Measurement indications.
louvre_cl020225577.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, bound in marbled paper, with brown leather spine. Inscription in gold letters: 'P. BARBOT. VOYAGE EN ITALIE. 1820-1822. TOSCANE'. On the first folio, handwritten presentation in pen.

louvre_cl020145408.title: Group of soldiers in a parade

louvre_cl020506867.medium: Pen and ink with watercolor enhancement
louvre_cl020506867.description: Description of the album: Printed journal of 64 pages under cover illustrated by Georges Auriol and enhanced with watercolor by Henry Somm. 23 pages contain marginal pen drawings sometimes enhanced with watercolor by Henry Somm. Ex-Libris of Doctor Lucien Graux on the front paste-down. H: 0.160; W: 0.115.

louvre_cl020559352.title: Almanac of 1697. Address Bureau for the Curious

louvre_cl020507215.title: Sailboat at sea; bow of a sailboat at sea
louvre_cl020507215.description: Description of the album: Album with cardboard cover covered in black grained paper, with dark green leather spine, comprising 15 folios. On the front cover, in black and gold letters: 'Album'. Cover: H: 0.119; W: 0.185. Sheet: H: 0.113; W: 0.1743. Label glued at top left on front cover, inscription in ink: E. Isabey 13; in pencil: 5. Traces of missing folios between folios 1 and 2 (numerous), 3 and 4 (1), 10 and 11 (1), 15 and the back endpaper (4). Folios 4 and 5 blank.

louvre_cl020568932.title: Small sheet with drawing of a knot and three decorations

louvre_cl020568932.description: Description of the album: Notebook of notes and sketches called "green notebook", with (modern) cardboard cover covered with almond green paper, identical spine bearing in gold letters the mention CARNET. H: 0.195; L: 0.135. It is composed of beige paper sheets, 3 blank unnumbered, then 97 numbered in pencil, top right, 1 to 97 and then 2 blank unnumbered. Sheet dimensions: H: 0.190; 0.125. Folios 25, 26, 45, 46, 47, 48, 56, 58, 59, 60, 62, 64, 65, 66, 75 are blank.

louvre_cl020221568.title: Study of Legs
louvre_cl020221568.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album, covered with gray cloth, composed of folios. Label glued on the front cover: 'Théodore Chassériau album 9'. L. 0.370, H. 0.330.

It comprises 63 numbered folios. Handwritten annotations. Label on the back of the endpaper: 'Peyrol, paper merchant, rue Taitbout, 36'. H: 0.260; W: 0.360. The binding is broken: the lower cover is detaching from the album.

Composed of sheets, it brings together copies after the antique as well as studies of the Life of Christ. Traces of missing folios between folios 6bis and 7, 33 and 34, 34 and 35, 35 and 36, 40 and 41, 49 and 50, 55 and 56, 56 and 57, 57 and 58.

At folio 71; small envelope containing a fragment of the old mounting of drawing inv 25111. At folios 85 and 86, handwritten inventory of the drawing book that contained the works of this album. Three blank pages before folio 1. H. 0.295; W. 0.234.

H: 0.195; L: 0.135. It is composed of beige paper sheets, 3 blank unnumbered, then 97 numbered in pencil, top right, 1 to 97 and then 2 blank unnumbered. Sheet dimensions: H: 0.190; 0.125. Folios 25, 26, 45, 46, 47, 48, 56, 58, 59, 60, 62, 64, 65, 66, 75 are blank. Folio 78 is detached. A fragment of the original album cover is attached to the front endpaper, thus forming a gusset, on which is glued a sheet of paper with notes. Another fragment of the original album cover is attached to the contr

louvre_cl020619052.title: Pendants

louvre_cl020015087.title: View of Civitavecchia

louvre_cl020019811.title: Sequence of the 4th Shot
louvre_cl020019811.medium: Brown ink, red ink, green ink, beige wash, blue wash, lead pencil, pen
louvre_cl020019811.description: Description of the album: Album covered in red morocco leather, the upper cover is decorated with a rich frame of Greek key patterns enhanced with rows of pearls and gold denticles. The corner connections are ensured by an acanthus leaf motif ending with a pinecone followed by a ram's head. The album consists of 14 double-pages. It begins with an unnumbered printed double page presenting the title: 'Project of the fireworks display fired at Versailles in the presence of His Majesty on May 15, 1771 on the occasion of the marriage of Monseigneur the Count of Provence, executed by Messrs. Torré, Morel & Seguin, Fireworks Masters to His Majesty': followed by 13 drawings on double-pages numbered 1 to 13.

louvre_cl020553787.title: Model for a masquerade costume
louvre_cl020553787.medium: Pen and brown ink, beige and pink wash, watercolor
louvre_cl020553787.description: A design drawing for a masquerade costume executed in pen and brown ink with beige and pink washes and watercolor. This preparatory study shows an elaborate costume design typical of theatrical or court entertainment traditions.

louvre_cl020216764.title: Three Figure Studies

Title 'Works of Engravings Made after the Paintings and Drawings of the late Antoine Watteau, Flemish Painter of the Royal Academy of Painting and Sculpture. Fourth & Last Part.', Paris, at Gusand's, Notre Dame Gate. Collection in two volumes, this second containing 147 plates.

Blank folios: 101 to 105, 109 to 121, 123 to 131, 136 and 137. 5 sheets torn out between folio 116 and folio 117. Torn folios: 136, 137, 138. H. 0.168; W. 0.108

louvre_cl020228258.title: June 30, 1892, at Peyrelebade, to Emile Schuffenecker
louvre_cl020228258.medium: Signed autograph letter, four pages in octavo
louvre_cl020228258.description: Description of the album: Album in brown sheepskin with relief leather ornaments and other metal ornaments, in oblong format, composed of 40 leaves, not including the endpapers. On the front endpaper, pasted label: 'Album that belonged to Paul Gauguin, containing drawings by Paul Gauguin, Emile Bernard and Louis Roy. H: 0.306; L: 0.213. Supplement: photos of Gauguin, Schuffenecker, Pissarro, and Cézanne. Correspondence: letters from Gauguin and various others. Featured in the exhibition: 'The Ardent Life of Paul Gauguin'. Gazette des Beaux-arts (G. Wildenstein), March 1937, no. 39 in the catalogue, page 41. Folios 19, 39 and 40 blank.

louvre_cl020207496.medium: Black chalk, with white chalk highlights, on beige paper. Fully mounted (18th century mounting); annotated on the verso of the mount, in pen and black ink: '18 la nation'.

L. 0.186; H. 0.257. Title on recto of folio 3 in pen and brown ink: 'Drawings by Alexandre Lenoir and Charles Percier engraved in the work entitled Museum of French Monuments published in year IX by Alexandre Lenoir founder and administrator of the Museum.' Traces of missing folios between folios 18 and 19, 22 and 23, 26 and 27, 30 and 31, 32 and 33, 38 and 39, 42 and 43, 46 and 47, 50 and 51, 54 and 55, 66 and 67.

Contains 3 isolated sheets. Trace of missing folio between folios 29 and 30 and 43 and 44. H: 0.110; L: 0.170.

Traces of missing folios before folio 1, between folios 1 and 2, 6 and 7, 19 and 20, 29 and 30. Folios 23 to 28 are blank.

Numbered, except for the printed pages, from 1 to 74, it offers views of naval combats. H. 0.254; L. 0.354.

louvre_cl020227298.title: Two Birds Drinking
louvre_cl020227298.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, bound in red morocco leather, containing drawings and letters from Chassériau to his father. Folios 1, 2, 6, 87 and 89 are blank. Includes a drawing mounted on a tab (RF 28112) between folios 86 and 87, and a loose sheet (RF 28113.bis). "While the Greeks were still fighting against the Ottomans for their independence, the French consul in Egypt, Bernardino Drovetti, a great collector of antiquities, suggested to the viceroy Mehemet Ali the strategic sending of a giraffe to France (24)...The "Courrier français" of July 5, 1827 evokes this procession "The giraffe has arrived in Paris a few days ago. A negro from Darfur, named Atir, and a Moor from Sennar, named Hassan, both sent by the pasha of Egypt, and wearing the turb

louvre_cl020112527.title: Decorative Motifs and Grotesques
louvre_cl020112527.medium: Pen and brown ink, gray wash over some black chalk strokes. Separation lines in pen and brown ink.
louvre_cl020112527.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious album of drawings, comprising 139 folios, bound in calfskin, with gilded tools and six ribs on the spine. The upper cover bears the inscription: 'LVOS' and the lower cover: 'ATNRE'. The folios do not bear manuscript numbering, however, numbering is present at the top right of the pages, in old handwriting (from 1 to 183). The drawings in the album are by François Bourlier (J. Thuillier, 1993), except for a few that seem to be by the hand of François Perrier. H. 0.401; L. 0.271 (binding) and H. 0.394; L. 0.269 (folio).

louvre_cl020131343.title: Sailboats near a jetty
louvre_cl020131343.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, with green leather cover with gold fillets, composed of 41 numbered folios. H. 0.700; L. 0.560.

louvre_cl020032367.description: Album description: Sketch album, covered in gray cloth, with broken elastic closure and small pencil holder. 42 folios. Round label on the back cover with the inscription: "AL1". Studio stamp on several folios. Traces of cut-out folios. H. 0.086; W. 0.130. Note an attached engraving on folio 10, recto, an attached photograph on folio 29, recto.

louvre_cl020035805.title: Study of a hooded monk, seated three-quarters to the left

louvre_cl020101520.title: The Holy Family with the young Saint John, in a landscape
louvre_cl020101520.medium: Black stone, partially reworked with pen and brown ink, abundant gray and brown wash, white highlights. Later retouching in brown wash. Fully mounted.

louvre_cl020565295.medium: graphite on paper. On verso, graphite annotations: M. Chabroche 33-35 Rodia - M. Pompon (?) 8 passage de l'industrie

louvre_cl020226993.title: Three Studies of Seated Women
louvre_cl020226993.description: Album description: Fourth of five notebooks of different formats composing a factitious album of rectangular form, bound in white parchment, placed in a box also made of parchment, and assembled towards the end of the last century by collector Xavier Haas. Unlike the others, this notebook does not appear to be by the artist's hand, and would rather contain studies traced or copied after Romney (cf. J. Roberts in exhibition cat. 'From Across the Channel: British art in French public collections', Paris, Musée du Louvre, 1994). This notebook measures: H. 22.5 cm; W. 37.2 cm, it contains 38 sheets and 46 drawn pages and blank folios (1 and 46 to 47). The overall binding measures: H. 30 cm; W. 48 cm.

louvre_cl020023162.title: Two fishing ships arranged side by side

[below the printer's mark]: They are sold in Antwerp on Camer street / at the sign of the golden sun, by me / Iehan Richard.

Florence, Nicolo de Lorenzo della Magna, 30 VIII 1481. 2°, illustrated. It includes 19 engravings illustrating Hell. The first two are printed, the following ones pasted. The first engraving at the bottom of the page is complete; engravings 3, 6 and 14 are from the 1st state, numbers 7, 9, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18 and 19 from the second state.

Some folios bear the workshop mark: 'Fortuny'. H. 0.150; L. 1.450;

louvre_cl020108955.title: The Manna in the Desert and Moses Striking Water from the Rock
louvre_cl020108955.medium: brown ink with pen; wash (brown); black chalk

louvre_cl020036630.title: Detail of the facade of Villa Corsini, term with bearded head and three architectural motifs
louvre_cl020036630.medium: Pen and brown ink, gray wash over graphite underdrawing
louvre_cl020036630.description: Description of the album: This sketchbook was drawn during the artist's stay in Rome between 1692 and 1699. It is now covered with a parchment binding, contemporary to its creation. It bears on the upper cover the autograph signature Oppenord. The inscription on the back of the binding, ESQUISE DE OPPENOR, is by another hand and probably later. The sketchbook now consists of ninety-seven sheets, three of which have been added (two at the beginning and one at the end), most likely during the 19th or 20th century.

The notebook is foliated in pencil, top right, from 1 to 57, and presents, on the reverse of the back cover, the label of the paper merchant Niodot. Lenoir TI / Museum of French Monuments...". Also found there is an old photograph reproducing the painting by Léon Mathieu Cocherau "Interior of a hall of the museum of French monuments". louvre_cl020596567.description: Description of the album: [Frontispiece:] Historical games of the kings of France, renowned Queens, geography and metamorphosis / By the late Mr J. DESMARETS Counselor/ Secretary & Controller General of / the Extraordinary of Wars / And engraved by DO LA BELLA. These same games are accommodated as easy cards / to play, & will be sold separately .....IN PARIS, / At NICOLAS LE CLERC's, on the Quay / des Augustins, at the descent of Pont S. Michel, near the hotel de Luynes, at the image S.Lambert / AND / At FLORENT LE COMTE's, rue S. Jacques, near / the Fountain S. Benoist, at the Royal Cipher / M.DC. XCVIII. / With the King's Privilege'. Notice to the reader. This volume contains four games invented in 1644 by Jean Desmarets de Saint-Sorlin to serve in the education of Louis XIV.

louvre_cl020521425.title: The Toilette

In a cartouche at the bottom of the composition, the title and four doubled verses: 'THE TOILETTE/ Butterfly fluttering from toilette to toilette / The fashionable man wants to captivate at once / Both the mistress and the maid / And his lovers of the day will all three be deceived.' Under the square line at bottom left 'I. H. E. inv S. Freudeberg del'. In the center, the printer's address 'In Paris at Buldet rue des Gesvres', on the right 'Voyez l'aine Sculp. 1774'

louvre_cl020140161.description: Album description: Cardboard album of violet papier-mâché, with violet cloth spine, composed of 69 sheets and 8 attached drawings. The cover is no longer attached to the sheets today. It offers studies of statues and medallions. Trace of missing folio between folios 21 and 22. H: 0.145; L: 0.098.

louvre_cl020119813.description: Description of the album: Black notebook, with blue spine, composed of 45 numbered folios, of which folios 33 to 36, and 38 to 44, are blank. It brings together landscape and figure studies. H: 0.163; W: 0.105.

louvre_cl020032337.title: Profile of a man, and head silhouette

On the front cover, round white label with pencil inscription: "AL10". On the back cover, supplier's label. Workshop stamp on several folios. Several sheets have been torn out (between folios 2 to 3 and 3 to 4 and 26 to 27,...). H. 0.093; L. 0.119

louvre_cl020126585.title: Church Between Two Trees

Wax seal on the front cover with the initials 'ED'. Folios 15 and 36 blank. Trace of missing folio between folios 3 and 4. H. 0.179; L. 0.223.

On the front cover, in gold letters: 'Album'. At the beginning and end of the notebook, numerous handwritten annotations, addresses, dates from a trip to London. Project concerning the arrival of the Queen of England. Cover: H. 0.141; W. 0.214. Sheet: H. 0.132; W. 0.210.

louvre_cl020232710.title: Manuscript Notes
louvre_cl020232710.description: Description of the album: Album bound in dark blue leather with metal clasp. The leather spine is damaged, the pencil holder is torn, and the back cover is separated from the gusset and spine of the album. On the clasp: Henry Penny / patent. Label on the gusset of the back endpaper: Henry Penny's / patent / improved metallic / books... Traces of missing sheets between folios 24 and 25, 2 sheets between folios 40 and 41, 1 sheet between folios 42 and 43, 56 and 57. It contains views of Italy, some photographs and numerous manuscript notes. H: 0.109; W: 0.190.

louvre_cl020006093.medium: Black stone, pen and brown ink, brown wash and white highlights, on brown-washed paper; H. 284; W. 335 mm; arched form. Fully mounted on a Mariette mount: H. 400; W. 492 mm. On the mount, in a cartouche, in pen and brown ink: ALEX. TIARINI
louvre_cl020006093.description: A baroque drawing depicting a religious figure in a moment of divine encounter, executed in black stone with brown ink wash and white highlights on brown-washed paper, presented in an arched format typical of religious compositions of the period.

louvre_cl020023755.title: Church at Saint-Ail

louvre_cl020023755.description: Description of the album: Notebook covered in beige cloth with elastic closure, comprising 34 numbered folios. Trace of missing folio between folios 14 and 15 and 15 and 16. Some of these folios are detaching. H: 0.121; W: 0.162.

louvre_cl020230196.title: Bonaparte Raising Up the City of Lyon

louvre_cl020119548.title: Sketch
louvre_cl020119548.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album in green paper, with green leather spine, composed of 42 foliated sheets numbered at the top right. Trace of missing folio between folios 18 and 19. Folio 41 is torn. A label is glued on the front paste-down: 'A la palette de Rubens - Leroy - rue de Sèvres, N° 6'. H: 120; W: 0.200.

louvre_cl020112677.title: Nude man in a contorted pose, study of right arm, sketches, lines and inscriptions
louvre_cl020112677.medium: Pen and brown ink, black chalk, stylus. Inscriptions in pen and brown ink, at top: 'Laur (?)'; in center, upside down: 'Rachoglieteli ["li"; in different handwriting?] / A [in different handwriting?] piè del nossro [to read: nostro]Cri/ sto' and at bottom: 'Som[m]o dio fuss[e] / In pensier / fusse que[s]to' and other illegible words. Fully mounted on a cut Mariette mount. Mount dimensions: H. 00,355 m; W. 00,183 mm
louvre_cl020112677.description: This Renaissance drawing presents a nude male figure in a contorted pose alongside studies of a right arm, executed in pen and brown ink with black chalk and stylus. The work features multiple sketches and various inscriptions in Italian, including religious references to Christ and invocations to the supreme God. The inscriptions appear in different handwritings and orientations on the sheet, suggesting multiple uses or additions over time.

Verso: upper left, 138 H in graphite, 138 repeated below in pen and brown ink; traces of red chalk rubbing. Watermark: P heart crescent moon / bunch of grapes / 1742 + P fleur de lys CVSSON BVLLE / AUVERGNE 1742.

louvre_cl020011899.title: Juno Seated on Clouds
louvre_cl020011899.description: A drawing depicting Juno, the Roman goddess, seated majestically upon clouds. This work is executed in the trois crayons (three-crayon) technique using red, black, and white chalk. The drawing is related to the theme 'Triumph of Love over the Gods' and connects to works housed at the National Gallery of Art in Washington and the Hôtel d'Argenton in Paris.

louvre_cl020010222.title: The Rest of the Holy Family during the Flight into Egypt

louvre_cl020010222.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, bound in green leather. Studies after antique or religious sculptures. H. 0.700; W. 0.550.

Printed in red sanguine.

louvre_cl020013486.title: Young nude man, seated, turned to the left, hands crossed on his thigh

louvre_cl020100958.title: Triumph of a Roman Emperor (fragment)
louvre_cl020100958.medium: Pen and brown ink, brown wash, ochre highlights, white highlights, beige washed paper. On two pieces of joined paper.

louvre_cl020514316.title: Urania
louvre_cl020514316.medium: Black chalk. Mounted on all four edges. Inscriptions on the sheet, toward the center: 55 four drawings; and at bottom right: 10th four drawings.
louvre_cl020514316.description: Description of the album: Roman album no. 5 composed of 26 sheets on which are mounted or drawn eighty-eight drawings and fifteen tracings. Drawings after the antique of single men, single women, couples, copies of old masters, landscapes, heads, animals, antique objects made between 1775 and 1780, in Rome, its surroundings, Florence, Bologna, Venice. Double numbering in pen and brown ink. A first, generally at bottom right, gives the number of the sheet in the album, the number of drawings found or that were found on the sheet. The second, generally at top right, relates to the original pagination of the two original collections.

louvre_cl020581911.title: The Pretty Aminte, wearing a bonnet hat, ribbons crossed over a plain dress
louvre_cl020581911.description: Album description: H: 0.338 m W: 0.245 m D: 0.016 m Open book: 0.485 m

louvre_cl020209929.title: Portrait of Mademoiselle Mayer, Artist Painter
louvre_cl020209929.medium: Pastel on canvas coated with gray-beige primer and stretched on frame. On the canvas, verso, number in white chalk: 3806. On the back, on the protective cardboard, number in pen and black ink 3,806 and initials L.P. Attached piece of paper with printed number: 650. Label from the Prud'hon exhibition organized in Paris at the Jacquemart-André museum in 1958.
louvre_cl020209929.description: Frame measurements: H: 00,587, L: 00,506 and depth: 00,085. Label on back: Jacquemart André Museum/ 158 bd. Haussman, VIIIe/ Prud'hon Exhibition 1958 / Mlle Mayer / Louvre Museum'. Pen annotations: '3,806', 'L. P.'. Label: '650'.

louvre_cl020038980.title: Silenus carrying the young Bacchus on his shoulders and advancing to the right
louvre_cl020038980.medium: red ink; colored ink with pen; graphite

louvre_cl020119933.title: Andromeda Chained to Her Rock
louvre_cl020119933.description: Description of the album: Account book, bound in brown cloth, composed of 72 sheets, of which folios 2, 30 to 33, 37 to 52, 54 to 56, 59, 61 to 64, 66 to 68 are blank. One folio missing between folios 3 and 4, two between folios 18 and 19, one between folios 36 and 37, two between folio 72 and the back endpaper.

louvre_cl020008295.title: Saint John the Baptist on Clouds

louvre_cl020561614.title: Studio Interior with an Artist and His Model
louvre_cl020561614.medium: pen and brown ink on paper. Drawn lengthwise
louvre_cl020561614.description: Description of the album: Sketch album with cloth cover mounted on cardboard with pencil case, comprising 67 sheets. Traces of missing folio between numbers 5 and 6. Label affixed to the front paste-down (stamped in blue ink) "Former Mon MALHOME/ TARAYRE Successor / TRACING PAPER Factory / PARIS / 79 boulevard Montparnasse, 79". Numbered annotation in upper left in pencil: 65. Circa 1882-1889. Notebook dimensions: H. 00,090; L. 00,150

louvre_cl020028970.title: Landscape
louvre_cl020028970.description: Description of the album: Cardboard notebook with marbled paper and green spine, composed of 65 folios of drawings or sketch pages, mainly views of Dauphiné, Savoy, and the Aosta Valley. Folio 62 is blank. Dated on the reverse of the upper cover: '1825'. The first drawing bears the date: 'May 26, 1825'. H: 0.105; L: 0.164.

louvre_cl020106281.title: Two children carrying a shield, seated on an imaginary animal
louvre_cl020106281.medium: Belonged to the 'Albums known as Maratta' from the Lambert Krahe collection
louvre_cl020106281.description: A drawing depicting two children carrying a shield while seated on an imaginary animal, executed in black pen and ink with brown wash technique.

louvre_cl020004979.title: A child lifts a veil to look at a nude child lying down

louvre_cl020225918.title: Naples: interior of Virgil's tomb
louvre_cl020225918.description: Description of the album: Factitious album bound in marbled paper with brown leather spine with gold lettering: 'P. BARBOT VOYAGE EN ITALIE. 1820-1822. NAPLES'. On one of the endpapers, in pen. P. BARBOT. Architectural voyage to Italy. 1820-1822'. Naples from May 3 to June 19, 1821'. H: 0.488; W: 0.330.

louvre_cl020138738.title: Studies for a Sculpted Bust
louvre_cl020138738.description: Album description: Notebook bound in gray-beige cloth, composed of 69 foliated sheets, containing compositional and figure studies... A handwritten note inserted indicates subjects such as: 'Dancer-Rothschild / Sword for the Duke of Aumale...'. The 23152 bis is placed between folios 10 and 11. H: 0.149; W: 0.098.

louvre_cl020220065.title: Caricature: a man in the piano and organ gallery
louvre_cl020220065.medium: Inscriptions in pen and brown ink at bottom: 'The piano gallery and its organs - Good heavens! I'm becoming enraged! I've only been in London for 24 hours and I've already heard the hymn [...]'. Cut drawing.
louvre_cl020220065.description: Description of the album: Factitious album with marbled paper covers and brown cloth spine, grouping drawings RF 23618 to RF 24028. Folio 77 is followed by folio 77 bis. H: 0.310; W: 0.445.

louvre_cl020039939.title: Four Scenes Illustrating Fables by La Fontaine
louvre_cl020039939.medium: Graphite pencil annotations at bottom left
louvre_cl020039939.description: Description of the album: Composite album grouping 59 drawings (recto and verso) numbered between inventory numbers RF 32157 to RF 32218. H: 0.545; W: 0.390.

louvre_cl020218539.title: Studies of Porcupine
louvre_cl020218539.medium: gray-beige paper; black stone; white highlights

louvre_cl020558939.title: The Triumph of Maximilian: seventieth plate. Three knights with banners bearing the coats of arms of Libein, Acht Gericht and Reineck
louvre_cl020558939.medium: Woodcut engraving. The number I4, in reverse, is engraved on the right. Watermark of crowned double-headed eagle type Meder (1932) 239a.

louvre_cl020109719.title: Studies of Bustards
louvre_cl020109719.medium: chamois paper; pastel; black stone; white highlights

louvre_cl020217859.title: Six Studies of Arab Women
louvre_cl020217859.medium: Inscriptions: 'rue Philippe n° 14 - M. de Sam ...(?) - seam of the shirt - flower - cherry and gold - sh(irt) - apri(cot) - bluish'.
louvre_cl020217859.description: Description of the album: Factitious cardboard album, covered with blue, brown and violet marbled paper, with dark blue leather spine and corners, foliated from 1 to 70 and containing numerous drawings from Morocco. H. 0.368; W. 0.272.

louvre_cl020538096.title: The Triumph of Paris and Helen
louvre_cl020538096.medium: Engraving. Signed at bottom left with monogram 'NW' and dated 1535.
louvre_cl020538096.description: An engraved work depicting the mythological triumph of Paris and Helen, executed in burin technique and signed with the monogram 'NW', dated to 1535.

louvre_cl020569016.title: The Beggars or Mendicants: The Two Pilgrims
louvre_cl020569016.description: Album description: Bound in red half-morocco with corners. Ex-libris of Baron James Mayer de Rothschild on the front paste-down. H: 0.475 m L: 0.343 m D: 0.050 m Open book: 0.665 m

louvre_cl020563237.title: Nude figure, standing on tiptoes, holding by the bit a winged horse, rearing, front view (Alexander and Bucephalus?). The tip of the wing is drawn on folio 26 recto
louvre_cl020563237.medium: pen and brown ink on paper. Drawn lengthwise
louvre_cl020563237.description: Description of the album: Sketch album with cardboard cover covered in fine blue cloth (rather faded), comprising 29 sheets. Sheet cut 3/4 between folios 14 and 15. Front endpaper: red printed label: PAPETERIE CHAPRON / COQUELIN, SUCr / 34, rue de Seine St Gn / specialty for architecture painting and drawing. Across, in graphite pencil: calculation operations. Back endpaper, across: handwritten annotations in graphite: plaster / cream for carters. Expenses or schedules in two columns: Grandcamp Isigny. Other figures. Indeterminate sketch. In 1896. Notebook dimensions: H. 00,090; L. 00,140

louvre_cl020036422.title: Sketch of a nude man, half-length

louvre_cl020512847.title: Winged Female Figure
louvre_cl020512847.description: Description of the album: Factitious bound album with green cardboard cover speckled with black, comprising 120 drawings in pen and brown ink or lead pencil on tracing paper or paper, glued by themes and mounted on 57 pages numbered in pen and brown ink. Handwritten list glued at the end of the album. Workshop stamp (Lugt, 933) on the drawings. Detached drawings on folios 3, 14, 15, 16, 26, 28, 29. On folio 14, pencil annotation: St Louis raising (?) the cross for the 2nd time / with his three sons. Folios 17, 20, 25, 27, 42, 43, 45, 46, 55 and 56 are missing. Small loose sheet between folio 4 and folio 5, eleven handwritten lines, in pen and black ink, Saint Clair in flesh. First bishop of Nantes / ... H. 0.505; W. 0.350.

louvre_cl020110625.title: The Torments of the Damned in Hell
louvre_cl020110625.medium: brown ink with pen; wash (brown); black chalk; white highlights

louvre_cl020141726.title: Handwritten Note

On the upper paste-down, there is a supplier's label: 'CARTOLERIA' / G.F. FERRINI / Roma / Piazza Colonna 211'. This collection groups together 43 sheets. H: 0.092; L: 0.155.

louvre_cl020502139.title: Sketch of a Man's Torso
louvre_cl020502139.medium: Belonged to the 'Albums known as Maratta' from the Lambert Krahe collection

louvre_cl020514643.title: Sketches after antique works, probably after Greek vases for the figures, on three registers

louvre_cl020514643.description: Description of the album: Sketch album with green cardboard cover (very worn), green leather spine (very worn) with a label glued at the top bearing no. 19, comprising 38 numbered sheets in pencil, top right, 1 to 38. Trace of torn sheet before folio 1 and between folios 11 and 12, 38 and the back endpaper. A sheet placed before folio 1, in modern pen writing, corrected and completed in pencil summarizes the contents of the notebook with references to folios.

louvre_cl020209046.title: Draped Figure; Study of Arm; Drapery Resting on Feet

A handwritten note inserted indicates subjects such as: 'Dancer-Rothschild / Sword for the Duke of Aumale...'. The 23152 bis is placed between folios 10 and 11. H: 0.149; W: 0.098.

louvre_cl020101658.title: A king (Solomon?) overseeing the construction of a building
louvre_cl020101658.medium: brown ink with pen; wash (brown); black chalk

Folios 12, 15, 18, 19, 21 to 24, 28, 29 are blank. Traces of torn folios between the front endpaper and folio 1, between folios 9 and 10, 17 and 18, 18 and 19, 21 and 22, 24 and 25, 25 and 26, 27 and 28, 29 and 30, 30 and 31, 35 and 36, 38 and 39, 41 and 42, 42 and the back endpaper.

louvre_cl020537720.description: A commemorative drawing depicting the tragic death of the Pilâtre de Rozier brothers, shown above their portraits rendered in medallion format. The work combines pen and black ink with gray wash technique to create a memorial illustration documenting this significant moment in early aviation history.

Couture' and a mother-of-pearl button on the right, comprising 39 folios. Folios 3 and 6 are blank. Traces of torn or cut folios between folios 12 and 13, 17 and 18, 28 and 29, 33 and 34, 35 and 36.

louvre_cl020129618.title: Three Horses
