louvre_cl020115322.title: Handwritten text
louvre_cl020115322.description: Description of the album: Manuscript bound in brown suede leather, with a gouache watercolor on the front cover and an engraved wood block on the back cover, comprising 182 sheets on which 59 watercolors are pasted. Bears a title in black: "NOANOA / Journey to / Tahiti". Folio 52 is blank. Folio 51 is missing. Both the old and modern foliation is faulty.

louvre_cl020134518.title: Man near a bedridden figure
louvre_cl020134518.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious album, bound in green marbled paper. Contains landscapes, studies after antique works. One sheet is attached to folio 1. Gap of 3.5 cm on the external edge of folio 36. Contains 42 folios. H. 0.106; W. 0.170.

louvre_cl020562985.title: Standing nude woman, three-quarters to the right, leaning on a table (?) near a tree. Unfinished sketch of a head and an arm
louvre_cl020562985.medium: graphite pencil, pen and brown ink (unfinished sketch) on paper
louvre_cl020562985.description: Description of the album: Sketch album with blue cardboard cover, black cloth spine, comprising 66 sheets. Traces of missing sheets between folios 1 and 2, 5 and 6, 42 and 43, 62 and 63 and after folio 66. Front endpaper: brown-red ink stamp: G SENNELIER / 3 Quai Voltaire. PARIS with in pencil: 0.25. Handwritten annotations in pen and brown ink: Flammarion Librairie 20 rue des Mathurins / Studio 2 / l'art et les artistes 2.25 / le Mercure 1.25.

louvre_cl020110608.title: Jupiter and Mercury at Philemon and Baucis

louvre_cl020563008.description: Description of the album: Sketch album with chamois cardboard cover, black cloth spine, comprising 33 sheets. Traces of missing sheets between folios 10 and 11, 11 and 12, 12 and 13. After 1918. Dimensions of the notebook: H. 0.083; W. 0.110

louvre_cl020504959.title: Interior
louvre_cl020504959.medium: Color indications in graphite. Annotated above the composition in graphite: 'Interior at P[...]'.
louvre_cl020504959.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with burgundy paper comprising 66 sheets. Two labels are glued on the front paste-down: Papeterie Cabasson and Imprimerie au Griffon 11r. de la Lancette, Paris / n°60686. A violet ink stamp of 'Giuseppe Giannini / Firenze / 13, via Tornabueni' is affixed to the front paste-down.

Following an error in the counting of inventory numbers, inventory numbers 491 DR, 492 DR and 493 DR are not assigned. H: 27.6 cm; W: 11.5 cm

louvre_cl020128161.title: Two Seated Children
louvre_cl020128161.description: Description of the album: Factitious album with black leather spine and covers with gold fillets containing 34 folios numbered from 1 to 32. H.69.8 L.56 cm.

louvre_cl020131925.title: Man Pulling a Canoe on the Sand
louvre_cl020131925.description: Description of the album: Factitious album with green leather cover with gold fillets, composed of 41 numbered folios. H. 0.700; W. 0.560.

louvre_cl020032167.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with bistre cloth, comprising 43 numbered sheets of paper in various colors. Some sheets have been torn out (before folio 1, between folios 15 and 16, and folio 27 is cut in half). Stretched closing elastic. Pencil holder on the binding. On the front cover, round white label with pencil inscription: 'AL6'. Supplier label on the front endpaper. Workshop stamp on several folios. H. 0.09; L. 0.142.

louvre_cl020209798.title: Perspective View of Ancy-le-Franc in Burgundy
louvre_cl020209798.medium: Inscription in pen and brown ink, at the bottom under the framing line: 'General view of Ancy-le-Franc in Burgundy [...]'

louvre_cl020614317.title: Trophy
louvre_cl020614317.medium: Watercolored etching. Bottom right: 'Huquier sculp.'. Center: 'with privilege of the King.'
louvre_cl020614317.description: Description of the album: Suite of three books: 'First book of different trophies invented by R. Charpentier sculptor to the King and engraved by Huquier.', 'Second book of different trophies invented by R. Charpentier sculptor to the King and engraved by Huquier.' and 'Third book of cartouches invented by J. de la Joüe (...) by (...) G. Huquier. In Paris, at Huquier's rue St. Jacq. near the Mathur.s H: 00,311 m L: 00,233 m D: 00,015 m Open book: 00,469 m

louvre_cl020213692.medium: Black stone, with white chalk highlights, on beige paper. Fully mounted (18th century mounting); annotated on the verso of the mounting, in pen and black ink: '55 la nation'.

louvre_cl020549779.title: Head of a Woman in Profile

Below: 'From the Cabinet of Monsieur de la Haye'/ 'In Paris at Demarteau Engraver to the King rue de la Pelterie at the Bell. C.P.R. 159'. Printed in red sanguine.

louvre_cl020574698.medium: pencil and green watercolor on vellum paper. Pencil annotations: May 10 Vincennes; in blue ink: Vincennes. Pencil pagination: 31
louvre_cl020574698.description: Description of the album: Notebook with chamois-colored cardboard cover, brown cloth spine ("Canson et Montgolfier n°1010, stamp on the back endpaper), vellum paper, detachable sheets. On the front cover: letter "F" in black ink at top right; recent white label bearing the date in pencil "1934", at bottom left: catalog raisonné label "ct106 (1934) removed during 2010 restoration. On the front endpaper: handwritten autograph annotations (addresses) and non-autograph ones. Round label: 1934. Catalog Raisonné label: Ct106 (1934). Annotation at bottom left in pencil: C 2. On the back endpaper: annotation at bottom right: C 3. Black ink stamp. 38 folios. The pagination of the notebooks in continuous sequence (graphite pencil

louvre_cl020112374.title: View of the Capitoline Square in Rome
louvre_cl020112374.medium: Red chalk, dated in red chalk: '1762'. Restored drawing.

louvre_cl020127550.title: Breton Bed

louvre_cl020127678.title: Meadow by the Edge of a River
louvre_cl020127678.description: Description of the album: Factitious album with black leather spine and covers with gold fillets containing 34 folios numbered from 1 to 32. H.69.8 L.56 cm.

louvre_cl020028795.title: Landscape: at Hökler near Zurich

louvre_cl020028795.description: Description of the album: Blue cardboard album, with blue leather spine, composed of 57 folios numbered at the top right. Trace of missing folio between folio 57 and the back endpaper. H. 0.104; L. 0.174.

louvre_cl020539200.title: Vignette in the form of a frieze with a chimeric man and an eagle

louvre_cl020573003.title: Couple of elderly people dancing to the left

louvre_cl020573003.description: Album description: H: 00.305 m L: 00.247 m D: 00.045 m Open book: 00.288 m

louvre_cl020119169.title: Landscape

BIS. A label from 'Bourienne, Paper merchant, rue des Carmes, no. 77, in Rouen', is glued to the front endpaper.

louvre_cl020115595.title: Handwritten Notes
louvre_cl020115595.description: Description of the album: Manuscript bound in brown suede leather, with a gouache watercolor on the front cover and an engraved wood block on the back cover, comprising 182 folios on which 59 watercolors are pasted. Bears a title in black: "NOANOA / Journey to / Tahiti". Folio 52 is blank. Folio 51 is missing. Both the old and modern foliation is faulty.

louvre_cl020117479.title: Part of a boat; landscape
louvre_cl020117479.medium: Notebook taken in the other direction
louvre_cl020117479.description: Description of the album: Bound notebook composed of 18 sheets. A blue label bearing the inscription: '314 J. Constable' is glued on the front paste-down. Traces of cut-out folios. H: 0.130; L: 0.190.

louvre_cl020013785.title: Head of a child wearing a bonnet
louvre_cl020013785.medium: black chalk; red chalk; stumped black chalk

louvre_cl020026421.title: The Toast
louvre_cl020026421.medium: Annotated above the frames: 'Velásquez / Rembrandt' and dated: 'December 26, 1864'.

louvre_cl020218628.title: Washhouse
louvre_cl020218628.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album with green marbled paper, green leather spine, comprising 43 numbered sheets from 1 to 43, in pencil, at the top right. It is composed of sketches for a painting representing a celebration under the First Empire, President Molé under the barricades, landscapes, figures and handwritten notes.

louvre_cl020143106.title: Handwritten Notes
louvre_cl020143106.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with green paper with a spine of leather in the same color, bearing on the upper cover, in golden gothic letters: 'Album' and a red wax seal. This collection consists of 51 sheets, of which folios 35 to 47 are blank, as well as folio 50. On the front cover, red wax seal. On the front endpaper, label with 'Rouen' (handwritten); packer's label 'Chenue'; label 'Alph: Giroux'. H. 0.126; L. 0.200.

louvre_cl020500436.title: Annotation
louvre_cl020500436.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, covered with brown-red coated cloth, with brown leather corners and spine, bearing on the front cover, in gold letters: 'J.F. Millet / 1814 - 1875' Interior

louvre_cl020026512.title: The Toilette: Woman at Her Toilette, Surrounded by Attendants
louvre_cl020026512.description: Description of the album: Oblong factitious album bound in gray-beige cloth; it includes drawings RF 12540 to RF 12657. The 'Fantin' stamp is affixed to the endpaper and the stamp of the National Museum of Luxembourg on the front

louvre_cl020036261.title: Reclining Nude Man, Leaning on a Lion: a River

louvre_cl020001365.title: Man, seen from behind, leaning forward

louvre_cl020041656.title: Bearded Man, Seen in Bust, Profile to the Right

louvre_cl020216947.title: Studies of Wild Boars
louvre_cl020216947.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious album, bound in green paper with black cloth spine. The lower corner of both front and back covers is torn. Double numbering of folios. It includes studies after Michelangelo, Titian, Carracci and studies after antique works. H. 0.150; W. 0.232.

louvre_cl020548968.title: France Groans from the Troubles that Divide Her
louvre_cl020548968.medium: Etching and engraving. Engraved signature at bottom left of the square line: 'Painted by François Boucher.' and at right: 'Engraved by Lau. Cars.' Then the inscription: 'France groans from the troubles that divide her. Fidelity consoles her, & presents her with zealous subjects. One of them holds a pomegranate, symbol of union. The sky opens; the Holy Spirit appears. Geniuses seem to inspire the idea of instituting an Order. The guardian Angel of France strikes down the enemies of the State & of Religion, while Peace & Abundance announce happier times.'
louvre_cl020548968.description: Etching and engraving. Engraved signature at bottom left of the square line: 'Painted by François Boucher.' and at right: 'Engraved by Lau. Cars.' Then the inscription: 'France groans from the troubles that divide her. Fidelity consoles her, & presents her with zealous subjects. One of them holds a pomegranate, symbol of union. The sky opens; the Holy Spirit appears. Geniuses seem to inspire the idea of instituting an Order. The guardian Angel of France strikes down the enemies of the State & of Religion, while Peace & Abundance announce happier times.'

louvre_cl020625204.title: Interior of a Romanesque church, timber vault, central nave flanked by columns
louvre_cl020625204.medium: Pen and black ink, gray wash, pencil strokes. Missing along the right edge. Stitch holes along the left edge. Glued along the left edge with a paper strip attached to the verso.
louvre_cl020625204.description: Description of the album: Sketch album, with red leather binding (H. 0.200; L. 0.235) comprising 91 foliated sheets from 1 to 90, on which are mounted 68 drawings in graphite, wash..., representing landscapes, palace interiors, courtyards, studies after the antique, after the masters, portraits. On the back of the lower cover, supplier's label from Prouverre de la Pommerie Md Papetier rue Neuve des Petits Champs.

louvre_cl020609763.title: Colonnade
louvre_cl020609763.description: Album description: Notebook of 44 sheets presenting 60 drawings of different techniques on 47 sheets, made in Rome in the years 1754-1765 then in Paris, in the years 1765-1770. Covered with its original binding in green paper cardboard, with red edges, with a partially erased inscription, in pen and brown ink on the cover: "all kinds of Sketches / made by Mr (R)ob(ert) (painter) / of the Former acade(my)". On about 20 pages, we see the first known drawings of the artist, imaginary architectures in pen and wash executed upon his arrival in Rome in 1754, there are also picturesque sketches of Roman life. Back in Paris, around 1773, Hubert Robert turns the notebook in the opposite direction and continues to use it, he draws there

louvre_cl020501890.title: A plant from Cels' garden: Polygonum polygamum (Polygonaceae)
louvre_cl020501890.medium: Watercolor and gray wash. Signed with pen: 'P. J. Redouté pinx'. Annotated: 'Plant no. 65'.
louvre_cl020501890.description: Description of the album: Original drawings from the Description of new and little-known plants cultivated in Cels' garden, accompanied by 12 unpublished drawings by Redouté. Album containing 12 watercolors from the Garden of La Malmaison and 100 (+ 1 sketch) watercolors and washes from Cels' Garden.

louvre_cl020108556.title: Resurrection of Christ
louvre_cl020108556.medium: brown ink with pen; white highlights; paper prepared in gray-mauve

louvre_cl020576993.title: The Famine
louvre_cl020576993.description: Album description: H: 00.302 m L: 00.247 m D: 00.040 m Open book: 00.475 m

louvre_cl020131781.title: Study of a Sailing Ship
louvre_cl020131781.description: Description of the album: Factitious album with green leather cover with gold fillets, composed of 41 numbered folios. H. 0.700; W. 0.560.

louvre_cl020131300.title: Sailboat Presenting from the Stern
louvre_cl020131300.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, with green leather cover with gold fillets, composed of 41 numbered folios. H. 0.700; L. 0.560.

louvre_cl020580369.title: Baron Kray
louvre_cl020580369.description: Album description: H: 0.628 m W: 0.489 m D: 0.038 m Open book: 0.977 m

louvre_cl020548812.title: The Fortune Telling
louvre_cl020548812.medium: Signed below the square line: on the left, 'F. Boucher inv.' - on the right, 'P. Aveline Sculpsit.' Below the title: 'Sold in Paris at Huquier's, With Royal Privilege.' Watermark: 'T (well) D'

louvre_cl020117318.title: Character Sketch
louvre_cl020117318.description: Description of the album: Sketchbook covered in dark green leather. Contains sketches of characters and animals. Pencil holder on the back cover. On the verso of the endpaper, oval label with: "Stationery and office supplies Guillaume Contarel". Folios 4 to 12 and 14 to 22 are blank. Traces of missing folios between folios 8 and 9, 28 and 29, 32 and 33. H. 0.088; L. 0.155.

louvre_cl020572342.title: The Bible of the Poor. The glorification of the Church; The coronation of eternal life; An angel speaks to Saint John
louvre_cl020572342.medium: Hand-colored woodcut printed with brown ink by rubbing. Watermarks: anchor surmounted by a cross; ox heads with eyes, ear and nose surmounted by a cross
louvre_cl020572342.description: Description of the album: Xylographic book published in the Netherlands around 1465. - 2°, 40 folios, [1-20²] printed on one side only from woodblocks corresponding to two pages at once. Each plate is signed: A, b-v,.a.-.m, n-o, .p.-q., r-s, .t.-.v., illustrated. This is the first edition. Complete copy, 40 hand-colored plates with Latin text.

louvre_cl020567886.title: View of Lake Bolsena
louvre_cl020567886.medium: Etching. Below the square line: "Silvestre fe.", "View of Lake Bolsena.", on the right: "Israel excudit"

louvre_cl020009798.title: Young nude man, standing, turned to the left, holding a cup
louvre_cl020009798.medium: Red chalk. Stains. Trace of horizontal fold in the middle. Old mounting: H. 0.727; W. 0.533, blue paper, black framing line, gold band, blue band, black framing line against the drawing. Watermark: AM heart surmounted by a fleur-de-lis VIMAL + bunch of grapes.

louvre_cl020219831.title: Boy wearing a cap, standing in left profile

louvre_cl020102350.title: Apostle and a child seated on clouds
louvre_cl020102350.medium: brown paper; black stone; white highlights

louvre_cl020624905.title: Landscape Sketch
louvre_cl020624905.description: Description of the album: Album of drawings bound in vellum. 56 folios numbered with pen in the upper right, no blank folios. H: 19.1 cm; W: 8.5 cm

louvre_cl020009547.title: First French attack at the Battle of Montebello in view of the city of Casteggio on June 8, 1800
louvre_cl020009547.medium: Gouache on paper. Annotation, bottom left: 'Battle of Casteggio, by Bagetti' H. 50.3; W. 78.5 cm. frame: H. 66.2; W. 181.3; D. 5 cm.

louvre_cl020598629.title: Saint Peter
louvre_cl020598629.description: Album description: H: 00,410 L: 00,265 D: 00,042 Open book: 00,547

louvre_cl020214341.title: A nude man, lying on the ground and a woman

louvre_cl020506652.title: Discharge of a handwritten note and a sketch
louvre_cl020506652.description: Description of the album: Bound notebook of beige canvas, composed of 43 white and different colored sheets; it contains figure studies and handwritten notes. Traces of missing folios between folios 5 and 6, 14 and 15, 25 and 26, 28 and 29, 30 and 31. H: 0.095; L: 0.128.

louvre_cl020105109.title: Drapery Study for a Sleeve
louvre_cl020105109.medium: gray paper; black chalk; white highlights; stumped black chalk

louvre_cl020575175.title: List of work and accounts
louvre_cl020575175.medium: graphite on paper. Folio used lengthwise
louvre_cl020575175.description: Description of the album: Sketch album with cloth cover mounted on cardboard (linen cloth) and pencil holder, comprising 49 sheets. Traces of missing sheets between folios 8 verso and 9 recto, 15 verso and 16 recto, 29 verso and 30 recto, 43 verso and 44 recto. Annotated on the front cover in ink and 1895 (graphite). Front endpaper, crosswise and turning the album: handwritten notes, including addresses, numbers and series of accounts.

louvre_cl020009842.title: A Choir of Angels
louvre_cl020009842.medium: Red chalk. Verso: upper left, 22 a in graphite, 22 reworked in pen and black ink. Watermark: unicorn in a circle surmounted by a P.

louvre_cl020002436.title: The Beheading of Saint John the Baptist
louvre_cl020002436.medium: Pen and brown ink, brown wash on beige paper

louvre_cl020134724.title: Altarpiece; handwritten note
louvre_cl020134724.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious album, bound in marbled paper with black leather spine. Contains a landscape study, character sketches, and numerous handwritten notes. Gaps on folios 31 and 34. Contains 42 folios. H. 0.115; W. 0.157.

louvre_cl020526602.title: The Virgin, the Christ Child; Saint Catherine and Saint Rose

louvre_cl020028288.title: Bust of a woman, in profile, hair contained by a hairnet
louvre_cl020028288.description: Description of the album: Factitious cardboard album, covered with marbled paper, with parchment spine. Foliated from 1 to 61 then 6 blank unnumbered folios. H. 0.540; L. 0.395.

louvre_cl020131077.title: Fishing boat, viewed from the front
louvre_cl020131077.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, with back and covers of dark green leather, with golden fillets, offering numerous seaside views. H.0.770; L. 0.600.

louvre_cl020229424.title: Landscape of a small town by a mountain lake
louvre_cl020229424.description: Description of the album: Belongs to a cardboard album, covered in beige cloth, containing studies of cathedrals, farms, landscapes... the pastels have been mounted. The album contains 30 folios. Many folios are missing. The album contains 13 sheets of tissue paper, with counter-proofs: between folios 14-15, counter-proof of RF39287, between folios 15-16, counter-proof of RF39298, between folios 16-17, counter-proofs of RF39299, RF39283, RF 39306, RF39290.

louvre_cl020514338.title: View of the staircase leading to the Portico of Vignola at the Capitol
louvre_cl020514338.medium: Black chalk, brush and brown wash. Mounted at four corners. Inscription on the sheet, upper right: 141 four drawings.
louvre_cl020514338.description: Description of the album: Roman album no. 5 composed of 26 sheets on which are mounted or drawn eighty-eight drawings and fifteen tracings. Drawings after the antique of single men, single women, couples, copies of old masters, landscapes, heads, animals, antique objects made between 1775 and 1780, in Rome, its surroundings, Florence, Bologna, Venice.

louvre_cl020506260.title: Silhouettes
louvre_cl020506260.description: Description of the album: Factitious album with brown leather spine and covers with 5 gilded fillets. Contains 35 numbered sheets from 1 to 34. Damaged binding spine (missing the upper part). H.0.735 L.0.580.

louvre_cl020626174.title: Charles de Bourbon, Duke of Vendôme

louvre_cl020127998.title: Group of men on a quay
louvre_cl020127998.description: Description of the album: Factitious album with black leather back and covers with gold fillets containing 34 folios numbered from 1 to 32. H.69.8 L.56 cm.

louvre_cl020106813.title: Study of a Stag, a Doe and a Dog

louvre_cl020029693.title: Monuments in a Landscape
louvre_cl020029693.description: Description of the album: Dismembered album, having lost its cover and binding, composed of 34 folios of drawings offering views of Italy, boats, landscapes. Folio 14 blank. On sheet 4, musical text with the dedication below: 'To his friend Michallon Hortense Gigli, 1821'. H. 0.115; W. 0.177.

louvre_cl020143208.title: Lines and Dots
louvre_cl020143208.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with greenish gray-brown paper, with green leather spine, composed of 42 sheets of different colors. Sheets 36, 38 are detached. Folios 35, 36, 40, 41 are blank. Stamp with initials "ED" on several folios: 1, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 18, 19, 27. H. 0.095; L. 0.150.

louvre_cl020042850.title: Dancers
louvre_cl020042850.description: A drawing executed in Conté crayon depicting dancers in movement, capturing the dynamic energy and grace of dance performance through gestural sketching techniques.

louvre_cl020230637.title: Death of Saint Faraise
louvre_cl020230637.medium: brown wash; blue paper; brush; white highlights
louvre_cl020230637.description: Description of the album: Album covered in brown leather, quite worn, containing forty-three drawings representing holy figures. H. 0.333; W. 0.267.

louvre_cl020123900.title: Head of a man or woman, wearing a cloth twisted around the head

louvre_cl020105079.title: Study of Three Children's Heads
louvre_cl020105079.medium: gray paper; black stone; white highlights

louvre_cl020003701.title: A cupid with arms raised to the sky, with a greyhound

louvre_cl020571799.title: Paradise
louvre_cl020571799.medium: Engraving. At bottom left outside the square line is engraved: 'I. Callot fecit' then at far right outside the square line is engraved: '15'.
louvre_cl020571799.description: Album description: Bound in red half-morocco with corners. Ex-libris of Baron James Mayer de Rothschild on the front paste-down. H: 0.470 m L: 0.345 m D: 0.047 m Open book: 0.654 m

louvre_cl020144899.title: Double study of a man, bust and half-length, three-quarters to the right
louvre_cl020144899.medium: Previously part of the Cappiello album -5-

louvre_cl020133833.title: Studies of Interlaced Letters
louvre_cl020133833.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious notebook bound in gray cloth, presenting various studies, often added, and handwritten notes. Traces of missing folios between folios 17 and 18, 30 and 31, 36 and 37, 39 and 40, and after folio 46. An 'S' is drawn on the upper cover. H. 0.104; L. 0.170.

louvre_cl020554638.title: Costumes of Festivals, Masquerades, Theaters, etc., of Louis XIV
louvre_cl020554638.description: A drawing documenting the elaborate costumes used in festivals, masquerades, and theatrical performances during the reign of Louis XIV, executed in brown ink with pen and gray wash technique.

louvre_cl020577369.title: Chamber Undress
louvre_cl020577369.medium: Etching enhanced with burin. Signed towards the middle right: Bonnart, del et sculp
louvre_cl020577369.description: Album description: H: 00.352 m W: 00.278 m D: 00.048 m Open book: 00.548 m

louvre_cl020106906.title: Letter D, inverted, decorated with a diviner
louvre_cl020106906.medium: gray wash; beige paper; white highlights; graphite

louvre_cl020102273.title: Seated Woman, Draped, Turned to the Right
louvre_cl020102273.medium: Belonged to the 'So-called Maratta Albums' from the Lambert Krahe collection

louvre_cl020537957.title: Seated Virgin Nursing the Infant Jesus
louvre_cl020537957.description: A drawing depicting the Virgin Mary seated while breastfeeding the infant Jesus, executed in pen and brown ink technique.

louvre_cl020216574.title: Sculpted Altar and Retable with Instruments of the Passion
louvre_cl020216574.medium: brown ink with pen; wash (gray); lead pencil
louvre_cl020216574.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album with marbled paper, white spine, with a blue leather label bearing in gold letters: 'J. Dubois/ Collection of original drawings'. It comprises 2 engravings and 168 drawings, most of which are pen drawings washed with bistre or India ink. The first 135 are by Jean Dubois; the others by Guillaume.

louvre_cl020595994.title: Frontispiece
louvre_cl020595994.description: Description of the album: [Title:] Historical eulogies of the bishops and archbishops of Paris, who have governed this Church for about a century, until the death of M. François de Harlay-Chanvalon, appointed by the King to the Cardinalate by Etienne Algay de Martignac. - In Paris, at François Muguet's, First Printer to the King, to the Clergy of France, & to M. the Archbishop, rue de la Harpe, at the three Kings. MDCXCVIII. With Approbation & Privilege of the King. Attributed to Etienne Algay de Martignac in the royal privilege. - Address to Paule de Gondi; Approbation of M. l'Abbé Courcier; Privilege of the King - Sig.: A4-N4, O2. - Illustrations: mark at the title; headpieces and tailpieces engraved on wood by Pierre Le Sueur placed before the title of each biography; 5 full-page portraits engraved on copper by

louvre_cl020222408.title: Five Studies of Feet
louvre_cl020222408.description: Description of the album: Composite album covered with blue, white, pink and burgundy marbled paper, with white spine, and with a red leather rectangle on the front cover indicating 'Théodore Chasseriau Album A6'. It contains 51 folios. H: 0.540; W: 0.390.

louvre_cl020022885.title: Handwritten Notes
louvre_cl020022885.description: Album description: Cardboard album covered with brown embossed paper with foliage motifs, with brown leather spine, comprising 33 sheets and 2 attached drawings (RF 10891 and RF 10892). H: 0.152; W: 0.238.

louvre_cl020215350.title: Studies for Sculptural Groups
louvre_cl020215350.description: Description of the album: Notebook bound in beige cloth. Contains sculpture studies: 'A Mother Woman', 'A Sundial', 'Courage', and a sketch for the Balzac monument. A label: 'TARAYRE stationery 78 bd de Montparnasse' on the back cover. 2 torn folios between folios 33 and 34. 1 torn folio between folios 57 and 58. 3 loose sheets in the notebook. Folios 60 and 61 fragile. H. 0.160; W. 0.103.

louvre_cl020025155.title: Boats on a canal bordered by houses
louvre_cl020025155.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album in marbled paper with paper spine, composed of 12 sheets paginated from 1 to 24 at the top right or from 1 to 12 at the bottom right, featuring landscapes; city views; boats. Dated by Moreau-Nélaton to 1843. The binding and certain sheets are detaching; sheet 11 is partially torn.

louvre_cl020104708.title: Moses Presenting the Tablets of the Law to the People
louvre_cl020104708.medium: brown ink with pen; wash (brown); red chalk

louvre_cl020037085.title: View of San Gimignano
louvre_cl020037085.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious album. Beige cloth cover. Landscapes and architectural views: Lake Geneva, Neuchâtel, Besançon, Royat, Clermont-Ferrand, Riom, Siena, San Gimignano. One drawing is dated 1917. H. 0.120; W. 0.180.

louvre_cl020501891.title: A plant from Cels' garden: Nepeta longiflora (Lamiaceae)
louvre_cl020501891.medium: Watercolor and gray wash. Signed with pen: 'P. J. Redouté del'. Annotated: 'Plant no. 66'.
louvre_cl020501891.description: Description of the album: Original drawings from the Description of new and little-known plants cultivated in Cels' garden, accompanied by 12 unpublished drawings by Redouté. Album containing 12 watercolors from

louvre_cl020141339.title: Two Studies of a Woman Riding a Dolphin
louvre_cl020141339.description: Album description: Semi-factitious notebook bound in beige canvas with pencil holder. Composed of 54 sheets, it brings together projects for statues and monuments. H: 0.095; L: 0.159.

louvre_cl020121474.title: Horse in left profile and rearing horse held by a soldier
louvre_cl020121474.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with green paper, with green leather spine, composed of 45 sheets of various colors, numbered from 1 to 44, with a folio 18bis. Wax seal on the front cover with the initials 'ED'. Folios 15 and 36 blank. Trace of missing folio between folios 3 and 4. H. 0.179; L. 0.223.

louvre_cl020001797.title: Study of Draperies
louvre_cl020001797.medium: Red chalk, traces of white chalk, stumping, on beige paper

louvre_cl020118183.title: Large Trees
louvre_cl020118183.description: Description of the album: Bound album with cardboard cover covered in purple embossed paper and brown cloth spine, composed of 138 sheets. On the front cover, wax seal from the Corot sale. At the top of the first page, Corot noted in pencil: 'Given in Brest, May 11, 1857, by Mrs. Camille Bernier'. Missing folio between 55 and 56. Blank folios: 101 to 105, 109 to 121, 123 to 131, 136 and 137. 5 sheets torn out between folio 116 and folio 117. Torn folios: 136, 137, 138. H. 00,168; L. 00,108

louvre_cl020227422.title: Nude Woman, Half-Length, Holding a Cornucopia Under Her Right Arm
louvre_cl020227422.description: Album description: This drawing is part of an album bound in red leather, with gold motifs bearing on the back in gold letters: 'DESCINS PREM. VAULUM'. H. 0.517; L. 0.347

louvre_cl020126458.title: Sketch of a Woman's Head
louvre_cl020126458.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered in black leather, gilt-edged, composed of 86 numbered sheets on grid paper, of which folios 36, 59 to 74, 76, 77 to 85, 79, 81 are blank. Two missing folios between folios 36 and 37, one between folios 37 and 38 and between folios 85 and 86.

louvre_cl020142931.title: Studies after Oriental Figures
louvre_cl020142931.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with green-black marbled paper with red leather spine, composed of 43 numbered sheets from 1 to 43, on paper of various colors, of which folios 6, 7, 10, 11, 14, 16, 19, are blank. Trace of torn folio between folios 41 and 42. On the front cover, red stamp "ED" from Delacroix's sale. White label glued next to the stamp. H. 0.140; L. 0.216.

louvre_cl020135657.title: Flute Player Walking to the Left
louvre_cl020135657.medium: red ink; colored ink with pen

louvre_cl020567824.title: Views of Paris: view of the King and Queen's apartments from the garden side
louvre_cl020567824.medium: Etching. Below the square line is engraved: 'View and Perspective of the part of the Louvre where are the / apartments of the King and Queen on the Garden side' Bottom right: 'Israel excud.'

louvre_cl020003004.title: Cupid; the little Saint John; putto; two studies for the blessing Christ Child; two studies of a young man-at-arms; nude man running; profile of a young man in bust
louvre_cl020003004.medium: Pen and brown ink, metalpoint, on watermarked paper. Watermark close to Briquet 8348-8349, 8354. Restored in 2003

louvre_cl020549176.title: The Discreet Messenger
louvre_cl020549176.medium: Etching and burin. At bottom left of the square line: 'Boucher', at right: 'Gaillard'. Watermark: eagle.

louvre_cl020204499.title: Profile of a Woman
louvre_cl020204499.medium: Belonged to the 'Albums known as Maratta' from the Lambert Krahe collection.

louvre_cl020118388.title: Leaning Figure
louvre_cl020118388.description: Description of the album: Brown-violet notebook composed of 60 foliated sheets. The red wax seal from the Corot sale is affixed to the back cover, and the sale seal on the recto folio 1. Two folios missing between folio 30 and folio 30 bis. Folio 30 bis torn. H: 0.099; L: 0.151.

louvre_cl020016351.title: Nude Woman Lying on an Animal Skin

louvre_cl020232337.title: A Sailboat at Sea
louvre_cl020232337.description: Description of the album: Bound notebook, cardboard, covered with beige cloth, containing 38 folios. On the front endpaper, label of the stationer E. Chenoz, color house, and on the back cover, space to store a pencil. H. 0.080; L. 0.121.

louvre_cl020136933.title: Man Seated in an Armchair
louvre_cl020136933.medium: Notebook taken in the other direction
louvre_cl020136933.description: Description of the album: Sketch notebook with cardboard cover of blue and black marbled paper, with brown-red cloth spine, comprising 42 folios. Three drawings are attached, including one drawn on an envelope, or former cover of this notebook, (RF 23115.3); it offers various studies of monuments including that of H. Regnault. Traces of torn sheets. Traces of missing folios between the cover and folio 1 and between folios 8 and 9. H: 0.148; L: 0.095.

louvre_cl020042121.title: Study of a cat, seated, front view, head turned left

louvre_cl020216555.title: Funerary monument project [Frémyot]
louvre_cl020216555.medium: brown ink with pen; brown wash; gray wash; graphite
louvre_cl020216555.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album of marbled paper, with white spine, with a blue leather label bearing in gold letters: 'J. Dubois/ Collection of original drawings'. It comprises 2 engravings and 168 drawings, most of which are pen drawings washed with bistre or India ink. The first 135 are by Jean Dubois; the others by Guillaume.

louvre_cl020557767.title: The Pleasures of Summer
louvre_cl020557767.medium: Etching and burin. Signed 'Painted by F. Boucher.' below the square line, on the left; 'Engraved by J. Daullé Engraver to the King and of the Imperial Academy of Augsburg', on the right. On both sides, coats of arms, the title and below: 'To Madame de Pompadour Lady of the Queen's Palace By her very humble and very obedient servant J. Daullé, The Original Painting belongs to Madame de Pompadour, in Paris at the Author's rue du Platre St. Jacques adjacent to the College of Cornouaille. A.P.D.R..' Watermark: eagle.

louvre_cl020562629.title: Landscape sketch with a stranded boat at the water's edge and row of posts (the drawing continues on folio 42 verso). Small sketch of figures
louvre_cl020562629.description: Description of the album: Sketch album with black oilcloth cover, comprising 56 sheets of grid paper, reddish edge. Circa 1901-1906. Missing sheet between folios 15 and 16. Notebook dimensions: cover: H. 00,150; W. 00,090; sheet: H.00,140; W. 00,090. Inside the album, a folded and annotated sheet: The weary peasant sees emerging from the earth the fruits (the fruit) of his thoughts.

louvre_cl020138027.title: Four Figure Studies
louvre_cl020138027.description: Description of the album: Notebook covered with gray and black marbled paper containing 72 numbered folios (including one bis, the 69th) featuring scenes after the antique, vases, columns, and studies after the model. Folio 1 partially cut out. Trace of missing folio between folios 25 and 26. H. 10.8. L. 17.9.

louvre_cl020030996.title: Young nude girl, standing, three-quarters to the right
louvre_cl020030996.medium: charcoal (black); beige paper; white highlights; sanguine; pricked for transfer

louvre_cl020133837.title: Two geographical maps
louvre_cl020133837.medium: black ink with pen; brown ink with pen; graphite
louvre_cl020133837.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious notebook bound in gray cloth, presenting various studies, often added, and handwritten notes. Traces of missing folios between folios 17 and 18, 30 and 31, 36 and 37, 39 and 40, and after folio 46. An 'S' is drawn on the upper cover. H. 0.104; L. 0.170.

louvre_cl020206598.title: Two Helmeted Men, Seen Half-Length
louvre_cl020206598.medium: Red chalk, with white chalk highlights, on beige paper. Irregular borders.

louvre_cl020538924.title: A Flute Player and a Drum Player
louvre_cl020538924.medium: Engraving. The monogram MT and the date 1543 are engraved at the top left. Numbering engraved at the top right: 1.

louvre_cl020114181.title: Two compositional studies in a spandrel
louvre_cl020114181.description: Description of the album: Cardboard sketchbook, covered with marbled paper with green and pink spots, partially dismembered. It offers compositional studies, from nature, landscapes, portraits. Folios 9 to 12 and 14 and 15 are blank. H. 0.195; W. 0.133.

louvre_cl020502089.title: Study sheet: two legs and several hands holding objects
louvre_cl020502089.medium: Black chalk, with white highlights, on gray-beige paper. Belonged to the 'Albums called Maratta' from the Lambert Krahe collection.

louvre_cl020040406.title: Landscape
louvre_cl020040406.description: Description of the album: Sketchbook with brown heavy canvas cover, containing 30 blue-colored sheets. Pencil holder on the front cover. Sketches of horse heads and landscapes. Folios 6 to 10, 12 to 22, 25, 26, 29 are blank. H. 0.134; W. 0.175.

louvre_cl020229592.title: Abla in front of the tent where the men are gathered
louvre_cl020229592.medium: black crayon; gray wash; white highlights; bristol paper

louvre_cl020208194.title: Five Armed Men Near a Burning House

louvre_cl020219296.title: Studies of costumes, hairstyles and a saddler's house
louvre_cl020219296.description: Description of the album: Album bound in beige cloth, with pencil holder in beige cloth, glued along the right edge of the front cover, composed of 91 sheets, numbered in pencil, top right, 1 to 2 bis, 3 to 90, offering drawings or sketch pages, mainly concerning a trip to Brittany. Label glued on the front endpaper: Boulevard Montmartre, 8 / DEFORGE ET CARPENTIER: Specialty for Painting / and Drawing. H: 0.187; L: 0.150

louvre_cl020562052.title: Masquerades and Court Ballets, 1572 - 1671
louvre_cl020562052.medium: Drawing mounted on a sheet with dimensions: H. 00.222; W. 00.150
louvre_cl020562052.description: Album description: Red morocco leather binding, cold-stamped fillets and frames, decorated corners and spine, Trautz-Bauzonnet gilding.

louvre_cl020619364.title: Rocaille Cartouche
louvre_cl020619364.description: Album description: Collection of 50 plates, without title page. Brown cardboard cover, traces of red wax on the front endpaper and on the back endpaper.

louvre_cl020577386.title: Lady of Quality in Summer Dress
louvre_cl020577386.medium: Etching enhanced with burin. At bottom right of the square line: 'N. Arnoult fec. 1687'
louvre_cl020577386.description: Album description: H: 00.352 m L: 00.278 m D: 00.048 m Open book: 00.548 m

louvre_cl020113538.title: The Multiplication of the Loaves and Fishes
louvre_cl020113538.medium: Signed in pen and brown ink, lower left: 'Dom° Tiepolo f'. Annotation in pen and brown ink on verso.
louvre_cl020113538.description: Album description: Green leather binding. On the cover, inscription in gold letters: 'DOMENICO / TIEPOLO / 1760'.

louvre_cl020578568.title: Louis Romainville
louvre_cl020578568.medium: Etching and burin. Below the square line on the left is engraved: 'P. Lacour Pinx.', on the right: 'Emmanuel Del. et Sculp. en 1785'. Below the verses: 'A Society of Citizens had this painting placed in the Foyer of the Theater Hall in Memory of this Actor in Bordeaux in 1784'.
louvre_cl020578568.description: Album description: Collection of theater scenes composed of 23 folios containing 14 engravings mounted on tabs and 7 glued to the recto. They were printed or created by I. Honervogt, Rousselet, Emmanuel, Thourneysen, Mathey. Bound in vellum.

louvre_cl020576686.title: Joseph lowered by his brothers into a cistern
louvre_cl020576686.medium: Engraving. Signed with monogram GP at upper right and dated '1546'
louvre_cl020576686.description: Album description: In-folio collection of the engraved work of Georg Pencz composed of 73 folios with 62 prints. Brown morocco leather binding. H.: 0.302 m L.: 0.240 m D: 0.040 m Open book: 0.500 m

louvre_cl020608380.title: The Good Ink
louvre_cl020608380.description: Album description: Collection containing the 'Studies taken from the common people or the Cries of Paris', five series, after drawings by Edmé Bouchardon, engraved by the Count of Caylus and Etienne Fessard, in Paris, 1737-1746. H: 0.328 m L: 0.252 m D: 0.027 m Open book: 0.493 m

louvre_cl020021604.title: Landscape
louvre_cl020021604.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album in brown canvas paper, with three-part pencil holder. Traces of torn folios between folios 19 and 20, 29 and 30. H. 0.210; W. 0.148.

louvre_cl020514651.title: Study of a Standing Woman
louvre_cl020514651.medium: Graphite on blue-gray vellum paper. Folio number at top right: 13
louvre_cl020514651.description: Description of the album: Album with brown leather cover and spine with metal clasp, pencil case and wooden pencil with ivory pommel, gilt edge, artist's monogram W.B. on the upper cover. 51 folios of blue-gray vellum paper. Sketches attached to the verso of folio 16 and the verso of folio 40. Studio stamp on the endpaper and on folio 1. Bellows on the reverse of the front cover containing: a visiting card of the painter, a visiting card of Baron Léon de Vrière with a study on the verso (RF 52744), a fragment of envelope with notes on collections of old engravings; a fragment of sheet with a list of colors; a pencil sketch (RF 52745). Cover: H: 00,080; L: 00,150; P: 00,015. The sketches in the notebook are quick notations on na

louvre_cl020227425.title: Half-length warrior, turned to the right, holding the shaft of a spear
louvre_cl020227425.description: Album description: This drawing is part of an album bound in red leather, with gold motifs bearing on the back in gold letters: 'DESCINS PREM. VAULUM'. H. 0.517; L. 0.347

louvre_cl020562684.title: Road at the entrance of a village with a church in the background. The tops of the trees on the right continue onto folio 22 verso
louvre_cl020562684.medium: graphite pencil on paper. Drawn lengthwise
louvre_cl020562684.description: Description of the album: Sketch album with cardboard cover covered in light blue paper, black cloth spine, cut corners, comprising 73 sheets. On the front endpaper, stamps THE PARIS AMERICAN ART Co/ 2 Rue Bonaparte / 125 bd du Montparnasse / PARIS / HAUSSE - 10%. Circa 1892-1901. Traces of missing folios between folios 48 and 49, 49 and 50, 60 and 61, 71 and 72. Dimensions of the notebook: H. 00,160; L. 00,100

louvre_cl020205084.title: Head of a Young Girl, with Eyes Raised
louvre_cl020205084.medium: Belonged to the 'Albums known as Maratta' from the Lambert Krahe collection

louvre_cl020020615.title: Handwritten Notes
louvre_cl020020615.description: Description of the album: Album bound in brown sheepskin containing drawings, watercolors and pencil notes, album relating to a painter's trip to Dieppe, Brighton and London. Folios 2, 5, 7, 13 to 17, 22 to 44, are blank. Traces of torn

louvre_cl020016246.title: Three Figure Studies
louvre_cl020016246.medium: Notebook taken in the other direction
louvre_cl020016246.description: Description of the album: Gray cardboard album with gray cloth spine comprising 88 sheets, of which folios 2bis, 8, 35 to 66, and 84 to 86 are blank. Traces of torn folios between folios 28 and 29, 32 and 33, 64 and 65, 67 and 68, 68 and 69. Folio 67 taken upside down.

louvre_cl020596468.title: Jason
louvre_cl020596468.medium: Etching. Typeset lettering below the composition.
louvre_cl020596468.description: Description of the album: [Frontispiece:] Historical games of the kings of France, renowned Queens, geography and metamorphosis / By the late Mr J. DESMARETS Counselor/ Secretary & Controller General of / the Extraordinary of Wars / And engraved by DO LA BELLA. These same games are accommodated as easy cards / to play, & will be sold separately .....IN PARIS, / At NICOLAS LE CLERC's, on the Quay / des Augustins, at the descent of Pont S. Michel, near the hotel de Luynes, at the image S.Lambert / AND / At FLORENT LE COMTE's, rue S. Jacques, near / the Fountain S. Benoist, at the Royal Cipher / M.DC. XCVIII. / With the King's Privilege'. Notice to the reader. This volume contains four games invented in 1644 by Jean Desmarets de Saint-Sorlin to serve in the education of Louis XIV.

louvre_cl020520467.title: Entry of the King and Queen into Paris - August 16, 1660

louvre_cl020616251.title: The Bat, the Bush and the Duck
louvre_cl020616251.description: Album description: In Paris. At Desaint & Saillant, rue Saint Jean de Beauvais. At Durand, rue du foin, entering by rue S. Jacques. M.DCC.LIX. From the printing house of Charles-Antoine Jombert. H: 00.429 m L: 00.290 m D: 00.037 m Open book: 00.590 m

louvre_cl020109265.title: Saint John the Baptist Baptizing the Pharisees
louvre_cl020109265.medium: brown wash; sanguine wash; black wash; white highlights; green wash; yellow highlights; pen

louvre_cl020039525.title: Portrait of Doctor Horace de Montègre, bust

louvre_cl020043386.title: Nude Woman, Crouching, in Profile
louvre_cl020043386.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, cardboard bound, covered with marbled paper, with ivory leather spine. Contains drawings RF 34601 to RF 34675. H. 0.540; W. 0.385.

louvre_cl020226130.title: Studies taken at Santa Maria del Popolo, Rome
louvre_cl020226130.medium: Annotated at top left in graphite: 'ROME / CHAPEL OF S.M. DU PEUPLE / BRAMANTE'. Signed at bottom right in pen and red ink with interlaced initials: 'PB'.
louvre_cl020226130.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, bound in marbled paper with brown leather spine. Gold lettering inscription: 'P. BARBOT. VOYAGE EN ITALIE. 1820-1822. P. BARBOT BENAIS THIERRY OEUVRES DE BRAMANTE'. On the

louvre_cl020030679.title: Gentleman seated at a table, handing a paper to a man
louvre_cl020030679.medium: gray wash; black chalk; white highlights; lead point

louvre_cl020530612.title: Medal of Francis I, King of France

louvre_cl020504913.title: Four studies of theater costumes: the singer or actor Lattanzio, 'Chiarichione' at Teatro Valle
louvre_cl020504913.medium: Signed at bottom right: 'Vincent 1772'. Drawing examination in transmitted light conducted on 10/12/2013 by Ariane de La Chapelle: absence of trace alteration. The presence of metallogallic ink is confirmed with certainty only on the manuscript inscriptions.

louvre_cl020023989.title: Manuscript Notes
louvre_cl020023989.description: Album description: Small sketchbook with flat covers and back in beige-gray canvas, with stretched elastic closure band and pencil holder, offering numerous studies of landscapes and local architectural elements. Gusset inside the back cover. Numbered sheets from 1 to 74. Folios 25 and 57 to 59 are blank. Folios 43 and 44 are torn. Sheet attached to the verso of the final endpaper with the inscription: "20/6 Moreau Nélaton". H. 0.152; W. 0.096.

louvre_cl020227090.title: Portrait of a seated man; portrait of a woman
louvre_cl020227090.description: Description of the album: Oblong notebook with marbled paper cover, Bradel type, composed of 29 sheets of very light blue-gray paper. The drawings RF 27902 to RF 27931 are attached. On a loose sheet, inscription in pen and brown ink by M. Louis Démonts: 'Deposit made by Mrs. Th. Reinach in 1910', and another inscription in graphite: 'See M. Carle Dreyfus regarding the origin of this notebook'. Traces of missing folios between folios 4 and 5 and 13 and 14. Folios 27 to 29 blank. Numerous inscriptions (poem, supply purchases). H: 0.155; W: 0.210.

louvre_cl020014611.title: Marco, the Saint Bernard dog of the artist

louvre_cl020229183.title: Young Woman Seated in an Interior Near a Reading Stand

louvre_cl020034813.title: Indeterminate Sketch
louvre_cl020034813.description: Description of the album: Sketch album, bound in parchment, containing 94 sheets and two guard pages. Preserved in a gray cardboard case with red leather spine. On the spine, inscription: 'L. Morel d'Arleux, voyage en Italie. Carnet de croquis'.

louvre_cl020219186.title: Spinning Wheel
louvre_cl020219186.description: Description of the album: Album bound in beige cloth, with pencil holder in beige cloth, glued along the right edge of the front cover, composed of 91 sheets, numbered in pencil, top right, 1 to 2 bis, 3 to 90, offering drawings or sketch pages, mainly concerning a trip to Brittany. Label glued on the front endpaper: Boulevard Montmartre, 8 / DEFORGE ET CARPENTIER: Specialty for Painting / and Drawing. H: 0.187; L: 0.150

louvre_cl020230080.title: Boats in the Port of Ajaccio
louvre_cl020230080.medium: Annotated at bottom right in graphite pencil: 'Ajaccio 25 (or 35) May (?).

louvre_cl020231546.title: Young Boy Holding a Pitcher with a Dog Nestled Against Him
louvre_cl020231546.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album, bound, covered with brown laid paper, and with a tawny morocco spine, bearing two titles: "ENGLISH SCHOOL" and "PLATES 1". It is composed of 81 drawings (RF 41658 to RF 41730) and one engraving (RF 42718.1) and forms volume 1 of 3. It also includes 2 printed pages. The drawings mainly represent copies. A work, preserved in a box placed after the three Normand albums, bearing the same binding (but missing the spine), describes compositions by English painters.

louvre_cl020554206.title: Costumes of Festivals, Masquerades, Theaters, etc., of Louis XIV
louvre_cl020554206.description: A drawing documenting the elaborate costumes used in festivals, masquerades, and theatrical performances during the reign of Louis XIV, executed in black ink with pen and gray wash technique.

louvre_cl020590509.title: The Virgin and Child with Saint Elizabeth and Saint Anne
louvre_cl020590509.description: Description of the album: Drawing notebook with cardboard cover covered in midnight blue paper, very deteriorated. The spine of the binding is destroyed. 71 folios; the versos of folios 10, 12, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 39, 51, 53, 54, 68, and the recto of folio 69 are blank; folio missing between folios 33 and 34. Handwritten annotation in graphite pencil, folio 1, upper right corner: "Notebook of the wife of / Rude, Mrs. Frémiet, offered to the department by / Madame Faber de Raulin / December 20, 1948 / [initials: "MA"? = Marcel Aubert?]". Label on the front endpaper: P.J. MEYVAERT and Widow F. PAUWELS, rue de la Madeleine in Brussels. Handwritten annotations in pencil on the back endpaper. H.: 00.25; W.: 00.198; Thickness: 00.03.

louvre_cl020137290.title: Armchair
louvre_cl020137290.description: Description of the album: Sketch notebook with beige canvas cover, comprising 49 folios. On the lower cover is pasted an entrance ticket to the Universal Exhibition of 1889. 2 cm gap along the entire height of folio 1. H.8.5 W.13.5 cm.

louvre_cl020005722.title: Minerva Watching Over Ulysses' Toilette
louvre_cl020005722.medium: Black stone, pen and brown ink. Annotation in pen and brown ink, bottom right: 'Nicolo' dell'Abb.'. Fully mounted.

louvre_cl020214063.title: Two parrot heads, one frontal, the other in profile
louvre_cl020214063.medium: Black chalk, pen and black ink, on yellowed and stained beige paper. Annotated on verso, upper left in pen and brown ink: '8'; center in black chalk: '143'.
louvre_cl020214063.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, assembled by Morel d'Arleux, grouping 117 drawings, bound in blue calfskin with gold lace, spine with three red leather labels, interior covers in blue and pink marbled paper. Handwritten list on a loose sheet not bearing the Louvre mark and placed after the front endpaper, presenting in three columns the numbers given by Morel d'Arleux, the reference to the album folio and the name 'Le Brun (Charles)'. On the first page, in pen and black ink: 'French School Volume 18-H-117 Drawings'. Drawings relating to Physiognomy, subject of the 'Conference on Human Physiognomy and its relationships with that of animals' that Le Brun led on March 28, 1671 before Colbert, at the Academy.

louvre_cl020593037.title: Frontispiece
louvre_cl020593037.description: Album description: [Title:] Historiarum Veteris Testamenti icones ad vivum expressae. Lyon, Subscuto coloniensis, published by Ioannem & Franciscum Frellonios, brothers, 1543. - Reader's notice. Numbered

louvre_cl020000795.title: Man Practicing with Saber and Sword
louvre_cl020000795.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with marbled brown paper. It bears on the cover a label with the following mention: 'Stefano della Bella', and on the spine, another, annotated: 'vol. I'. This volume is paginated from I to 126, each of the sixty-three sheets that compose it bearing a number on the front and back. The drawings are glued, either in full, or by a tab on the sheet of the album. H: 0.460; L: 0.365.

louvre_cl020574293.title: Homage to Cardinal Scipione Borghese called the Quos Ego

louvre_cl020019284.title: Three Women Personifying Poetry, Painting and Music
louvre_cl020019284.medium: Miniature on ivory on round box

louvre_cl020221357.title: Woman, bust-length, face resting on right hand
louvre_cl020221357.description: Description of the album: Factitious album covered in beige cloth, with a red leather rectangle on the front cover indicating 'Théodore Chasseriau Album 7'. H: 0.370; W: 0.330.

louvre_cl020200557.title: Woman in the presence of two hunchbacks, and diners at table, beside
louvre_cl020200557.medium: brown ink with pen; wash (brown); black chalk

louvre_cl020561578.title: Three Projects for Sculptural Groups Including an Equestrian One
louvre_cl020561578.medium: pen and brown ink on paper
louvre_cl020561578.description: Description of the album: Sketch album with cloth cover mounted on cardboard with pencil case, comprising 67 sheets. Traces of missing folio between numbers 5 and 6. Label affixed to the front

louvre_cl020016783.title: Manuscript notes: Carle Vernet 1789
louvre_cl020016783.description: Album description: Small notebook bound in red leather. Ribbed spine with gilding. Front cover featuring an oval reserve outlined with golden dots. Box: ANONYMOUS French 18th century. H. 0.140 L. 0.850

louvre_cl020015846.title: View of the Church of the Ascension in Jerusalem during stormy weather
louvre_cl020015846.medium: Pen and brown ink, brown wash, gray wash, graphite. At right in pen: 'Church of the Ascension Jerusalem'.
louvre_cl020015846.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, composed in the 19th century of drawings by several artists, by Madame Henri Armand Rolle, née Marguerite Thérèse Manceaux.

louvre_cl020625079.title: Landscape Study
louvre_cl020625079.medium: Pen and gray ink, pencil, gray wash
louvre_cl020625079.description: Description of the album: Album of drawings bound in vellum. 102 folios numbered with pen in the upper right, of which 17 are blank (folios 47, 48, 49, 50, 51, 52, 53, 75, 76, 77, 78, 79, 80, 81, 82, 85, 86). Following an error in the counting of inventory numbers, inventory numbers 491 DR, 492 DR and 493 DR are not assigned.

louvre_cl020018216.title: Aesculapius standing, frontal view
louvre_cl020018216.medium: Pen and brown ink, brown wash, over black chalk sketch. Numbering at upper right in pen and brown ink: '61' and annotation at lower left: 'da Listeso' and inscription at right: 'Palazzo Ruspoli'.
louvre_cl020018216.description: Description of the album: Album of 324 sheets bound in brown leather binding, with a label attached to the upper cover with the number: 'N° 80' inscribed in pen and brown ink. All versos of the album bear the offset from the recto of the following folio. The spine has lost most of the decorated leather with gilded tooling that covered it as well as the six raised bands. At the beginning of the album, four manuscript sheets in pen and brown ink, without inventory number, sewn together, with title: 'Table of drawings by J. Parrossel'. A loose sheet with handwritten annotation in pen and black ink, without inventory number, title page of an English sale catalogue from 1904.

louvre_cl020624866.title: Landscape Sketch
louvre_cl020624866.description: Description of the album: Album of drawings bound in vellum. 56 folios numbered with pen in the upper right, no blank folios. H: 19.1 cm; W: 8.5 cm

louvre_cl020131741.title: Group of sailboats going to the right
louvre_cl020131741.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, with green leather cover with gold fillets, composed of 41 numbered folios. H. 0.700; L. 0.560.

louvre_cl020217462.title: Landscape with the Castle of Loches
louvre_cl020217462.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, bound in gray marbled paper, brown leather spine. Landscapes, copies after masters and after antique works. Some drawings are drawn on tracing paper. H. 0.277; W. 0.385.

louvre_cl020103450.title: Nude man, kneeling, turned to the right, extending his arms to the left
louvre_cl020103450.medium: Belonged to the 'Albums known as Maratta' from the Lambert Krahe collection

louvre_cl020580434.title: Anecdote of the Day
louvre_cl020580434.description: Album description: Height: 0.628 m, Width: 0.489 m, Depth: 0.038 m, Open book: 0.977 m

louvre_cl020579609.title: Bertrand Barère de Vieuzac
louvre_cl020579609.description: Album description: Height: 0.628 m, Width: 0.489 m, Depth: 0.038 m, Open book: 0.977 m

louvre_cl020220480.title: Caricature portrait, soldier, standing, in right profile: Charles Hutin?

louvre_cl020208425.title: Bust of an old woman, front view, head raised
louvre_cl020208425.medium: gray paper; black chalk; white highlights; red chalk

louvre_cl020210529.title: Druid Preaching on the Sixth Day of the Moon

louvre_cl020227895.title: A Battle from the Time of Louis XIV
louvre_cl020227895.description: Description of the album: Factitious album bound in gray-beige cloth, in oblong format, bearing on the upper cover a label with almost completely erased annotations. It groups together drawings RF 28589 to RF 28656 glued on 50 sheets and on the lower endpaper. Some drawings are illustrations for Alexandre Dumas' The Three Musketeers.

louvre_cl020209222.title: The Great Illumination of the Park of Versailles during the Wedding Festivities of the Dauphin Louis XVI and Archduchess of Austria Marie-Antoinette, May 19, 1770

louvre_cl020208737.title: The Fury of Duels Stopped
louvre_cl020208737.medium: gray-beige paper; black stone; white highlights

louvre_cl020021180.title: Christ between two angels, after a mosaic from the apse of Saint-Vital

louvre_cl020556875.title: Costumes of Festivities, Masquerades, Theaters, etc., of Louis XIV
louvre_cl020556875.description: A drawing documenting the elaborate costumes used in the festivities, masquerades, and theatrical performances during the reign of Louis XIV, executed in black pen and ink with gray wash technique.

louvre_cl020608752.title: View of Saint John of the Florentines
louvre_cl020608752.description: Album description: H: 00.219 m L: 00.338 m D: 00.045 m Open book: 00.713 m

louvre_cl020549137.title: The Amiable Housekeeper
louvre_cl020549137.medium: Etching and engraving. At bottom left of the square line: 'F. Boucher Pinx.', at right: 'P. Duverbret Sculp.'. Below the title 'L'AIMABLE MENAGERE', appear six doubled verses. Watermark: T (well) Dupuy Fin Auvergne 1742.

louvre_cl020201407.title: Christ Humbly Listening to the Virgin's Reproaches

louvre_cl020031316.title: Strollers in a tree-lined avenue bordered by fences
louvre_cl020031316.medium: Numbered in pencil at bottom right: 16. The upper part of the page has been largely truncated. In the lower part, in black chalk with gray wash highlights, avenue of trees and figures.
louvre_cl020031316.description: Description of the album: Title: Sketchbook. Dimensions: H. 18.7 cm; W. 12.8 cm; thickness 2 cm. Octavo format. Support: laid paper of variable weight, between 90 and 120 g/m² depending on the sheets. Full marbled calf binding from the 18th century with frame

louvre_cl020103054.title: The Creation of Eve, the Original Sin and the Creation of Adam
louvre_cl020103054.medium: Belonged to the 'Albums known as Maratta' from the Lambert Krahe collection

louvre_cl020134717.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious album, bound in marbled paper with black leather spine. Contains a landscape study, character sketches, and numerous handwritten notes. Gaps on folios 31 and 34. Contains 42 folios. H. 0.115; L. 0.157.

louvre_cl020231710.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album, bound, covered with brown laid paper, and with a tawny morocco spine, bearing two titles: "ENGLISH SCHOOL" and "PLATES 3". It is composed of 82 sheets and

louvre_cl020554400.title: Costumes of Festivals, Masquerades, Theaters, etc., of Louis XIV
louvre_cl020554400.description: A drawing documenting the elaborate costumes used in festivals, masquerades, and theatrical performances during the reign of Louis XIV, executed in black ink with pen and gray wash technique.

louvre_cl020221470.title: Portrait of a woman, half-length view, with flowers in her hair
louvre_cl020221470.medium: Lead pencil on irregularly cut gray-beige paper. Note at the bottom of the mounting: 'Study for the portrait of Mademoiselle Cabarrus-madame Saint-Amand-Martignon, which is currently at the Museum of Quimper, Finistère'. Restored in 2002.
louvre_cl020221470.description: A preparatory drawing depicting a woman in half-length view, adorned with flowers in her hair, executed in lead pencil on gray-beige paper.

louvre_cl020516342.title: The Sun
louvre_cl020516342.medium: Print composed of a central woodblock and three others for the frame (columns on left and right, base with cartouche for the planet's name). Above the central woodblock, two stanzas of two typographed verses: 'The Sun shines over all planets / Be truly friendly, my children // In 365 days quickly / I run through the firmament.' Plate numbering at top left: 4. Above the two verses, the excudit: 'Albrecht Glockendon Illuminist. / First of August. 1531.'
louvre_cl020516342.description: Print composed of a central woodblock and three others for the frame (columns on left and right, base with cartouche for the planet's name). Above the central woodblock, two stanzas of two typographed verses: 'The Sun shines over all planets / Be truly friendly, my children // In 365 days quickly / I run through the firmament.' Plate numbering at top left: 4. Above the two verses, the excudit: 'Albrecht Glockendon Illuminist. / First of August. 1531.'

louvre_cl020209689.title: Young girl protecting her face from the sun's heat with her...
louvre_cl020209689.medium: Pen and brown ink, gray wash, white highlights. Framing lines in pen and brown ink. Mounted at the two left corners on the same sheet as the following drawings (Inv. 32 366 bis, ter and quater).
louvre_cl020209689.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, cardboard bound, covered with marbled paper in beige, black, yellow, with blue cloth spine. It consists of 18 sheets, including two guard pages at the beginning and end of the album and 14 sheets numbered on the verso in green pencil (from no. 12 to no. 23 and no. 31). 44 drawings are mounted, including 1 with a verso (Inv. 32358 to Inv. 32368 and Inv. 32376). These studies are by Bernard Picart and two are attributed to him (Inv. 32376 and Inv. 2376 bis). They present fashion figures and characters from the Commedia dell'arte.

louvre_cl020595037.title: Comedy Surrounded by Cupids, Three of Whom Play on a Stage
louvre_cl020595037.description: Album description: The engravings are numbered from 1015 to 1396 (382 engravings total, according to the inventory). Number 1081 is a drawing. Height: 0.622 m, Width: 0.454 m, Depth: 0.043 m, Open book: 0.935 m

louvre_cl020116646.title: Studies of women after the antique
louvre_cl020116646.description: Album description: Factitious album, cardboard covered with blue marbled paper, with black leather spine and corners. Study sheets: figures, peasant scenes, armor studies. Contains drawings RF 8072 to RF 8168. Folios 55 to 63 are blank. H. 0.379; W. 0.281.

louvre_cl020121333.title: Half-naked man with raised arms, bound at the wrists

This album is housed in a cardboard case of marbled paper with a black leather spine bearing a red label reading: 'E. Delacroix. Saint Sylvester Album'. Blank folios: 4 to 9, 11 to 14, 16 to 19, 22 to 24, 26 to 27, 30, 34 to 38, 40, 42, 43, 46, 49, 53 to 56, 60 to 62, 64, 66, 68, 69, 75, 76, 78, 80 to 82, 84, 85, 87, 89 to 91, 93 to 96, 98, 101, 103, 106, 112, 114, 116, 117, 119, 120, 123 to 127, 130, 133, 136, 139 to 142, 144 to 146, 149, 151, 153 to 158, 158 to 160, 162 to 164, 166, 167, 169, 172 to 174, 178, 179, 181 to 183, 185, 186, 189, 191, 192, 194 to 203, 205. Trace of missing folio betwe

louvre_cl020231086.title: Venus and Adonis
louvre_cl020231086.description: Album description: This drawing is part of a sketch album with a leather cover and flap, bearing on the upper cover, in pen: 'n° 9' comprising 22 sheets. Traces of torn folios between folios 13 and 14, 16 and 17, and 18 and 19. Annotated in black crayon. Cover is notched.

louvre_cl020200984.title: Half-figure of draped woman, turned to the left

louvre_cl020128738.description: Description of the album: Composite album, with green leather spine and covers, with gold fillets, offering numerous studies of landscapes, figures, from nature. H. 0.700; W. 0.560.

louvre_cl020105678.title: Resurrection of Lazarus
louvre_cl020105678.medium: brown ink with pen; wash (brown); white highlights

louvre_cl020625841.title: View of Donchery, near Sedan
louvre_cl020625841.medium: Black stone and watercolor on watermarked laid paper. Pen and ink annotation: View of Doncheri near Sedan.
louvre_cl020625841.description: Description of the album: Notebook of 74 pages including 67 with drawings on watermarked laid paper, half-leather binding, cardboard covers, leather corners, leather spine with inscription 'various sketches'. The notebook is complete, without torn folios. The drawings, most of which are captioned, are in black stone and watercolor and the annotations in pen and ink. The notebook was used by the artist in 1809. Notebook dimensions: 20 x 16.2 cm

louvre_cl020579520.description: Album description: Height: 0.628 m, Width: 0.489 m, Depth: 0.038 m, Open book: 0.977 m

louvre_cl020008464.title: Latona complaining to Apollo and Diana about Niobe's contempt; Apollo

louvre_cl020220186.medium: Inscriptions, pen and brown ink, on the right: '8'; below: 'Well! Mr. lawyer, aren't you shooting this hare? - Not at all, I want to first inquire about his background in the area, I want to see his file before doing anything!'
louvre_cl020220186.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, with marbled paper covers and brown cloth spine, grouping drawings RF 23618 to RF 24028. Folio 77 is followed by folio 77 bis. H: 0.310; L: 0.445.

louvre_cl020579668.title: Fraternal Suppers in the Sections of Paris
louvre_cl020579668.description: Album description: H: 0.628 m W: 0.489 m D: 0.038 m Open book: 0.977 m

Trace of missing folio between folios 18 and 19. Folio 41 is torn. A label is glued on the front paste-down: 'A la palette de Rubens - Leroy - rue de Sèvres, N° 6'. H: 120; W: 0.200.

louvre_cl020017711.description: Description of the album: Album with marbled cardboard cover in green and blue, green cloth spine with gold inscription: A C G Lemonnier / Sketches.

louvre_cl020026604.title: Landscape Sketch
louvre_cl020026604.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with speckled gray-green, purple and red paper. Purple cloth spine. In red pencil on the front cover: 1864. Composed of 46 numbered sheets, offering numerous landscapes and handwritten notes. Many folios missing between fol. 45 and fol. 46. H. 0.200; L. 0.155.

louvre_cl020008820.title: A Bishop Standing, Turned to the Right

louvre_cl020227342.title: Tree-lined avenue in perspective, a horse in the foreground
louvre_cl020227342.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, bound in red morocco leather, containing drawings and letters from Chassériau to his father. Folios 1, 2, 6, 87 and 89 are blank. Includes a

louvre_cl020562558.title: View of a street in Katwijk leading to the sea
louvre_cl020562558.medium: Pen and brown and black ink, pencil, gray wash
louvre_cl020562558.description: Description of the album: This sketchbook is bound in gilded parchment and comprises 69 folios numbered in pen and brown ink in the upper right corner of the right page. Both endpapers are also drawn upon. The inventory number is inscribed on folio 5. The inventory numbers 499 DR, 500 DR, 503 DR, 504 DR and 520 DR have been assigned to blank pages (the album does not contain enough drawings to assign these numbers). It contains studies of trees, dunes, animals, as well as numerous views of the countryside around Leiden, drawn during the artist's walks along the Haarlemmertrekvaart (Haarlem to Leiden canal) or towards the North Sea, along the Old Rhine, passing through Rijnsburg, to the fishing villages of Ka

louvre_cl020012784.title: A lord standing before a table, holding a command staff
louvre_cl020012784.medium: brown ink with pen; black chalk; red chalk; watercolor; graphite
louvre_cl020012784.description: Description of the album: Factitious album, with brown leather spine and covers, and gold fillets, offering portrait studies of court figures. Folios 8 to 13, 23, 27 and 79 to 94 blank. Folio 27 torn out. H. 0.456; W. 0.325.

louvre_cl020120603.title: Bent Man Tying a Sack, and a Woman in a Tree-Lined Field

louvre_cl020225374.title: Rome: Villa Borghese
louvre_cl020225374.medium: Graphite pencil. Annotation in graphite pencil: 'ROMA - villa Borghese. Date: 'Rome, July 16, 1821'.
louvre_cl020225374.description: Description of the album: Volume forming a factitious collection, half-bound in brown sheepskin, marbled covers containing drawings RF 26623 to RF 26820. On the first page, pen inscription: P. Barbot, Architectural voyage to Italy 1820-1822. Journey from Paris to Rome from August 30 to October 3, 1820. Some drawings from this part of the journey are interspersed with those from the voyage to Tuscany (1821) as relating to it. Rome from October 3, 1820 to May 2, 1821, from June 19 to September 24, 1821, from November 10, 1821 to April 30, 1822 (surroundings of Rome). Return to France. From Rome to Paris, from May 1 to July 24, 1822.

louvre_cl020117873.title: Landscape Sketch
louvre_cl020117873.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with black paper with raised patterns, composed of 58 foliated sheets. Between folios 57 and 58, there is the trace of a torn folio, and folio 50 is blank. The front cover bears the stamp of the Corot sale.

louvre_cl020518398.title: Heads of Old Men and Character Types

louvre_cl020582294.title: He only has to come / he will be treated in this manner / 1 Constitution, castigating the counter-revolutionary with the right of / Man and the decree [...]

louvre_cl020014836.medium: Pen and brown ink, lead pencil. Annotation below the drawing, in pen and brown ink: '(model...) / fireplace hearth for the Paris workshop'. Engraved monogram of the artist, cut out and pasted between the drawing and its caption. The drawings RF 2591 BIS, RF 2591 TER, RF 2591 QUATER are pasted on this drawing.
louvre_cl020014836.description: Description of the album: Album covered in fawn leather, marked with the artist's monogram in gold letters, containing 43 folios numbered in pencil at the top right from 1 to 43. Interior covers lined with mauve fabric. End

louvre_cl020036960.description: Description of the album: Sketch album with parchment cover and flap, comprising 59 sheets. Copies of paintings and sculptures. Many folios missing before folio 1, between folios 8 and 9, 16 and 17, 38 and 39 (2), 51 and 52 (numerous). Folios 51 and 58 blank. H. 0.282; W. 0.203.

louvre_cl020508959.title: Antique Family Scene

louvre_cl020132311.title: Sailor, standing, three-quarters to the left

louvre_cl020117827.title: Bust of a man, and standing woman
louvre_cl020117827.description: Description of the album: Cardboard sketchbook, covered with green paper, with black leather spine. The top cover is very damaged. Torn folio: 24, 35. H. 0.117; L. 0.170.

louvre_cl020140105.title: Handwritten Address
louvre_cl020140105.description: Description of the album: Cardboard notebook of violet papier-mâché, with violet cloth spine, composed of 69 sheets and 8 attached drawings. The cover is no longer attached to the sheets today. It offers studies of statues, of medallions. Trace of missing folio between folios 21 and 22. H: 0.145; L: 0.098.

louvre_cl020529115.title: Eleven sketches by Adrien Féart after Poussin

louvre_cl020554144.title: Hairstyle Model
louvre_cl020554144.description: A drawing study focusing on a hairstyle arrangement, executed in black ink with pen and gray wash technique.

louvre_cl020140567.title: A woman, standing, half nude
louvre_cl020140567.description: Description of the album: Notebook covered in black cloth, composed of 75 sheets. Sheet 75 is detached. H: 0.152; W: 0.092. Gilded edges.

louvre_cl020118545.title: View of Venice
louvre_cl020118545.description: Description of the album: Sketch notebook with cardboard cover, covered with dark green paper, with dark green leather spine. Folio 12 is blank. Trace of missing folio between folio 30 and folio 31. Folio 47 torn. H. 0.116; W. 0.155.

louvre_cl020135609.title: Draped Figure with a Child
louvre_cl020135609.description: Description of the album: Sketchbook bound in beige cloth, offering numerous studies of sculpted groups. Trace of 2 missing folios between folios 74 and 75. Contains 76 folios including folios 47bis and 48bis. H. 0.155; W. 0.100.

louvre_cl020218696.title: Woman, turned to the right, lifting the bottom of her dress
louvre_cl020218696.medium: Signed, bottom right, in black crayon: 'F'
louvre_cl020218696.description: Description of the album: Sketch album, cardboard, covered with green cloth, with black leather spine and corners, offering notably character studies. Label glued on the front endpaper "24 Soho square". Folios 111, 134, 138 to 149, 152 to 154, 158, 172, 175 to 184 and 186 to 194 blank. H. 0.378; W. 0.245.

louvre_cl020135277.title: Sculpted Monument, with a Figure
louvre_cl020135277.description: Description of the album: Sketchbook bound in gray cloth, ink-stained on the lower and upper covers, comprising studies of sculpture, monuments, vases, as well as various handwritten notes. Contains 57 folios. H: 0.149; W: 0.095.

louvre_cl020128606.title: Portrait of Henri Harpignies
louvre_cl020128606.description: Description of the album: Factitious album with red morocco leather back and corners, comprising at the beginning two photographs of the painter and 29 drawings (RF 10837 to RF 10865). In this album is preserved number 64, second year of the magazine 'L'Actualité Française, Etrangère et Littéraire Illustrée...' (Sunday April 4, 1901).

louvre_cl020016035.title: Head of a man seen from the front; reworking of the chin; mouth

louvre_cl020619044.title: Round-shaped brooch

louvre_cl020506715.description: Description of the album: Notebook covered with black paper, with purple cloth spine. It is composed of 87 sheets and brings together projects for funerary monuments and statues. H: 0.142; W: 0.095.

louvre_cl020012313.title: Two Profiles of Bearded Men
louvre_cl020012313.medium: black stone; paper tinted with red chalk

louvre_cl020553829.description: Description of the album: Vellum notebook, composed of five bifolios bound by a small cord, preserved in a case bound in navy blue morocco leather. Closed collection: H. 196 mm, W. 140 mm

louvre_cl020210182.title: Architecture and ornament of a church vault
louvre_cl020210182.medium: brown ink with pen; wash (gray); gold highlights; watercolor

louvre_cl020584973.title: Order of the Slavonians
louvre_cl020584973.description: Description of the album: [Title:] 'Clergy of the entire Roman Church subject, Or, OF ALL PONTIFICAL ORDERS ABSOLUTELY OF BOTH SEXES, HABITS, WITH MOST ARTISTIC FIGURES, to which FRANCISCUS MODIUS's individual octostichs are added, now first EXPRESSED BY JUDOCUS AMMANNUS: NEVER before similarly published. WITH THE ADDITION OF A SINGULAR BOOKLET BY THE SAME FRANCISCUS Modius of Bruges, in which the origin, progress & reason for the vestments of each ecclesiastical order is briefly outlined from various historians'. Woodcut. [Place and date of edition:] Frankfurt at the expense of Sigismundus Feyrabendius. 1585.

louvre_cl020219422.title: Trees in a landscape; two seascapes
louvre_cl020219422.medium: The folio is detached from its binding.
louvre_cl020219422.description: Description of the album: Cardboard register in marbled paper with cloth spine; an oval label is glued to the front cover. It is composed of 24 sheets. H: 0.387; W: 0.250.

louvre_cl020217302.title: Studies of Peasants
louvre_cl020217302.description: Description of the album: Pocket notebook bound in vellum comprising 92 sheets. It contains two handwritten notes and 183 pen studies made in Provence. H: 0.165; W: 0.110.

louvre_cl020039359.title: Landscape
louvre_cl020039359.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious sketch album, bound in aubergine-colored leather with gilded spine fillets. The covers are decorated with embossed geometric motifs in different colors, the corners are adorned with gilded metallic elements. The upper cover is detached from the binding. H. 0.255; W. 0.350

louvre_cl020135116.title: Standing Man
louvre_cl020135116.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious cardboard album with red and black marbled paper, with brown paper spine, composed of 41 folios; it contains sketches after antique works, probably dating from Chapu's stay in Italy, between 1856 and 1860, and handwritten notes. H: 0.107; W: 0.158.

louvre_cl020208950.title: Standing Warrior, Seen to the Knees; Hand Studies
louvre_cl020208950.medium: brown paper; black chalk; white highlights; red chalk
