louvre_cl020119611.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with blue-green binding paper, composed of 47 numbered folios on lined paper, of which folios 40 to 46 are blank. Trace of missing folio between folios 1 and 2, 46 and 47, and eight missing folios between folio 47 and the back endpaper. The front cover bears the stamp of the Corot sale.

louvre_cl020115470.title: Handwritten Notes
louvre_cl020115470.description: Album description: Manuscript bound in brown suede leather, with a gouache watercolor on the front cover and an engraved wood block on the back cover, comprising 182 sheets on which 59 watercolors are pasted. Bears a title in black: "NOANOA / Journey to / Tahiti". Folio 52 is blank. Folio 51 is missing. Both the old and modern foliation is faulty.

louvre_cl020135339.title: Handwritten notes (addresses)

louvre_cl020135339.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious notebook of brown embossed paper with floral motifs, with a brown grained paper spine, containing studies after Antique works, sculptures including some from the Renaissance, and numerous handwritten annotations (mostly addresses). Contains 89 folios. H.16.5 W.11.2 cm.

louvre_cl020038082.title: Standing Figure on His Boat
louvre_cl020038082.description: Album description: Sketchbook with beige cloth cover, comprising 43 sheets, all of which bear the artist's stamp, except folio 12. It brings together studies of Venice and its surroundings. H: 0.097; L: 0.148.

louvre_cl020231716.title: Titania and Bottom

louvre_cl020231716.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album, bound, covered with brown laid paper, and with a tawny morocco spine, bearing two titles: "ENGLISH SCHOOL" and "PLATES 3". It is composed of 82 sheets and

louvre_cl020619874.title: Proper decoration for a large gallery and decoration for a library
louvre_cl020619874.description: Album description: [On the title page]: Approved on September 5, 1757 by Members of the Royal Academy of Architecture. Volume III. 3rd in 1760 and 4th in 1761. // In Paris At the author's Rue Saint Jacques at the Golden Chariot. Second tome of a series of six, published between 1757 and 1768.

louvre_cl020015156.title: Roman Battery Undermined, During the Siege of Rome
louvre_cl020015156.medium: brown ink with pen; beige paper; graphite

louvre_cl020218464.title: Two Studies of Flayed Arms
louvre_cl020218464.description: Description of the album: Factitious cardboard album, covered with marbled paper in violet, brown and bluish tones, with dark blue leather spine and corners, offering mainly anatomical studies. H. 0.512; L. 0.315.

louvre_cl020219470.title: Boatmen
louvre_cl020219470.description: Album description: Cardboard album, covered with blue-green marbled paper, with brown cloth spine, with broken cover. It comprises 10 numbered sheets. H. 0.202; W. 0.168.

louvre_cl020035376.title: Four Draped Figures, Prostrated Toward the Left

louvre_cl020212088.title: An Ecclesiastical Peer
louvre_cl020212088.medium: Pen, black ink, Chinese ink wash and white highlights on beige paper
louvre_cl020212088.description: Description of the album: Album of the coronation of Louis XV volume 2. Cardboard album with light brown leather spine and corners. Alternating drawings and engravings.

louvre_cl020120106.title: Landscape
louvre_cl020120106.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with dark green leather, comprising 89 folios numbered from 1 to 89, of which folios 2 and 72 to 75 are blank. Two folios missing between folios 25 and 26, four between folios 55 and 56, seven between folios 60 and 61, two between folios 67 and 68 and between folios 71 and 72, three between folios 75 and 76. H. 0.143; L. 0.101.

louvre_cl020123300.title: House with a fence on the left and surrounded by trees in the background

louvre_cl020109574.title: Studies of Pelicans
louvre_cl020109574.medium: chamois paper; pastel; black stone; white highlights

louvre_cl020143098.title: Plain Landscape
louvre_cl020143098.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with green paper with a spine of the same colored leather, bearing on the upper cover, in golden Gothic letters: 'Album' and a red wax seal. This collection consists of 51 sheets, of which folios 35 to 47 are blank, as well as folio 50. On the front cover, red wax seal. On the front endpaper, label with 'Rouen' (handwritten); packer's label 'Chenue'; label 'Alph: Giroux'. H. 0.126; L. 0.200.

louvre_cl020020870.title: Study for the portrait of Madame Blanchet de Rives (1884)

louvre_cl020228222.title: Peasant Woman Seated on the Ground, Three-Quarters to the Left
louvre_cl020228222.description: Description of the album: Album in brown sheepskin with relief leather ornaments and other metal ornaments, oblong format, composed of 40 sheets, not including the endpapers. On the front endpaper, pasted label: 'Album

louvre_cl020207525.title: The Crossing of the Rhine
louvre_cl020207525.medium: Black chalk, gray-blue wash. Red chalk framing line; squared for transfer in black chalk.

louvre_cl020024388.title: Soldier, from behind, behind a low wall, shouldering his rifle
louvre_cl020024388.description: Album description: Bound album, covered in gray cloth, with pencil holder and two cloth closure ribbons. It is composed of 33 numbered sheets, offering numerous studies from nature. H. 0.111 (with pencil holder); W. 0.154.

louvre_cl020517312.title: Figure of an Old Man with Short Beard

louvre_cl020596751.title: Medea
louvre_cl020596751.medium: Etching. Typeset lettering below the composition.
louvre_cl020596751.description: Description of the album: [Frontispiece:] Historical games of the kings of France, renowned Queens, geography and metamorphosis / By the late Mr J. DESMARETS Counselor/ Secretary & Controller General of / the Extraordinary of Wars / And engraved by DO LA BELLA. These same games are accommodated as easy cards / to play, & will be sold separately .....IN PARIS, / At NICOLAS LE CLERC's, on the Quay / des Augustins, at the descent of Pont S. Michel, near the hotel de Luynes, at the image S.Lambert / AND / At FLORENT LE COMTE's, rue S. Jacques, near / the Fountain S. Benoist, at the Royal Cipher / M.DC. XCVIII. / With the King's Privilege'. Notice to the reader. This volume contains four games invented in 1644 by Jean Desmarets de Saint-Sorlin to serve in the education of Louis XIV.

louvre_cl020106202.title: Preaching of Saint John the Baptist
louvre_cl020106202.medium: This drawing is on the same mounting as INV 21565. Belonged to the 'Albums called Maratta' from the Lambert Krahe collection.

louvre_cl020013534.title: Hand, Three Feet, and Parrot Head

louvre_cl020209136.title: Study of a Drapery and a Half-Figure
louvre_cl020209136.medium: blue paper; black chalk; white highlights; red chalk

louvre_cl020554446.title: Hairstyle Model
louvre_cl020554446.medium: black ink with pen; colored ink with pen
louvre_cl020554446.description: A detailed drawing executed in black and colored ink with pen, documenting a specific hairstyle design. This work serves as a model or reference for a particular coiffure arrangement, likely created for instructional or documentary purposes within the French artistic and fashion tradition.

louvre_cl020536354.title: Saint-Séverin Church; detail of the charnel houses

louvre_cl020535817.title: Frontispiece: France receives homage from the ten books of architecture by Vitruvius. The backgrounds with the facade of the Louvre under construction and the triumphal arch of the Saint-Antoine gate were engraved by S. Le Clerc

louvre_cl020010388.title: Bent foreleg of a horse, viewed at knee height
louvre_cl020010388.medium: Red chalk. Trace of horizontal fold in the middle. Fully mounted on a reinforcement sheet cut following graphite lines: H. 0.729; W. 0.529. Watermarks: heart topped with fleur-de-lis vimal + auvergne / bunch of grapes / 1742 (work sheet); double-headed eagle bearing a heart, topped with a crown + i puits dupuy fin / auvergne 1742 (reinforcement sheet).

louvre_cl020608507.title: Palace of Orléans
louvre_cl020608507.description: Album description: H: 00.219 m L: 00.338 m D: 00.045 m Open book: 00.713 m

louvre_cl020019219.title: View of part of the fortifications of Maastricht, after the bombardment

louvre_cl020105594.title: Three monks busy preparing food in a kitchen

louvre_cl020553857.title: Costumes for Festivals, Masquerades, Theaters, etc., of Louis XIV
louvre_cl020553857.medium: black ink with pen; wash; watercolor
louvre_cl020553857.description: A detailed drawing documenting the elaborate costumes used in festivals, masquerades, and theatrical performances during the reign of Louis XIV, executed in black ink with pen, wash, and watercolor techniques.

louvre_cl020113169.title: The Presentation of the Virgin at the Temple
louvre_cl020113169.description: Description of the album: Album bound in brown calfskin, decorated with bronze studs, probably an original Venetian binding from the end of the 15th century. The works are executed on parchment. Contains drawings RF 1475, 1 to RF 1556. H. 0.453; W. 0.305.

louvre_cl020111037.title: Shaft of a column from the Borghese Chapel, at Santa Maria Maggiore

louvre_cl020135982.title: Sketch of a Seated Figure
louvre_cl020135982.description: Description of the album: Notebook covered with pale blue paper, comprising 43 numbered sheets offering projects for sculptural groups and studies after the antique. Trace of missing folio between folios 27 and 28. 1 loose sheet cut from a newspaper, not foliated. H.15 L.9.7 cm.

louvre_cl020004029.title: Aeneas Carrying His Father Anchises
louvre_cl020004029.medium: Pen and brown ink. Fully mounted on English mounting.

louvre_cl020111803.title: Achilles stretched over the body of Patroclus

louvre_cl020106082.title: Virgin and Child
louvre_cl020106082.medium: Pen and black ink, brush and brown wash, gray wash, over black chalk underdrawing

louvre_cl020573157.title: Manuscript Annotations
louvre_cl020573157.medium: pencil on laid paper. Manuscript annotations: Cariani, Savoldo. Pencil pagination: 3
louvre_cl020573157.description: Description of the album: Album with brown cardboard cover, brown coated cloth spine, laid paper, detachable sheets (dotted perforations). Former numbering: 64 pages; folio numbering: in graphite pencil, on verso of folios, top left, crossed out: 32 folios. Missing folios between folios 8 and 9; 1 torn folio (tab) between folios 4 and 5; between folio 32 and cover plate, all folios torn out (tabs). On the cover plate: top right "F" in graphite traced over in black ink (François?); bottom right in ink: "1907?"; bottom left: Catalogue Raisonné label: "ct 20 (1907)" red felt-tip pen removed during restoration; red felt-tip cross. On the reverse of the upper plate, manuscript annotations in

louvre_cl020030970.title: The Dome of the Invalides and the Hill of Montmartre
louvre_cl020030970.medium: brown ink with pen; wash (brown); blue paper; brush; white highlights

louvre_cl020574576.title: Two Half-Length Figures: A Woman Wearing a White Hat and a Nun
louvre_cl020574576.medium: pencil and watercolor on paper. Pencil pagination: 63. On verso, pencil pagination: 64; former black ink pagination crossed out in pencil: 32
louvre_cl020574576.description: Description of the album: Notebook with canvas-covered cardboard cover with pencil slider. On the front cover: letter "N" in blue overwritten "F" in black. Black ballpoint pen annotation bottom right: 1928. Recent white label bearing the date "1928", removed during 2010 restoration. On the front endpaper: handwritten annotations including artist's addresses. Round label: 1928; Catalogue Raisonné label: Ct85 (1928) and annotations by the artist's descendants.

louvre_cl020120037.title: Landscape
louvre_cl020120037.description: Description of the album: Cardboard album covered with dark green leather, comprising 89 folios numbered from 1 to 89, of which folios 2 and 72 to 75 are blank. Two folios missing between folios 25 and 26, four between folios 55 and 56, seven between folios 60 and 61, two between folios 67 and 68 and between folios 71 and 72, three between folios 75 and 76. H. 0.143; L. 0.101.

louvre_cl020109882.title: Four Standing Men in Conversation
louvre_cl020109882.medium: Inscription by another hand, at bottom center: 'bruegel'

louvre_cl020214246.title: Bust of a woman, hands joined, head turned to the right
louvre_cl020214246.medium: Belonged to the 'Albums known as Maratta' from the Lambert Krahe collection

louvre_cl020119182.title: Landscape
louvre_cl020119182.medium: The drawing continues on the recto of folio 42
louvre_cl020119182.description: Description of the album: Notebook with blue marbled cardboard cover and green spine, with a label glued on the front cover indicating 'n° 14 / 1833', composed of 53 leaves, of which folios 28, 29 and 46 to 51 are blank, and an envelope containing drawing RF 8722. BIS. A label from 'Bourienne, Paper merchant, rue des Carmes, n° 77, in Rouen', is glued on the front endpaper.

louvre_cl020140816.title: A Love Seen from Behind
louvre_cl020140816.medium: red chalk wash; gray-blue paper; lead pencil
louvre_cl020140816.description: Description of the album: Notebook with coated canvas cover with black floral motifs, green canvas spine with 72 sheets. Blue and white inventory label on the front cover. H.12.3 W.10 D.1 cm.

louvre_cl020582150.title: The End of the World
louvre_cl020582150.description: Album description: H: 0.460 m, W: 0.319 m, D: 0.035 m, Open book: 0.882 m

louvre_cl020130683.title: Seashore
louvre_cl020130683.description: Description of the album: Composite album, with back and covers of dark green leather, with golden fillets, offering numerous views of the seashore. H.0.770; W. 0.600.

louvre_cl020210053.title: The Circumcision
louvre_cl020210053.medium: brown ink with pen; wash (brown); white highlights

louvre_cl020232562.title: A horseman, wearing a toque and fur coat, drawing a bow
louvre_cl020232562.description: Album description: Album bound in brown cloth, with pencil holder, containing 58 sheets. It bears a label glued on the back cover, with the inscription: '4026 / 1972 / M. Yvon Adolphe /1 lot of

louvre_cl020204422.title: A Young Woman Kneeling Holding an Object
louvre_cl020204422.medium: Belonged to the 'Albums known as Maratta' from the Lambert Krahe collection

louvre_cl020137357.title: Character Sketches
louvre_cl020137357.description: Description of the album: Sketch notebook with beige cloth cover, comprising 49 folios. On the lower cover is pasted an entrance ticket to the Universal Exhibition of 1889. There is a 2 cm gap along the entire height of folio 1. H.8.5 W.13.5 cm.

louvre_cl020125033.title: Study
louvre_cl020125033.medium: Previously reported on the recto of folio 33 of the album Boudin Eugène -2-

louvre_cl020204523.title: The Adoration of the Shepherds
louvre_cl020204523.medium: Belonged to the 'Albums known as Maratta' from the Lambert Krahe collection

louvre_cl020507151.title: Caricatures of a man from behind and a woman in profile
louvre_cl020507151.medium: Notebook taken in the other direction
louvre_cl020507151.description: Description of the album: Album with cardboard cover covered with black patterned paper. Cover: H: 0.105; W: 0.174. Sheet: H: 0.101; W: 0.170. Label glued at the top left on the front cover, inscription in ink: E. Isabey 10; in pencil: 7. Traces of missing folios between folios 6 and 7 (1), 9 and 10 (1), 10 and 11 (3), 11 and 12 (1), 15 and 16 (2), 16 and 17 (2), 20 and 21 (3), 24 and final endpaper (3). Folio 11 blank and torn.

louvre_cl020232498.title: Handwritten Notes
louvre_cl020232498.medium: colored ink with pen; graphite pencil
louvre_cl020232498.description: Description of the album: Notebook bound in brown leather, with metal clasp, gilt edges and bellows pocket on the back cover. On the spine, in gold letters: H. Penny's improved patent. 5.; on the clasp: H. Penny / patent. On the bellows of the back cover, label: H. Penny's / improved... Trace of cut folio between folios 1 and 2. Trace of missing sheet between folios 14 and 15, 29 and 30. H: 0.079; 0.125.

louvre_cl020233412.title: Landscape
louvre_cl020233412.medium: Notebook taken in the other direction
louvre_cl020233412.description: Description of the album: Album with beige cloth cover, comprising 31 sheets. A label from the stationer Guerrier-Chapron is glued on the front endpaper, at the top, on the left. Traces of 6 torn sheets between the front cover and folio 1, 2 sheets between folios 3 and 4. This notebook contains numerous handwritten notes and sketches of boat rigging. H: 0.163; W: 0.238.

louvre_cl020140454.title: A horseman accompanied by men on foot
louvre_cl020140454.description: Description of the album: Semi-factitious notebook covered with blue paper. Composed of 67 leaves, it brings together copies and studies executed in Italy. The cloth spine no longer exists, many folios are detaching (fol. 17, 21 and 66). Traces of offset. H. 0.130; W. 0.097.

louvre_cl010111721.title: Statuette: Écorché
louvre_cl010111721.description: It is represented standing, the body resting on the left leg; the right leg and left arm raised, the right arm lowered.

louvre_cl010113097.title: Statuette: Atalanta
louvre_cl010113097.medium: Material: bronze (gilded bronze), Material: ebony (base), Material: copper (copper inlay in base), Technique: metal techniques
louvre_cl010113097.description: Atalanta running dressed in a short tunic and sandals, right leg raised, holding a bow in her left hand. Base similar to that of Hippomenes.

louvre_cl010117440.title: Round Double-Case Watch
louvre_cl010117440.medium: Gold, gilded brass, diamond, metalwork techniques (goldsmithing), enameled
louvre_cl010117440.description: Outer case covered with translucent blue enamel decorated at the center with an eight-petaled flower in diamond roses surrounded by two garlands set with diamond roses. Bezel decorated with four floral garlands in diamond roses alternating with four openwork cartouches with scrollwork. The inner case is pierced with scrollwork and two engraved reserves, one with a mascaron, the other with a landscape. White enamel dial, two hands enriched with diamond roses.

louvre_cl010105374.title: Two-handled Vase: Allegory (?)
louvre_cl010105374.medium: Material: ceramic, Material: majolica=maiolica, Technique: cold decoration (ceramic techniques->coating and decoration)
louvre_cl010105374.description: Two-handled vase with ovoid body. Belongs to the group known as 'black ground'. A young woman, her gaze turned to the right, holding a sword. Decoration formed by paintings applied cold over a brown-black glaze.

louvre_cl010405127.title: Colony of Madagascar
louvre_cl010405127.description: Obverse: Louis XIV, with long curly hair, head turned to the right / legend: LUDOVICUS XIIII. REX CHRISTIANISSIMUS. Signed: J. MAUGER. F. Reverse: The colony is designated in the manner of ancient medals by an ox. This one has a hump on its back like those seen on this island (buffalo). It is near an ebony tree, a very common tree in this country / legend: COLONIA MADAGASCARICA. (Colony of Madagascar) In the exergue: M.DC.LXV. (1665) 2 identical examples (OAP 1322 and OAP 1323)

louvre_cl010213933.title: Razor strop (with ebony handle) and case (in morocco leather) from the toiletry set of Napoleon I then Tsar Alexander I
louvre_cl010213933.classification: goldsmithery, silverware; toiletry set; razor strop; case
louvre_cl010213933.description: Ebony handle and rectangular case of red morocco leather with gold vignette on the edges.

louvre_cl010113341.title: Plate with tenons
louvre_cl010113341.medium: Material: earthenware; Technique: glaze = coating = enamel = varnish (ceramic techniques->coating and decoration); Color: green
louvre_cl010113341.description: A Renaissance period ceramic plate dating from 1500-1600, made of earthenware with green glazing technique applied as surface coating and decoration.

louvre_cl010208853.title: Table spoon of Queen Hortense
louvre_cl010208853.description: On the top, the spoon's finial is decorated with a bucranium and a cascade of pendant ornaments; on the bowl, Queen Hortense's coat of arms, within an oval, is surmounted by a small head; below, a woman dressed in antique style holding a spear and dead game (Diana the huntress?). The reverse is decorated with palmettes and scrollwork.

louvre_cl010100556.title: Table cutlery
louvre_cl010100556.description: Plain and flat stems, spatulas engraved on the exterior with a cipher.

louvre_cl010116677.title: Plate
louvre_cl010116677.description: Decorated in the center with a crown of flowers framing a rose. The rim, formed of three red and white striped bands, is divided by three elongated medallions, bearing a vase on a white background. Contoured border in green and gold.

louvre_cl010117326.title: Pair of mounted vases with gray background
louvre_cl010117326.medium: Material/Technique: gilded bronze (mounting), Material: ceramic, Material: hard-paste porcelain, Material: bronze, Technique: gilded = gilding (metal techniques)
louvre_cl010117326.description: Pair of gray background vases, gilded figures, gilded bronze mounting. Gray and plain background. Reserved band painted in gold with a running frieze of antique-style figures representing, on the first vase, an indeterminate scene, on the second the union of a couple before an altar. Handles with scrollwork and square bases in gilded bronze.

louvre_cl010112001.title: Lotus leaf nelumbo nucifera
louvre_cl010112001.medium: Material: copper (mercury gilded), Technique: cloisonné enamel (metal techniques->enameled = enameling = enamel)
louvre_cl010112001.description: On the edges of the curved lotus leaf, two frogs and a snail.. This small object, rare in cloisonné and of remarkable aesthetic quality, is a decorative version of a type belonging to what is called wenfang si bao 文房四寶 (The scholar's treasures).

louvre_cl010099284.title: Armchair
louvre_cl010099284.medium: Material: wood, Material: skin = leather, Material: metal, Technique: bamboo/wood/horn/ivory/shell techniques (joinery)
louvre_cl010099284.description: Armchair with claw feet on balls, openwork backrest. Carved wood, leather upholstery.

louvre_cl010111280.title: Screen Model
louvre_cl010111280.description: Model for the face of a screen intended to be woven at the Gobelins Manufactory for the Salon de la Paix at the Tuileries. Four panels painted on canvas and mounted in wooden frames. Within a blue hexagon, bordered by a silver laurel frieze, stands a child between two flower baskets. The attributes allow identification, from left to right, of Architecture, Lyric Poetry, Astronomy and Sculpture. Around this hexagon develops a decoration of brackets, scrollwork and spirals, in greenish bronze tones on a violet-brown background. A torus of leaves, punctuated by rosettes at the corners, frames the whole.

louvre_cl010098326.title: Hourglass Frame
louvre_cl010098326.description: This cylindrical hourglass frame with two openings and sliding doors is decorated on its body with small flowers and arabesques on a striped background. Its circular bases, ornamented with a frieze of leaves, each have a dial with a sun-shaped index to count the elapsed hours (1-XII)

louvre_cl010101166.title: Breakfast Saucer of the Five Senses, from the cup 'Smell and Aromatics'
louvre_cl010101166.description: Saucer with chrome green ground. In the center, a musk deer among roses and lilies. Border with urns containing aromatic essences, and plants (vanilla...). Commissioned by Louis XVIII from the Sèvres

louvre_cl010097461.title: Book Binding Median Plaque: Crucifixion
louvre_cl010097461.medium: Champlevé copper, enameled, engraved, chased, gilded. Blue (medium, light), green, red, yellow, white, black enamels.
louvre_cl010097461.description: The green cross with pattée ends stands out against a blue enamel background decorated with a regular pattern of rosettes. Christ, slightly arched on the cross, wears a perizonium knotted in front. Under the suppedaneum bordered with dots, Adam emerges from his tomb. The cross, the Virgin and Saint John are placed on mounds of overlapping earth. Above the cross, two half-length angels with lowered wings emerge from semicircles decorated with polylobed clouds. Above the titulus, the hand of God appears in a cruciform nimbus, under a trilobed cloud.

louvre_cl010100965.title: Barometer-thermometer
louvre_cl010100965.medium: Material: horn (green); Technique: metal techniques (furniture bronze)
louvre_cl010100965.description: Barometer-thermometer, the first surmounted by the other and formed of a circular dial, the second veneered with green horn and framed with rocaille contours, flowers in chased and gilded bronze.

louvre_cl010103148.title: Statuette: Elephant
louvre_cl010103148.description: A bronze elephant statuette created around 1590 in Augsburg, Germany during the Renaissance period. This decorative sculpture combines artistic craftsmanship with functional design as a fountain element.

louvre_cl010107956.title: Dagger pommel: walking griffin / shield charged with a three-towered castle
louvre_cl010107956.medium: bronze, colored glass, champlevé enamel technique, metal techniques
louvre_cl010107956.description: Oval lobed dagger pommel. On the front face, a winged griffin walking to the left, with traces of blue enamel. On the reverse, a castle with three crenellated towers. The frame of both faces is decorated with four palmettes.

louvre_cl010393828.title: The Restoration of the Elector of Trier
louvre_cl010393828.description: Obverse: Young Louis XIV in bust, head turned to the right / legend: LUDOVICUS XIIII. REX CHRISTIANISSIMUS. Signed: J.MAVGER. F. Reverse: France presents to the elector a sword and a crosier, marks of his dignity, and a shield bearing his arms / legend: TUTELAE GALLICAE FIDELITAS. (France faithful to protecting its allies) In the exergue: ELECTOR TREVIRENSIS IN / INTEGRUM REST. / M.DC.XLV. (The Elector of Trier restored to full possession of his States, 1645)

louvre_cl010103565.title: Marble Mosaic Fireplace
louvre_cl010103565.medium: Material: marble, Technique: marble mosaic (stone/cement/plaster/wax techniques->mosaic)
louvre_cl010103565.description: A decorative fireplace created by Francesco Belloni between 1800-1820 in Paris, France, crafted using marble mosaic technique. This architectural element represents the refined domestic luxury of the French Empire period.

louvre_cl010448503.title: Marriage of the Count of Provence (Louis XVIII)
louvre_cl010448503.description: Obverse: Bust of the king, profile turned to the right / legend: LUD. XV. REX. CHRISTIANISS. Signed: B. DUVIVIER F. Reverse: The arms of France and Sardinia, separated by olive branches, hanging from a column / legend: FELIX SACRAE CONCORDIAE RENOVATIO (Happy renewal of a sacred alliance) Exergue: M. J. LUD. SARD. REGIS. FILIA / LUD. STA. XAV. COMITI PROV. / NUPTA / M DCC LXXI (Marie-Joséphine-Louise, daughter of the King of Sardinia, married to Louis-Stanislas-Xavier Count of Provence, 1771)

louvre_cl010102939.title: Decorative nail in the shape of flowers, from a pair (OA 912 A and B)
louvre_cl010102939.classification: architectural element, interior and exterior decoration, joinery
louvre_cl010102939.description: Anonymous decorative nail in the shape of flowers, part of a pair, made of wrought iron. This architectural element served both functional and decorative purposes in interior and exterior joinery.

louvre_cl010112644.title: Ice bucket with inner bowl and lid, from a pair (R 550), from a table service (R 550 to 675)
louvre_cl010112644.medium: Material: soft-paste porcelain, Technique: ceramic techniques
louvre_cl010112644.description: Made of soft-paste porcelain decorated with small flowers enhanced with gold fillets and a blue ribbon on the rim. Broken foot.

louvre_cl010450445.title: Napoleon in Toulouse
louvre_cl010450445.description: Obverse: Laureate head of Napoleon / legend: NAPOLEON EMP. ET ROI. Signed: ANDRIEU F. Reverse: The city of Toulouse standing before the emperor, who shows her the plan of projected embellishments for Toulouse / legend: PRAESENTIA DONIDQUE TOLOSA FELIX (Toulouse happy with his presence and his gifts) Exergue: XXV. JULII. / MDCCCVIII. Signed: ANDRIEU F. Single copy

louvre_cl010116258.title: Cup
louvre_cl010116258.description: Small hemispherical cup in banded and reddish agate.

louvre_cl010117779.title: Queen's armchair, from a set of four (with OA 10499 1, OA 10499 2 and OA 10499 3)
louvre_cl010117779.description: Backrest curved at the top and contoured. Armrests with arm pads, on console. Belt molded like the backrest, arms and feet, the latter cambered. Sculpted decoration of flower bouquets, isolated and on the feet, of a leaf. Frame upholstery. Modern green velvet upholstery.

louvre_cl010106930.title: Torch, from a pair
louvre_cl010106930.medium: Material: Saint Lucie Wood=Saint Lucie Cherry or False Cherry (Prunus mahaleb) (carved)
louvre_cl010106930.description: Torch with carved floral decoration, from a pair (with MRR 181), in Saint Lucie wood

louvre_cl010115527.title: Writing Table
louvre_cl010115527.medium: Material: wood, Material: skin = leather, Material: copper alloy, Technique: bamboo/wood/horn/ivory/shell techniques (joinery)
louvre_cl010115527.description: Writing table with leather top. 4 fluted legs with large ring at the base. Front and straight sides with plain belt in slight diamond point - 2 keyed drawers underlined by a molding above with projecting molded edge sheathed in black leather.

louvre_cl010104434.title: Painted engraving of the Duke of Reichstadt
louvre_cl010104434.description: "Charles Joseph François Duke of Reichstadt", bust portrait, three-quarter view to the left, in civilian dress (1827) with Joseph Steinmüller (1795-1841) as draftsman of the model.

louvre_cl010104504.title: Shallow Bowl on Annular Foot
louvre_cl010104504.description: Shallow bowl on annular foot with smooth central boss and fluted basin. At the center, the slightly raised boss is decorated with a golden rosette on a blue background. Beyond a blue band, the basin is ornamented with 17 curved concave white flutes, with red highlights on a blue background. The reverse, dark blue/black under the boss and blue under the basin, is decorated with golden motifs.

louvre_cl010116464.title: Plate Fragment
louvre_cl010116464.description: Concentric zones with alternating colored backgrounds, borders, festoons, wide course of white sinuous leaves modeled in blue, dotted chevrons

louvre_cl010097759.title: Tray: figures in a landscape
louvre_cl010097759.description: Four-lobed tray in black porcelain with two figures in a landscape.

louvre_cl010104892.title: Boat-shaped seal matrix with its red wax impression: Chapter of Presles
louvre_cl010104892.description: The field is occupied by six hooded heads arranged in pairs and surmounted by a seventh larger one.

louvre_cl010117230.title: Handscroll: On the River During the Qingming Festival (Qingming shanghe tu 清明上河圖)
louvre_cl010117230.medium: Material: paper, Technique: ink (drawing techniques), Technique: watercolor painting (painting techniques)
louvre_cl010117230.classification: drawing, pastel; book art: miniature, illumination
louvre_cl010117230.description: This 18th-century scroll is a miniature reinterpreted version of the Qingming shanghe tu (On the River During the Qingming Festival) by Zhang Zeduan 張擇端 (1085-1145), one of the most famous works in the history of Chinese art. Commissioned by Emperor Qianlong himself from Yao Wenhan, a court painter, it was completed in 1768, then remounted to be reduced the following year so it could be stored in a small casket, and finally, two years later, calligraphy of a poem composed by Qianlong was added.

louvre_cl010467894.title: Homage to Adriano Balbi
louvre_cl010467894.description: Obverse: Portrait facing right / legend: ADRIANUS BALBI / NAT. VENETIAE D. XXI APRIL. MDCCLXXXII (Adrian Balbi, born in Venice on April 21, 1782) Signed: BOUVET F. Reverse: circular legend: DECUS COSMOGRAPHIAE / PARISIIS MDCCCXLII (1842); a laurel crown; inscription in the field: ATLAS / ETHNOGRAPHIQUE / DU GLOBE / MDCCCXXVI (1826) / - / ABREGE / DE / GEOGRAPHIE / MDCCCXXXII (1832) Inscription on the edge: Ship's prow / copper Single copy Italian geographer and statistician, born in Venice in 1782, died in Vienna in 1848.

louvre_cl010116376.title: Medal: Pope Leo XII / pontifical emblems
louvre_cl010116376.medium: Material: bronze (gilded bronze), Technique: metalwork techniques
louvre_cl010116376.description: A commemorative medal featuring Pope Leo XII and pontifical emblems, created during the contemporary period (1800-1900) in the Western world. This gilded bronze medallion represents papal authority through traditional Catholic iconography and serves as both religious and commemorative art.

louvre_cl010114814.title: Three-light candelabra, from a pair (OA 6900 C)
louvre_cl010114814.medium:
Material: bronze (patinated and gilded)
Technique: metal techniques (furniture bronze)
louvre_cl010114814.description: Candelabra with triangular base with notched faces, formed by three crouching griffons in black bronze, supporting a gilded bronze base surmounted by a winged and draped woman in black bronze, whose raised arms carry an antique-form cup with three bobéchons.

louvre_cl010096637.title: White Eagle of Poland mounted as a clasp
louvre_cl010096637.medium: Enameled gold (white, black, dark translucent blue enamel), hessonite garnet (formerly called hyacinth), ruby (one hundred forty-nine rubies), emerald, shell pearl
louvre_cl010096637.description: Clasp in enameled gold enriched with garnets. It is formed by the white eagle of Poland with black feathers, wings spread, head topped with the imperial crown. Its talons hold the regalia of the kingdom of Poland, those on the right the golden scepter, those on the left, the orb with cross, enameled in translucent dark blue and surrounded by garnets.

louvre_cl010105805.title: Pair of vases (Etruscan-style scroll vases)
louvre_cl010105805.description: Pair of vases (Etruscan-style scroll vases). Porcelain. Celestial blue ground, with garlands of flowers burnished for effect. At the neck, reserved zone painted with a frieze of vine leaves in natural colors, with two purple ribbons.

louvre_cl010105329.title: Spouted jar with coat of arms, from a pair with OA 1632
louvre_cl010105329.description: Ovoid body; serpentine handles. Decoration formed of scrollwork within a crown of foliage. At the base the coat of arms "divided with green field showing a running hare, and blue field with three golden mountains, separated by a silver band". An inscription: "AQa .D. ENDIVIA" [endive water (Cichorium intybus)].

louvre_cl010110034.title: Large round plate from the service of the King of England and Hanover George III, from a set of six (OA 10613 to OA 10618)
louvre_cl010110034.description: Contoured plate bordered with ribboned reeds

louvre_cl010099091.title: Hexagonal tile: trefoil foliage
louvre_cl010099091.medium: Material: ceramic, Material: majolica=maiolica, Material: faience
louvre_cl010099091.classification: architectural element, interior and exterior decoration, framework; floor tile
louvre_cl010099091.description: Blue and manganese decoration on white ground.

louvre_cl010462363.title: The King's Journey to the Northern Departments
louvre_cl010462363.description: Obverse: Laureate head of Charles X facing right / legend: CHARLES X KING OF FRANCE AND NAVARRE. Signed: DEPAULIS. F. / DE PUYMAURIN D. Reverse: Charles X mounted on a chariot drawn by four horses, receives the keys that a city presents to him. Another city, holding an olive branch, follows the chariot.

louvre_cl010100695.title: Agate Box
louvre_cl010100695.medium: Primary material: gold; Secondary material: agate; Technique: chased (metal chasing techniques); Material: onyx
louvre_cl010100695.description: Round box with regular contours, made of agate and onyx mounted in gold. The lid and bottom of the box are each formed by a convex agate plate, shell-carved, engraved with leaves and circled with gold with plain moldings. Vertical body, cage-mounted in gold and decorated with eight rectangular panels (four large alternating with four small) in agate and onyx. Spout with contours on the front of the lid.

louvre_cl010436044.title: Woerden Rescued
louvre_cl010436044.description: Obverse: Louis XIV, with long curly hair, head turned to the right / legend: LUDOVICUS XIIII. REX CHRISTIANISSIMUS * Reverse: In the middle of a marsh, a column to which a shield is attached. Victory places on top of this shield an obsidional crown of green and flowering herbs / legend: CASTRIS BATAVORUM CAPTIS ET DIREPTIS (The Dutch camp taken and pillaged) In the exergue: WURDA OBSIDIONE / LIBERATA * / M.DC.LXXII * (Woerden rescued, 1672)

louvre_cl010104523.title: Casket with the monogram IHS
louvre_cl010104523.description: Rectangular casket with pyramidal lid. The casket, blue on three sides with a white border, black on the reverse, is fitted with four gilded oval feet decorated with white half-pearls. The front bears the christogram 'IHS' surmounting a vase, in gold. The lid, blue on the edge, white with blue and green highlights on the pyramidal part, has a gilded oval grip button decorated with white half-pearls. The interior of the casket is white; the interior of the lid is blue and bears the letters 'IHS'. Grip button and feet probably added in the 19th century.

louvre_cl010113976.title: Bas-relief in a gilded frame: The Annunciation
louvre_cl010113976.description: Mary, on the right, is kneeling before a prie-dieu on which a book is open, her head turned toward the angel and her right hand on her heart; her left hand is placed on the book. The angel Gabriel, on the left, arrives walking, holding a scepter in his left hand (his right hand is broken). The Holy Spirit, in the form of a dove, is in a cloud. The decor on the right is that of an interior with a canopied bed whose curtains are half-open; between the Virgin and Gabriel, a large vase of flowers

louvre_cl010112446.title: Ring with IHS monogram
louvre_cl010112446.medium:
Technique: metal techniques (goldsmithing)
Material: vermeil = gilded silver
louvre_cl010112446.description: Ring with flat band, terminated at each end by a term adjusting by means of volutes with an oval bezel bearing the IHS monogram in relief.

louvre_cl010117520.title: Powder Flask: a chimera facing the sun

louvre_cl010101127.title: Belt Buckle
louvre_cl010101127.description: Formed by a winged angel head on which is attached, soldered, a hook, the belt buckle articulates, by means of a hinge, with a rectangular piece. This piece is decorated with openwork Gothic decoration standing out against a red velvet background. At the center of the openwork part, a nielloed silver plate represents a bust of a young man in profile to the left, surmounted by the motto AMO. The association of this profile with this motto suggests that the buckle was originally part of a "betrothal belt" that a young man traditionally offered to a young girl as a sign of union

louvre_cl010109466.title: Oval Astronomical Watch
louvre_cl010109466.description: Oval astronomical watch with polished case; enamel dial decorated with polychrome translucent enamel leaves. Disc bearing the names of months and dates. Under a ring are indicated the hours in Roman numerals. Three openings reveal the name of the day, the age and figure of the moon. Two hands. Movement modified in the 18th century: equipped with a spring balance-spiral regulator protected by a single-footed cock, pierced and engraved with scrollwork. Fusee fitted with a chain; worm screw.

louvre_cl010247705.title: German translations of Latin authors that belonged to the Duke of Reichstadt: Aulus Persius Flaccus Satires. Vienna, 1794.
louvre_cl010247705.description: German translations of Latin authors that belonged to the Duke of Reichstadt: Aulus Persius Flaccus Satires. Vienna, 1794. 1 volume in-12, half binding.

louvre_cl010418534.title: Campaign of 1696
louvre_cl010418534.description: Obverse: Louis XIV, with long curly hair, head turned to the right / legend: LUDOVICUS MAGNUS REX CHRISTIANISSIMUS. Signed: J. MAUGER. F. Reverse: Mars, seated before a camp, leans on the shield of France; near him, his horse / legend: MARS IN HOSTILI SEDENS. (Mars peacefully encamped in enemy territory) Exergue: M.DC.XCVI (1696) 2 identical examples (OAP 1455 and OAP 1456)

louvre_cl010111774.title: Plate: The Month of June
louvre_cl010111774.description: Grisaille plate on black background with gold and red highlights, depicting the month of June with sheep shearing in front of a farm building, with the sign of Cancer in a cloud on black background; on the rim, four vases, four masks, eight dragons; on the reverse, a central rosette with four divergent herms holding straps framing two satyr heads and two masks with round headdresses. Inscription: "IUNG" in gold on the building.

louvre_cl010482805.title: Chandelier
louvre_cl010482805.medium: Material/Technique: Electrical wire, Material: ferrous alloy, Technique: gilded (metal techniques), Material: glass, Technique: painted (metal techniques)
louvre_cl010482805.description: Chandelier in fragmentary state accompanied by 4 small plastic bags containing fragments and pendant drops.

louvre_cl010114806.title: Small two-light candelabra from a pair
louvre_cl010114806.medium: Material: bronze (gilded), Material: marble, Technique: metal techniques (furniture bronze)
louvre_cl010114806.description: Small two-light candelabra in the form of flowering branches. The shaft is a young boy holding flowers

louvre_cl010115048.title: Snuffbox
louvre_cl010115048.medium: Material: wood (painted with Martin varnish), Material: gold, Technique: metalworking techniques (goldsmithing)
louvre_cl010115048.description: Small snuffbox in painted wood (Martin varnish) decorated on all its faces with small cupids against landscape backgrounds. Edge of the lid and throat in gold.

louvre_cl010465961.title: Commemoration of the Battle of Cowpens, January 17, 1781
louvre_cl010465961.description: Obverse: Colonel John Edgar Howard, on horseback, pursues a standard-bearer who has lost his sword and hat; above the scene flies a Victory / legend: JOH. EGAR. HOWARD LEGIONIS PETITIUM PRAEFECTO (To John Edgar Howard, commander of the infantry) In the exergue: COMITIA AMERICANA (American Congress) Signed: DU.VIV. Reverse: A laurel crown; inscription in the field: QUOD / IN NUTANTEM HOSTIUM ACIEM / SUBITO IRRUENS / PRAECLARUM BELLICAE VIRTUTIS / SPECIMEN DEDIT / IN PUGNA AD COWPENS / XVII. JAN MDCCLXXXI (Making a sudden charge into the wavering enemy line, he provided a brilliant example of military virtue at the battle of Cowpens on January 17, 1781)

louvre_cl010101138.title: Plaque: The Crowning with Thorns, from a series of ten 'The Passion of Christ'
louvre_cl010101138.description: Rectangular plaque in grisaille on black background with gold and red highlights, depicting the crowning with thorns. Christ, seated in profile, appears to speak to a kneeling man who sticks out his tongue at him while another pushes a crown of thorns onto his head using a small fork and a third raises a club (?); a group comments on the scene on the left; the counter-enamel is in fondant.

louvre_cl010113323.title: Round Plate: Two Nude Hunters
louvre_cl010113323.description: Two nude hunters, after an engraving by Master IB with the Bird, The Abduction of Ganymede. On the reverse: lustered rinceaux; the inscription 'Giacomo' has been read by specialists as the painter's signature.

louvre_cl010113484.title: Sheath, from a pair (with OA 10490)
louvre_cl010113484.medium: Wood, gilt bronze, copper, ébénisterie (fine woodworking) techniques
louvre_cl010113484.description: Scabellon identical to OA 10490, with medallion showing a woman's profile turned to the right, on the shaft.

louvre_cl010117532.title: Sword of Justice
louvre_cl010117532.medium: Steel (engraved and gilded), horn, leather, velvet
louvre_cl010117532.description: Scabbard, black leather, gilded metal. Sword, gilded and tapered metal guard with fringe. Flintlock, horn handle. Knives, horn handle.

louvre_cl010103484.title: Pyriform Vase
louvre_cl010103484.description: Small vase with pyriform body and fluted cap in green jasper speckled with light red; base set with gilt copper.

louvre_cl010111167.title: Portable alms box in the shape of a house or holy oils container
louvre_cl010111167.description: Of rectangular form, it is equipped with a high lid with four slopes, topped with a handle. It is decorated with light engraved scrollwork, and closed on one of the short sides by a hasp lock; a crenellated band conceals the joint of the lid. On one of the long sides is attached a silver disc, decorated with a cross. In the corresponding face of the lid, a horizontal slot has been made.

louvre_cl010467588.title: Institution of the Prize of the Academy of Fine Arts of Lucca for the Most Beautiful Work, 1805
louvre_cl010467588.description: Obverse: Facing heads of the prince and princess of Lucca and Piombino; circular legend: ELISA. NAPOL. AUG. SOROR. ET FELIX. I. PRINCC. LUCAE. ET. PLUMBINI. (Elisa, sister of Napoleon, and Felix I, prince of Lucca and Piombino) Signed: SANTARELLI. F. Reverse: Circular legend: ACAD. LUCENSIUM/ NAPOLEONEA. INSTITUTA A. M.DCCC.V. FELICITER. (The Napoleon Academy of Lucca happily instituted, year 1805); a laurel crown; inscription in the field: DIGNIORIBUS / MUNERANDIS (Most beautiful work)

louvre_cl010099942.title: Round Watch
louvre_cl010099942.medium: Material: gold, shell pearl, brass (gilded). Technique: metalwork (goldsmithing), enameled
louvre_cl010099942.description: Oval enamel medallion depicting a bucolic scene surrounded by a frieze of white crescents, a frieze of arches alternating with fleurons, and a border of leaves. Bezels decorated with half-pearls, bezel opening by a pusher set into the case at 3 o'clock. Dial with Arabic numerals, pierced hands decorated with pearls. Movement equipped with a verge escapement and a repeating strike on gong. Pierced cock with radiating decoration. Regulation rosette marked 'Fast-Slow'. Glass case.

louvre_cl010101829.title: Prayer Rug with Niche Decoration
louvre_cl010101829.medium: Material: wool, Material: silk, Technique: carpet (textile techniques)
louvre_cl010101829.description: carpet with cream background, blue, green, red motifs, etc...

louvre_cl010111767.title: Salt Cellar: Neptune and Amphitrite
louvre_cl010111767.medium: Painted enamel on copper, wooden base (restored)
louvre_cl010111767.description: Oval salt cellar in baluster form in grisaille on black ground, depicting the triumph of Neptune and Amphitrite. The upper rim is decorated with cut leather work and fruits with two lion heads; the interior is in fondant.

louvre_cl010101261.title: Plaque: Nativity; Adoration of the Shepherds; Bathing of the Child
louvre_cl010101261.medium: Elephant ivory, bone (frame). Gilded silver, wood.
louvre_cl010101261.description: The icon consists of a relatively thick solid wood panel, hollowed out in its central part, where an ivory plaque is fixed, representing the Nativity, fitted with a first frame composed of three thin ivory rods carved with stylized palmettes, the fourth, on the left, having been restored in plaster.

louvre_cl010107330.title: Medal. The Opera
louvre_cl010107330.description: 2 bi-facial bronze medals. For the first: Obverse: facade of the Paris Opera. Reverse: first floor plan. Jean Lagrange identified. The second is in the gallery.

louvre_cl010111477.title: Small Shallow Cup
louvre_cl010111477.medium: Material: glass, Technique: colored (glass/stained glass/plastic techniques), Technique: mold-blown = mold blowing (glass/stained glass/plastic techniques->blown)
louvre_cl010111477.description: Small shallow cup with wide rim. Colorless glass with small handles terminated by blue cabochons. Molded decoration.

louvre_cl010101227.title: Clock 'The Archangel Saint Michael, victor over Satan, announces the reign of God'
louvre_cl010101227.medium: Material: bronze, Technique: patinated (metal techniques), Technique: gilded (metal techniques)
louvre_cl010101227.description: Representing 'The Archangel Saint Michael, victor over Satan, announces the reign of God' (Model of the group exhibited by Jehan Duseigneur at the Salon of 1834)

louvre_cl010104371.title: Shell-shaped Watch
louvre_cl010104371.description: Shell-shaped watch; pear-shaped case with two ribbed covers mounted on hinges. Frame of the dial cover engraved with foliage and striated; frame of the opposite cover engraved in similar manner. Case middle decorated with a frieze of vine leaves and grape clusters. Molded button; pendant missing. Dial plate engraved with a village view and scrollwork featuring a cherub head and two rabbits; it supports a ring with Roman numerals. Movement equipped with openwork cock and ratchet engraved with flowering scrollwork. Verge escapement, fusee with gut cord. Baluster-shaped pillars.

louvre_cl010114240.title: Copper Tray
louvre_cl010114240.medium: Material: copper, Material: silver (traces of silvering?), Technique: repoussé (metal techniques), Technique: hammered (metal techniques)
louvre_cl010114240.description: copper tray with concentric radiating motifs, in the center a rosette and around it two circles of copper palmettes.

louvre_cl010102107.title: Round Bowl: Three-Quarter Bust of a Woman
louvre_cl010102107.medium: Technique: ceramic techniques; Material: majolica; Technique: grand feu decoration (ceramic techniques - coating and decoration); Technique: metallic luster (ceramic techniques - coating and decoration)
louvre_cl010102107.description: Polychrome decoration. Blue background with lustred scrollwork. Inscription: 'Hipolita.Bell'. Reverse decorated and lustred

louvre_cl010381304.title: Cylindrical Goblet
louvre_cl010381304.medium: Material: crystal, Technique: cut (glass/stained glass/plastic techniques)
louvre_cl010381304.description: Reverse cut with a 16-pointed star. Base with flat ribs. Cut decoration of four-petaled flowers with micro-diamonds or striations separated by rhombuses.

louvre_cl010114801.title: Three-light wall sconce from a pair
louvre_cl010114801.medium: Material: bronze (gilded). Technique: metal techniques (furniture bronze)
louvre_cl010114801.description: Wall sconce decorated with quiver surmounted by turtle doves. After the model by Feuchère for Marie-Antoinette's cabinet de toilette at Saint-Cloud.

louvre_cl010115975.title: Paneling Elements (Hôtel Guimard)
louvre_cl010115975.medium: Material: wood (gilded and painted). Technique: carved (bamboo/wood/horn/ivory/shell techniques). Technique: bamboo/wood/horn/ivory/shell techniques (joinery)
louvre_cl010115975.description: Double door and overdoor panel, a mirror trumeau and two parecloses and a window fitted with mirror shutters.

louvre_cl010476041.title: Plate: Parable of the Prodigal Son
louvre_cl010476041.medium: Ceramic, maiolica, grand feu decoration technique
louvre_cl010476041.description: Compendiario decoration in blue, yellow, green and manganese on white ground. Scene in the center of the plate after an etching by Flemish painter-engraver Hans Bol published by Bartholomeus de Momper. On the rim, grotesque decoration: at the center of each side, under a canopy, a putto seated on an armillary sphere dominates two nude figures back to back; in the lower part, candelabra motifs terminate with two dolphins facing each other around a mascaron; in the upper part, three putti, successively standing, seated and flying, alternate with a vase and a torch, the last two framing the date "1567" inscribed in a central cartouche.

louvre_cl010101968.title: Covered Cup: Adam and Eve
louvre_cl010101968.description: Covered cup in grisaille with red and gold highlights. Inside the cup, the temptation of Adam and Eve; on the exterior of the lid, God reproaching Adam and Eve after sin, Adam and Eve expelled from Paradise by an angel, Adam working the earth before Eve; around the knob, leather work; inside the lid, two sphinxes, two twisted children terms and four monsters around a fleuron; the baluster foot is decorated with leather work, foliage garlands and terms; the reverse of the foot is black with golden fleur-de-lis.

louvre_cl010067046.title: Sibyl Unrolling a Paper
louvre_cl010067046.medium: fresco transferred to canvas on canvas

louvre_cl010061789.title: The Beggars or The Cripples
louvre_cl010061789.description: The real meaning of the scene, particularly the significance of the fox tail motif (a motif dear to the Gueux rebels manifesting in Brussels in 1567), remains obscure. The evocation of beggars wearing fox tails may testify to a popular tradition linked to Carnival and notably to Mardi Gras, as emphasized by the paper mitre of a mocking bishop (cf. Sellink).

louvre_cl010066299.title: Eberhard, Count of Württemberg, called The Weeper

louvre_cl010060456.title: Landscape at Sunrise
louvre_cl010060456.description: Note the curious detail of a Holy Family painted on the wall of a sort of ruined oratory, located on the wooded slope in the background, on the left. – From the most beautiful and virtuosic period – late – of the artist, around 1665. From before 1810 and until at least 1845, associated as a pendant (possible, says the 1810 catalog) to another Pynacker, the Landscape with White Goat (Wallace Collection, London, cf. Hofstede de Groot, no. 155; Harwood, no. 71), which appears alone at an earlier sale (Leiden, 10 V 1774, no. 4).

louvre_cl010067536.title: Woodwork
louvre_cl010067536.description: Decorative fragments from the Hôtel Lambert in Paris

louvre_cl010057148.title: Hercules
louvre_cl010057148.description: Study and restoration proposal for the fresco decoration painted by Primatice in a window embrasure of the ballroom (also called 'Henri II room') of the Château de Fontainebleau.

louvre_cl010061286.title: Portrait of a Woman in Riding Attire
louvre_cl010061286.description: In the background of the landscape, to the right, the silhouette of a castle with crenellated towers, probable allusion to the family estate of the model who remains unknown to this day.

louvre_cl010059896.title: The Guitar Player, Spied from Above
louvre_cl010059896.description: To be dated around 1640, according to Margret Klinge (written communication, May 2006).

louvre_cl010065870.title: Scenes from the Massacres at Chios. Greek families awaiting death or slavery
louvre_cl010065870.description: Greek families awaiting death or slavery, etc. (see various accounts and newspapers of the time) [excerpt from the 1824 Salon catalog]

louvre_cl010054831.title: Shepherds in a landscape, also called Shepherd playing the flute, shepherdess listening to him

louvre_cl010056145.title: Portrait of an Italian Woman (Vittoria Caldoni?)

louvre_cl010063051.title: The Foundation of the Trinitarian Order
louvre_cl010063051.description: The scene represents the first mass of Saint John of Matha (1160-1213) celebrated in the chapel of the Archbishop of Paris, Maurice de Sully: at the moment of the elevation of the host, an angel appears to him, arms crossed and hands placed on the heads of two kneeling slaves, revealing his mission to found an order for the ransom of Christian captives taken by the Barbary corsairs (the Trinitarian order); in the upper part, the Trinity. Format: arched

louvre_cl010054015.title: Portrait of a Man, called Ferdinand François d'Avalos

louvre_cl010060275.title: Portrait of a Lady of Quality in a Park Setting, with a Toy Spaniel at Her Side
louvre_cl010060275.description: The name of Jan de Baen, a portraitist highly appreciated in the aristocratic circles of The Hague, seems more appropriate here than that of Andries Hanneman, mentioned in the previous catalogue. – To be dated around 1660-1680.

louvre_cl010055832.title: Portrait of Mademoiselle Roland, daughter of the sculptor

louvre_cl010056021.title: Calm Sea with Tower on Promontory

louvre_cl010059449.title: Venus and Cupid Spied Upon by a Satyr, formerly called Jupiter and Antiope
louvre_cl010059449.description: The later title 'Jupiter and Antiope' results from a confusion often made with the representation of 'Venus and Cupid' (see Correggio at the Louvre, INV. 42, error corrected regarding this latter painting by Mirimonde [1984], p. 175-179). Here we clearly recognize winged Cupid and Venus (with milk spurting from her breast), goddess of generation. A beautiful example of Goltzius's peaceful Mannerism in his final phase, influenced by Rubens and his broad, living and colorful plasticity, while the Venus refers back to Michelangelo and Primaticcio.

louvre_cl010056490.title: Trophy of Arms
louvre_cl010056490.description: Study and restoration proposal for the fresco decoration painted by Primatice on a window pier in the ballroom (also called 'Henri II room') of the Château de Fontainebleau.

louvre_cl010061258.title: The Triumph of Mordecai
louvre_cl010061258.description: 18th century (1738-1739) French painting depicting the biblical triumph of Mordecai from the Book of Esther, housed in the Paintings Department of the Louvre Museum.

louvre_cl010062576.title: Sea Wolf
louvre_cl010062576.description: Painting in very poor condition. Difficult, under these conditions, to know whether it is a seal, a walrus or a sea lion.

louvre_cl010065045.title: Portrait of Adolphine Købke (1820-1880), sister of the artist
louvre_cl010065045.description: Købke, who often used family members as models, depicted his young sister Adolphine Augusta several times (two painted portraits, private collection, showing her younger, cf. Krohn, no 2, and older, cf. Krohn, no 184), and a drawn sketch, D. 1835, showing her seated, Statens Museum for Kunst in Copenhagen). The girl, looking somewhat sulky, dressed in a pretty checkered dress, was then eleven years old (she was born on December 13, 1820); she would marry Wilhelm Nicolai Petersen, a cousin of the Købke family, on August 29, 1845.

louvre_cl010056365.title: View of part of the Port and city of Bordeaux taken from the Salinières side

louvre_cl010067173.title: The Seine before the Pont Neuf, also called The Seine downstream from the Pont-Neuf, in Paris, with the Louvre on the left and the Collège des Quatre Nations on the right
louvre_cl010067173.description: A topographical painting depicting the Seine River in Paris before the Pont Neuf bridge, showing the Louvre Palace on the left bank and the Collège des Quatre Nations on the right bank, created in 1754 during the mid-18th century.

louvre_cl010059930.title: Monsieur Arnault de Gorse, brother of the writer Antoine Vincent Arnault

louvre_cl010239510.title: Louis-Philippe, King of the French (1773-1850)
louvre_cl010239510.description: In the background, the fortifications of Paris in 1848.

louvre_cl010063147.title: Berthe Juliette Dubois, child (1797-1849), daughter of Antoine Dubois, Napoleon's physician

louvre_cl010064290.title: Peaches, Grapes and Pineapple on a Stone Table

louvre_cl010242125.title: Ceiling: Hall of Septimius Severus - The Rape of the Sabines, above the cornice, west side

louvre_cl010054795.title: Adoration of the Magi (fragment of a painting)

louvre_cl010062788.title: Angelica and Medoro
louvre_cl010062788.description: Angelica, Queen of Carthage, watches her lover carve their names on a tree (Ariosto "Orlando Furioso", chapter XIX)

louvre_cl010065917.title: Emperor Theodosius Receives His Pardon from Saint Ambrose, Archbishop of Milan

louvre_cl010062141.title: Gallant Conversation at the Entrance of an Inn
louvre_cl010062141.description: Attributed to Jan Miel until Villot. The signature was long misunderstood, due to the confusion existing between the different (Van der) Meer. Although of Italianate taste, the painting – one of the rare signed works by the artist – seems, by its date, to predate the painter's probable stay in Rome (present in that city from at least 1655 until 1657). – A fairly similar version, signed, in Hamburg (museum catalogue, 1966, no. 291, with the correct attribution).

louvre_cl010064358.title: Still Life with Carp
louvre_cl010064358.description: Freshwater fish (carp with moon, bream, pike, perch, roach, chub). Very rare in Van Beyeren's work, the motif of the suspended carp – with symbolic (?) or merely decorative value – is found among many of his contemporaries (Putter – painter whose niece Beyeren married in his second marriage – Verbeeck, Dirven, Kuvenis, Duynen, Dirk Govertsz., Susenier, etc., cf. Foucart, Meijer). – Work from the artist's early years of production, around 1645-1650.

louvre_cl010065019.title: Saint Anthony and the Miracle of the Mule

louvre_cl010093236.title: Woman's Mask
louvre_cl010093236.description: A marble sculptural fragment from a tomb, created in France between 1585-1600 during the Renaissance period. This funerary mask represents a woman and would have been part of a larger tomb monument, serving as a commemorative portrait of the deceased.

louvre_cl010092075.title: Louis XVI (1754-1793) King of France
louvre_cl010092075.description: A marble bust depicting Louis XVI, King of France from 1774 to 1792, who was executed during the French Revolution in 1793. This sculptural portrait represents the last king of France before the fall of the monarchy.

louvre_cl010093365.title: Escutcheon with the Arms of Lorraine
louvre_cl010093365.description: A heraldic escutcheon carved in alabaster displaying the coat of arms of Lorraine, created in 1551 by Dominique Florentin. This high relief sculpture represents the heraldic symbols associated with the Duchy of Lorraine, carved with the refined technique characteristic of Renaissance stone carving. Originally associated with the collegiate church of Saint-Laurent, this work exemplifies the intersection of religious, political, and artistic patronage in 16th century France.

louvre_cl010090892.title: Head of a Satyr
louvre_cl010090892.description: A carved stone fragment depicting the head of a satyr, created around 1855-1856 in France. This architectural element served as a modillion, a decorative bracket used to support a cornice in classical architecture.

louvre_cl010089802.title: Two Children Fighting Over a Nest
louvre_cl010089802.description: A marble sculptural group depicting two children engaged in a dispute over a bird's nest, created in France between 1885-1900.

louvre_cl010093624.title: Music
louvre_cl010093624.description: A marble bas-relief sculpture representing Music, created in France between 1685-1700 during the Baroque period. This allegorical work personifies the concept of music through sculptural form, likely featuring musical instruments and symbolic elements that convey the harmony and divine nature of musical art. The piece exemplifies the refined artistic traditions of late 17th-century French sculpture, created during the reign of Louis XIV when the arts flourished under royal patronage.

louvre_cl010092689.title: Panther of Tunis
louvre_cl010092689.description: A bronze statuette depicting a panther from Tunis, created by French sculptor Antoine Louis Barye before 1902. The work features a distinctive green-orange patina finish and represents the artist's expertise in animalier sculpture, capturing the essence of this North African feline predator.

louvre_cl010093926.title: First model of the mausoleum of Cardinal de Fleury (1653-1743)
louvre_cl010093926.description: First model of the mausoleum of Cardinal de Fleury (1653-1743)

louvre_cl010090180.title: Hippolyte Fortoul
louvre_cl010090180.description: Fortoul, Hippolyte (1811-1856) politician, man of letters and historian; Minister of Public Instruction and Religious Affairs (1851-1856). Profile facing left

louvre_cl010090212.title: Ney
louvre_cl010090212.description: Ney, Michel (1769-1815) Duke of Elchingen; Prince of Moskova; Marshal of the Empire (1804). Profile to the left, on the obverse; on the reverse, full figure (his execution).

louvre_cl010087086.title: Walking Lion
louvre_cl010087086.description: S on the terrace in front: BARYE

louvre_cl010091635.title: Funeral mask of Henri II
louvre_cl010091635.description: A funeral mask of Henri II made in plaster, representing the posthumous likeness of the French king who ruled from 1547 to 1559.

louvre_cl010094363.title: Capital decorated with birds fighting monstrous quadrupeds

louvre_cl010090071.title: Julien the Sculptor
louvre_cl010090071.description: Julien, Pierre (1731-1804) sculptor. Profile facing left.

louvre_cl010090533.title: Roederer
louvre_cl010090533.description: Roederer, Pierre Louis (1754-1835), Count of the Empire, senator, state counselor. Profile facing right.

louvre_cl010095478.title: The Toilette of Venus
louvre_cl010095478.description: A terracotta bas-relief sketch depicting the toilette of Venus, the Roman goddess of love and beauty, created around 1693 by French sculptor Pierre-Etienne Monnot. This preparatory work captures an intimate moment of the goddess during her beauty ritual.

louvre_cl010091837.title: Vase decorated with godrons and serpent-shaped handles
louvre_cl010091837.description: A marble vase featuring decorative godrons (fluted ornamental elements) and handles crafted in the form of serpents. This restored antique piece dates from 1500-1800 and represents the sophisticated decorative arts tradition of the Renaissance and early modern periods.

louvre_cl010094785.title: Diana
louvre_cl010094785.description: A marble sculpture depicting Diana, the Roman goddess of the hunt and the moon, created by French sculptor Anselme Flamen (known as Flamen Père) in 1694 during the reign of Louis XIV.
